### Glamazon — 2012-04-11T14:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13695>

So I did not sleep well at all last night. I have a problem sleeping well at night before I started the reset. It's been a life long problem since I became a night shift worker in my 20s as a nurse for about 20 years. I work a regular day job for the last 10 years. The reset is helping some and the glasses are helping me to relax earlier. But...this allergy stuff just makes it difficult to get good sleep. I can't breathe well. I am tired.

No, didn't hurt my eyes with the ice pack because I had to hold it in place with my hands. My eyes are still puffy and itchy. Just not as bad. My skin feels softer but that may be just because I am being more diligent about putting coconut oil on it every day.

So far so good. Thanks for the encouraging remarks.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-11T16:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13722>

After I was doing face dunks for a week, I started also dunking my arms. I was using the bathroom sink, and there is just enough room to get most of my arm in, minus the hands. I figured after dunking my face a couple times, I had all this ice water, why not use it some more?

I ended up getting a cup (with a handle because the water was so cold on my hands) to pour water on my shoulders also. I was really self-conscious about my flabby arms, so I took this extra step. And yes, it did help a bit with the flab.

One day I would dunk my right arm first, then the next day I'd dunk the left arm first (because the water would warm up during this process.)

### Jane — 2012-04-11T20:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13842>

**Day 3**

I used ice water from the fridge at 54 F this am. At first it was painful, especially eyes when I took my face out of the water to take a breath. After a few dunks it didn't hurt anymore, though that may have been due to the water warming up eventually to 58 F. I guess I'll need to add some ice next time. I did a total of 35-10 second dunks for a total of 5.8 minutes. My face temp was 62 F at the end. My sinuses ache slightly, but they've been draining ever since I started the Leptin RX. Other than that, I feel invigorated, even though I didn't sleep much last night.

### freesia — 2012-04-12T01:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-13983>

Jane, good work! 35 dunks! That's commitment!

I wondered about adding a couple of ice cubes after each dunk to counteract the warming effect? Although as skin temp lowers there will obviously be less of a warming effect.

My husband is so impressed with benefits I'm getting from the the Leptin RX. In his words "it's like you're getting two extra hours of quality time in your day" - referring to the time before lunch and dinner I don't spend in a semi-crashed state. Anyway, after being initially a bit reserved about CT, which I started talking about pretty soon after beginning the Leptin RX, he's now _fully_ supportive of my plan to cold adapt. Yay!

I have only a minor background headache, and no sinus issues today, and I'm planning to CT with a few more dunks (inspired by Jane!) after dinner tonight.

### ealachan — 2012-04-12T03:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14017>

I got in a cold tub for the first time tonight. HOLY MOLEY, people. The water was 70 degrees, and it actually wasn't hard for me to get in at all, but I started shivering about 2 minutes in. I was shocked, as spot-icing never makes me shiver, walking outside in the cold (like 30 degrees) with no coat doesn't make me shiver, walking around naked in a 60 degree house doesn't make me shiver!

I spent 10 minutes in the water, then got out. My skin where it had been under water was reading at 71 degrees when I got out. I've still got kind of a sub-shiver (just feels like slight muscle tenseness without any actual shaking) thing going on 10 minutes later. I have just a hint of that lovely leaden-legged cold-tiredness I used to get as a kid when I'd spend literally hours every day in the pool or lake.

My biggest problem is that we have a really weird bathtub and there's nothing to help haul my fat butt out of it once I get down in it. I basically have to rely on my pretty measly upper body strength to propel myself up out of the tub when I'm done and that doesn't work so well. Sooo...I think it's back to icing for me, at least until I can start getting into the lake or my mom opens her pool up.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-12T03:13:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14022>

Hi Ealachan, congrats! Yes, the tub is a lot different from icing. 70 degrees and 10 minutes for the first time sounds perfect. If you wait a week, you will find that you shiver much less the next time. Until the pool opens, you could continue icing, with maybe some face dunks, and maybe soaking your feet in ice water? Some people take cool showers, that also might help.

You'll find that several weeks of CT helps with strength.

I've been icing several times a week for about a month. I did start out with face dunks before starting with the icing. My showers are always cool, though I've only just gotten a thermometer today so I'm not sure how cool yet - I'll check in the morning!

Do you think cold (my tap water comes out at around 50 degrees at the coldest) foot soaks would have any benefit? I can easily do those...I'd just sit on the edge of the tub. I did it a couple times over the weekend, in fact.

Tonight I filled the bowl with ice water from the fridge and the temp was 54 F again. Before I dunked, I filled a pitcher with crushed ice and checked the temp of the water at 15 dunks. It had gone up to 56 F so I dumped in the ice and it went down to 51 F. I managed to get my skin temp down to 56 F.

It's great that your husband is so supportive. Mine loves how the Leptin RX is working for me, except when I'm detoxing and is supportive of how I'm eating (VLC), but he is skeptical of the CT. I didn't tell him I had started with the face dunks and he caught me at it this evening when he walked in from a meeting. I wasn't trying to hide it from him, because I'm doing it no matter what, but I wasn't sure what his reaction would be. I just want to prove to him that this works.

### Jane — 2012-04-12T06:25:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14094>

But after a few months of Leptin RX and no exercise, I'm able to pull myself out. I'm sure CT will make us superwomen!

### Jane — 2012-04-12T06:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14105>

**Day 3 pm**

Windy and cold all day, but I didn't turn on the heat, went barefoot outside and didn't put on a sweater all day. I got funny comments from DH, but oh well....

Tonight I filled the bowl with ice water from the fridge and filled a pitcher with crushed ice. I started dunking at 54 F and added ice when the temp went up. It brought the temp down to 51 F. Dunked 65 times for 10 seconds each. A total of 10.8 minutes. My skin temp was 56 F when I finished. I'm getting there! My whole face hurt at first, but after I reached 25 dunks, it stopped hurting. At 45 dunks, the water started to feel almost warm on my face so I tested it and the temp was only 54 F. It's strange but it feels relaxing, almost like meditation or a Zen sort of thing or a peaceful place. No sinus pain. My facial skin feels tight. It's interesting that a friend came to the door to drop something off and he said, "You look really great! I don't know why, but you do." Guess what, I'd just finished my CT.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-12T07:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14119>

So miserable today with these damn allergies or is it detox?

People were commenting at work. "your eye is red and swollen". My eyes itch so much. i am almost finished with the box of tissues I only bought two days ago. Damn...you would think I would lose ten pounds just from all the fluid that is coming out of my nose. I don't want to take any antihistamines because I don't want that crap drying up inside of me.

Instead my lips are getting chapped. I bought some homeopathic eye drops which are helping my eyes feel more comfortable.

Face dunk only twice tonight. Water 50 degrees F out of the tap. 10 seconds. Candlelight. I am just too wiped out and tired from this allergy stuff to put in more effort. 2nd dunk was painful.

I know the protocol says only twice for as long as I can hold my breath. I would like to push further. Just to review: (this from the Getting Started part that was just added to Dr. K's blog)

_Face Dunking:

What you need: skin thermometer, a timer, ice, and a bathroom sink or larger bowl.

Remove make-up or facial products

Get the water between 50-55 degrees

Dunk your face into the ice water and hold as long as you can

Record time and dunk again

Continue for the next two weeks and work your way to **using less breaths**

DO NOT let your skin temperature fall below 50-55 degrees_

The less breaths is confusing. Wouldn't it be for longer breaths?

### freesia — 2012-04-12T08:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14132>

@ Glamazon Less breaths/dunks to get to the ideal, or towards ideal, temperature maybe? But it would take more than 2 in the beginning for most of us I think?? That instruction is a little fuzzy around the edges!

65 Jane!! My face aches just thinking about it...

 Two hours yet until my Day 3. Looking forward to it.

### .lina — 2012-04-12T11:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14154>

i interpret less breaths as holding them for longer

i started facedunking last night after a small workout session, and it was extra hard this time because my cat Keso kept trying to drink the water from the bowl while i was in it, so i had him slurping next to my ear and i kept bursting out in laughter underwater! which is... interesting XD ... but i did a good session and found that it was easier to hold my breath after some time - i usually just get about 10 seconds max.

i need to get a thermometer though... i keep just checking my colours. but myes, proper tools.

afterwards i took a shower and i had the funniest sensation - i started out with warm water and as i increased the coldness of the water, i did shiver and gasp and such, but then as soon as i turned the water off my body and the air around me felt warm, as if i radiated warmth. it felt great, as up til this point i had only felt cold to the core from the showers. i went to bed with just a thin sheet over me and slept awesomely

and yes, i had planned only facedunking and hold off on the cold showers until after 2 weeks, but they are quite addictive so i couldn't help myself

### freesia — 2012-04-12T11:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14155>

Day 3. 6 dunks.

I realised I probably made a mistake the first day by having a warm shower right after my CT. I didn't shower my face...but anyway...

Tonight I decided to have my warm shower first, then I gradually lowered the temperature to cool and spent 3 or 4 mins quite comfortably cool. Got out, towelled off, downed another glass of iced water, wrestled on my sheepskin slippers and went straight into dunking. Water was 40, not sure on skin temp.

### Jane — 2012-04-12T19:56:54+0300

That would mean holding my breath for about 7 minutes to reach the ideal temp of 55 F. The water temp was between 51-54 the whole time. After about the 20th dunk my face doesn't hurt anymore.

>
>
>
> _Face Dunking:
>
>
>
> What you need: skin thermometer, a timer, ice, and a bathroom sink or larger bowl.
>
>
>
> Remove make-up or facial products
>
> Get the water between 50-55 degrees
>
> Dunk your face into the ice water and hold as long as you can
>
> Record time and dunk again
>

> The less breaths is confusing. Wouldn't it be for longer breaths?

I can commiserate on your allergy symptoms! Before Leptin RX, I woke every morning with clogged sinuses and blew gunk from my nose all morning. Now I don't have that problem, but my sinuses drain all day (I take that as a good thing).

Any misery I'm feeling now, I pretty much chalk up to detox. How is your D3 level? Per Dex's comments I up mine from 10,000 to 20,000 for a week when I'm really miserable. Momagrok has said she uses vitamen C to fight infections starting at 1000mg and increasing until loose bowels occur. I haven't tried this due to chronic loose bowels from IBS. She also suggested soaking in warm detox baths: 2 cups of Epsom Salts or Fermented Apple Cider Vinegar. This really eases my symptoms though a warm bath contraindicated for CT. I think it works for me because my liver is overloaded from leaky gut and detoxing through the skin helps. It's also the only way I can get my magnesium because of the IBS.

Re: breaths, I know what I'm doing right now is not according to protocol, but I can't hold my breath very long because of asthma. Also, I can't read a clock without my glasses so I count the seconds in my head. Right now I'm shooting for more time and reaching 55 F skin temp until I can. It's a n=1 thing so I don't expect anyone else to do what I'm doing.

### agatha — 2012-04-12T21:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14349>

> **Glamazon;14188 wrote: So miserable today with these damn allergies or is it detox?
>
>
>
> People were commenting at work. "your eye is red and swollen". My eyes itch so much. i am almost finished with the box of tissues I only bought two days ago. Damn...you would think I would lose ten pounds just from all the fluid that is coming out of my nose. I don't want to take any antihistamines because I don't want that crap drying up inside of me.
>
>
>
> Instead my lips are getting chapped. I bought some homeopathic eye drops which are helping my eyes feel more comfortable.
>
>
>
> Face dunk only twice tonight. Water 50 degrees F out of the tap. 10 seconds. Candlelight. I am just too wiped out and tired from this allergy stuff to put in more effort. 2nd dunk was painful.
>
>
>
> I know the protocol says only twice for as long as I can hold my breath. I would like to push further. Just to review: (this from the Getting Started part that was just added to Dr. K's blog)
>
>
>
> _Face Dunking:
>
>
>
> What you need: skin thermometer, a timer, ice, and a bathroom sink or larger bowl.
>
>
>
> Remove make-up or facial products
>
> Get the water between 50-55 degrees
>
> Dunk your face into the ice water and hold as long as you can
>
> Record time and dunk again
>
> Continue for the next two weeks and work your way to using less breaths_**_
>
> DO NOT let your skin temperature fall below 50-55 degrees_
>
>
>
> The less breaths is confusing. Wouldn't it be for longer breaths?

Hi Glamazon

sorry to hear of your suffering - the itching is worse than anything. I rub coconut oil on my nose, lips and around my eyes. doesn't sotp the itch but stops the skin becoming dry and sore from all the streaming and wiping.

### freesia — 2012-04-13T00:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14428>

> **Jane;14375 wrote:** The funny thing is that I felt the cold on my skin, but I wasn't cold. Does that make any sense?

Yes that does make sense.

I was out early this morning looking at the sunrise in a very brisk temperature - it's mid Autumn here - and I registered the chill on my face as a pleasant sensation. I breathed it in and savoured it. I've _always_ liked cool air on my face, but this was icy cold. I feel like I'm teaching my body an old forgotten language, one it has a keen desire to re-learn.

Next week I may even venture outside without the slippers.....

Happy CTing everyone.

### Jane — 2012-04-13T05:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14590>

Freesia, well put. Love this!

### Jane — 2012-04-13T06:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14592>

a.m.:

I did 65 dunks at 10 seconds each except for 3 times when I managed 20 seconds each for a total of 11.3 minutes. With tap water and ice I started at 48 F. My skin temp was 55 F after 10 dunks. I had to add ice after 20 dunks even though the water temp was 50 F at that point, just to keep my skin temp at 55. Ended with skin temp at 54 F and water at 47. I had no ill effects after the initial pain. My face and throat (which wasn't in the water) were cherry red and the skin felt tight all day. I had a lot of energy though I didn't sleep well at all last night.

p.m.:

Since I didn't sleep well last night, I thought the 65 p.m. dunks might have been too invigorating, so I decided to try for fewer dunks and seeing how long I could hold my breath. I dunked five times with variable times: 10,15 and 20 seconds.

How did everyone else do?

### .lina — 2012-04-13T10:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14633>

i tried dunking in the morning but i couldn't cover the redness afterwards with makeup

and people kept asking me if i had a tan or was blushing :O so evenings it is... hehe.

but hey, what is best - cold shower before or after dunk? or is that up to prefs maybe.

i showered after dunk last night (cat was more eager than me to fill up the bowl, lol)

and i had the lights off all the time, just some weak light from a neighbouring room.

then on with my face-mask and into bed.

btw, do any of you moisturize after dunking? i am unsure what to do, i really hate that

tight dry feeling i get after a while, but i also feel like it might counteract the cold if i use

a moisturizer?

### agatha — 2012-04-13T12:38:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14642>

### freesia — 2012-04-13T12:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14643>

Hi .lina,

I don't moisturize personally, but I do know what you mean by the tight dry feeling. It doesn't bother me too much so far. It's hard to imagine how moisturizing would interfere too much but maybe it should be no warmer than your skin when you apply it? Hope someone will correct me if they think that's the wrong advice!

@Jane, you are doing well getting some dunks up to 20 seconds! What type of skin thermometer are you using?

My day 4:

I noticed extra sweating since last night. So in the .am I took a shower and got it fractionally cooler than yesterday for the final few minutes. I'm taking things slow....no shock horror cold blasts for me!

In the evening I did six dunks, I forgot to prepare quite enough ice to counteract what turned out to be slightly warmer tap water temperature today, and my water was 45. I managed slightly longer dunks too one up to 14 seconds. I felt the water warm slightly and I was out of ice so I stopped at six when I'd have been happy to do more. Tomorrow maybe?

I find I'm looking forward to my CT each day!

Best to All.

I was startled to see that I looked better without my make-up! I must have looked more pasty before. The dunking gave me some color and a tanned face is expected here in California (though I'm not getting much sunshine at this time of year).

Even in my fifties I can't use moisturizer, never have because it makes my face break out and I like the tight feeling of my skin after the dunks. It doesn't feel drying to my face, but I have combo oily skin. I would think that after the dunk would work the best since the protocol says to cleanse the face first, but I'm not an expert.

That is so funny about the cat. I don't think mine would get anywhere near a bowl of cold water. They prefer drinking out of our backyard fishpond.

Don't know about the showers. I would think whatever works best for you would be fine.

You are doing great! At this point are you finding that the dunks and showers are helping, hindering or neutral in affecting your sleep?

### NickieG — 2012-04-14T03:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14985>

Hi

I started face dunking a week ago. Then, didn't time my eating correctly and missed dinner. I was surprised at not being hungry during the evening. I was more surprised at missing face dunking. I find it relaxing after dinner.

I have noticed acne around my mouth since face dunking and slight headaches in the evening prior to face dunking. I rarely have acne or headaches so I am wondering if this is a detox symptom. After face dunking my headaches are gone.

I am on my 5th week of the Leptin Reset.

Nickie

### Jane — 2012-04-14T04:58:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-14993>

Welcome to the thread Nickie! Acne and headaches can definitely be detox symptoms. I broke out in 2 huge pimples almost like cysts (which I've never had before) when I started the Leptin FX. The skin is one of the body's major detoxification organs. Reed Davis in a podcast at Underground Wellness said it is the body's "last resort" when the other detox organs (liver, gut, lymph etc.) are overloaded. so the CT may be stepping up your detox process already started by the Leptin reset you've been doing. It's interesting that the CT is helping the headaches...great news!

### Jane — 2012-04-14T06:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15007>

**Day 5**

a.m.:

Face dunked 70 times for 10 seconds each.

Starting water temp 50 F.

After the 20th dunk skin temp 54 F.

Ending at skin temp 57 F.

Ending water temp at 55 F.

Total minutes 11.6.

So far it seems the water temp needs to be 50 or lower to achieve and maintain a 55 F skin temp. Again, had lots of energy all day, even though I woke up feeling groggy in the morning.

p.m.:

Dunked 5 times for 10-20 seconds each. It began to wake me up so I stopped. Having been a night owl all of my life I'm still struggling to establish an earlier bedtime.

Happy dunking everyone!

Tomorrow I'm going to try dunking in the am as well, then I'll see whether I still get a headache.

### ealachan — 2012-04-15T17:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15303>

20 minutes with legs submerged to the knee in 60 degree water. Skin was 80 degrees going in, 60 coming out. Body temp was 97.5 going in, 98.8 coming out! WOO!  Toes didn't go numb at all, water didn't feel shockingly cold when I got in...the whole thing was just kind of a non-event, which I think is a good thing, right?

### freesia — 2012-04-15T23:39:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15392>

> Body temp was 97.5 going in, 98.8 coming out! WOO!

Love this!

Yep I'm going for non event, mostly. LOL. Good to be able to measure temps all the time so you know you're moving forwards. Are you getting any noticible detox from the CT ealachan?

I didn't update with my day 6 yesterday because I was running late and needed sleep!

So, **DAY 6**: About 9am I had a cool shower, turning to cooler for several minutes. Then I did 8 dunks in 45 degree water. I drank a couple of 8oz glasses of iced water during the shower and before the dunks. I had a headache free day

8.30pm I did another 8 dunks in 45 degrees. I had 3 glasses of iced water including one I finished off in bed after dunking. I had a bonus CT experience I hadn't planned on tonight! To get more of my face comfortably into the bowl I reach right under it and support it with my forearms up on a slight angle towards me. I thought I'd make my last dunk a good one but I pushed the time out a bit far and came up for air with a jerk...tipping freezing water down my front and a couple of icecubes into my clevage....LOL.

Hoping to get a thermometer so I can measure skin temp this week. I'm also planning to get those cool showers cooler yet. Gradual works best for me...accidents aside.

Glamazon hope you're doing OK. You'll be looking foward to allergy season being over!

### Jane — 2012-04-16T06:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15509>

a.m.:

45 dunks for 10 seconds each

Water temp 51 F

At 40 dunks skin temp 54 F

Ending skin temp 56 F

Ending water temp 51 F

After a lukewarm shower, I blasted cold water (64 F) on my front, back, both sides and each inner thigh for 30 seconds each. I had goosebumps but didn't shiver. It felt so refreshing.

p.m.:

5 dunks 3 for 25 seconds each and 2 at 20 seconds each

Water temp at 50 F

Ending skin temp 63 F

Instant hot flash immediately after.

Yesterday I had a sharp shooting pain in my left breast and felt bloated all day (possible estrogen dump into my system?). This morning the scale had gone down 3 pounds. After a month's stall in weight loss I'm down 4 pounds this week, for a total of 24 pounds since Leptin RX starting 2 months ago!

With weight loss and age ahem...I've noticed a bit of loose puckery skin under my chin. When I dunk, I make sure to get that area into the water too. Perhaps it's just wishful thinking, but that area seemed a bit tighter. I may just keep dunking my face even after I start the regular CT to see if it is really helping. Who needs to go under the knife for a facelift?

### Jane — 2012-04-17T06:08:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15819>

a.m.:

dunked 30 times for 10 seconds each

Water temp 48 F

After 20 dunks skin temp was 53 f

Ending water temp 48 F

Ending skin temp 53 F

After a lukewarm shower I blasted all sides of my body with cold water for about 5 minutes--didn't feel the cold at all after awhile. Sleep is still not good, but I felt refreshed for the rest of the day after the CT. The healthy pink color has returned to my lips. No more lipstick for this gal!

p.m.:

Dunked 5 times-20 seconds twice, 25 twice and 27 on the last dunk.

Water temp 50 F

Ending skin temp 60 F

I don't know if it has anything to do with the Leptin RX or CT, but since I've been dunking, every time I take my glasses off I forget to put them back on and don't remember until I try to read something. Usually I have to wear them all the time (bifocals). I won't know until I see the eye doc whether or not there is actual improvement in my prescription, but heh, I'll take it.

### freesia — 2012-04-17T11:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15840>

Good going Jane with those nice long dunks! I notice my eyesight sharpens up a bit after CT. Possibly other senses too?

Yesterday **Day 7, I showered in the evening for 10 minutes close to the cold setting then followed with dunking (8 times, 15 seconds the longest) in 45 degree water.

Today Day 8**, I showered 20 minutes inching right down until it was full on cold (checked after and it was 60 degrees). I guess the last 5 minutes were on the absolute coldest setting.

Turning the water down gradually works for me. I have check my self talk and make sure I'm registering the increasing cold as a positive thing. My warm adapted instinct is to cringe at it but I'm coaching myself to embrace it more. I didn't dunk tonight because I had neglected to make ice! When I opened the freezer just now to put iceblocks in for the morning the cold air hit me and I definitely felt it as a welcome thing.  Self talk's working?

...It's school holidays here and I've been out early with the kids the past 2 days so that's why I haven't managed two CT sessions. The rest of the week I hope to manage am and pm. Hope to stay on top of the ice situation too!

### Glamazon — 2012-04-17T16:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-15873>

Although I have not been able to do this for several days I was able to jump right back in and go longer.

I was able to do two 20 second dunks this time where as before I could barely make it to 10 seconds.

The water was 50 degrees. My skin temp after was 73 and 68 respectively on my forehead, where there is no real fat layer.

There was no pain like last time. But, I am not as sick as I was over the weekend.

As a side note I did get my telomere test results yesterday.

My real age was 25 versus my chronological age of 54. Hmmmm. I started leptin reset on 2/21/12 and had my blood drawn on 2/30. I posted more about it on the telomere thread.

I was \*not\* expecting that.

I also want to add, thanks for the kind words. It was a beyatch of weekend including water leakage thru the ceiling yesterday. Sigh. I just have a slight case of the runny nose and itchy eyes now.

I am encouraged by all the progress everyone is making. Good job! woo hoo!

### Glamazon — 2012-04-18T16:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-16202>

Skin temp before 95 on forehead.

Water temp 50. Two dunks.

Skin temp after 73. 10 second dunk

Skin temp after 75. 15 second dunk

The second one hurt this time.

Interesting affect of doing them in the AM after my breakfast. I think I am even more alert during the day.

Hi Ladies, enjoying watching your journey. I'm up to baths, but what I did with the dunks is use a snorkel. All that in and out was annoying to me. I easily got to 20 mins in two weeks of icy water. Using an IF therm, water would get to about 32ish and face about 45-50.

And, I have kept the face dunks! When I started I had some detox symptoms from just that, so it must be doing SOMETHING. Also, both myself and my hubby think our wrinkles and skin looked better on dunking. So I've kept it in case it actually helps in the vanity dept.

Plus, with my head in water for 20 mins, nobody can talk to me!

### freesia — 2012-04-19T01:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-16375>

I think detox has hit here too. (Hence no update yesterday.)

I'm just 18 days into LR so it's hard for me to tell what exactly is causing detox. I'm not getting any obvious estrogen dumping or hormone shift, but over the past few days low blood pressure has been bothering me more and more. I also feel as if I might be becoming intolerant of my my morning cup of coffee. So yesterday, **Day 9**, I took it easy and just did 10 dunks in 50 degree water. Didn't push it and made sure my husband was close by. I drank iced water all evening. Love it.

Feeling better today and will update day 10 later.

Jane, hope you're feeling better today.

Glamazon - love that alert feeling! Years ago I always used to end my morning shower with a blast of cold all over. The half hour after that was the best I felt all day

Cavemam - thanks for the tips. 20 minutes!!

### Glamazon — 2012-04-20T16:13:24+0300

### AKMan — 2012-04-20T18:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17128>

You folks doing the face-dunking: You are SO ready for a cold bath! I resisted it for a long time, thinking it would be miserable. The face dunking is really just getting your body ready for what's to come, if you just jump into cold water, unaclimated, you'd hyperventillate, heart would race, and vessel contraction would cause problems. Since your body now knows what cold is, it will let you 'chill-lax' on your cold dips.

If I could start over, here's what I'd do...face dunks for a week or so. Then, fill a tub as full as you can with water as cold as you can get it--32 degrees, if possible. Use lots of ice, make it so it's forming a skim of ice on top and ice cubes don't melt. Get it at least calf-deep. Step in very fast and experience the pain, shock, gasping, weird noises you'll make, all that. Stand there as long as you can, but at least a couple minutes. Feel how the pain goes away and is replaced by numbness. Drop down to you knees if you can. Do this naked. If you are male, watch in awe as your "meat and two veg" shrink up and disappear (but nothing compared to waist deep in freezing water, trust me).

On this first dip, don't go even think about full immersion, just the feet and/or legs.

Next night...fill tub with 55 or 60 degree water. Stand in it and notice the difference from 32 degree water--night&day, right? Drop to knees, then sit in it as deep as you can go. Shoot for 10 minutes, but you'll be able to do more. As you step in and settle into the water, pay close attention to your breathing. Breath normally, deeply, don't let your refelexes take over.

Do 55 or 60 degrees for a couple days, but my bet is you'll be down to 45 degrees in a couple days. And the thought of a dip in 32 degree water will eat at you until you do a full body in 32 degrees. I can happily say, 'been there, done that'!

I will be watching you guys progress, can't wait to see how you do.

Good luck!

AKMan

### Jane — 2012-04-20T21:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17178>

a.m.:

50 dunks at 10 seconds each

Starting water temp 49 F

At 30 dunks skin temp 54 F

Ending water temp 50 F

Ending skin temp 55 F

Cold shower (65) hitting all sides and body parts for 1 minute each for a total of 10 minutes after lukewarm shower. I love the cold showers, contrary to popular belief they seem to wake up my libido....

Detox was not as bad! I didn't do any CT in the evening, but did sit with my legs in 70 F pool for about an hour. My sleep is still crappy, waking up almost every hour on the hour from midnight on.

### freesia — 2012-04-21T08:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17337>

I missed a day of CT, yesterday. Low blood pressure problems\* have worsened steadily the past 5 - 6 days and yesterday it got so I couldn't stand without dizziness. I had to be rescued from the city and brought safely home!...Bit embarrasing...but nevermind...moving on...

I'm not sure if I'm low on electrolytes. I took some mineral salt and potassium in water after dinner last night, and again before breakfast today, and I am feelng much better. I'd put the strange feelings, incl low blood pressure, down as a consequence of detox. I have high arsenic and lead reading from a recent toxic minerals hair test so perhaps LR and CT is mobilising some real nasties.

I took chelators last thing yesterday too. Wore sexy blue-blockers from sundown and slept really well. I was well enough to hit the farmer's market this morning so all is good.

This afternoon (**Day 11**) I had a cold shower and kept it on a cold blast for a solid 15 minutes. After I got out I did 16 dunks in iced water (45 degrees). I drank a litre of deliciously freezing iced water over the hour before the shower.

AK Man I like the method you suggested! Hoping to start baths when the kids are back at school next Monday.

Like Jane I'm quite enjoying the showers.

\*I have lowish blood pressure at the best of times.

### ealachan — 2012-04-21T18:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-17440>

15 min @ 60 degrees, legs only. Water felt warm after about 3 minutes...weird! Didn't actually want to finish when the timer went off, but given what happened after CT last weekend (whether it was the CT that caused it or not), I figured I'd go slow for a while.

Skin temp was 80 before I got in, 62 when I got out. Body temp (oral) went up 2.5 degrees 10 minutes after CT.

Hi Lanie, nice to have you along

**Day 12**, Had a busy morning so I just did evening CT. I've moved to two meals on the Leptin Reset, bringing my evening meal forward a bit so it worked quite well for me to hit the shower right after I'd dished up the rest of the family's meal. Still nice and full with plenty of fat in the tank, which I do think helps for withstanding prolonged cold. Anyway, nice to be finished earlier and in bed with a book (& sexy-blueblockers) at the same time as the kids! So anyway, I had a nice long cold shower followed by 20 face dunks in 45 degrees. I had a litre of iced water but half was drunk in bed after my CT. Not sure how ideal that was.

I feel baths in my near future...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-23T07:29:09+0300

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-04-23T20:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18119>

32 degrees?!?! Wowsers, that's cold! How did you work up to that? Why not the 50-55 degrees that Dr. Kruse recommends on the blog? I would also agree about the showers. I can spend and hour in my 50 degree pool, but 50 degree showers are BRUTAL!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-23T22:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18166>

> **Trisha;18192 wrote:** 32 degrees?!?! Wowsers, that's cold! How did you work up to that? Why not the 50-55 degrees that Dr. Kruse recommends on the blog? I would also agree about the showers. I can spend and hour in my 50 degree pool, but 50 degree showers are BRUTAL!

I just decided to do it! I was motivated by trying to steepen my personal curve to help with sub-q fat. I only intended 10 mins - but once in, I thought it was easier to do than some warmer temps. So even though I thought I was not adapting very fast, it was a total mental boost because if I can sit down in freezing water for 30 mins then I must be adapting! (hands, feet, head out).

I think those of you in pools and swimming, temp needs to be approached more carefully as you are getting the whole body freezing.

I also am having some major detox post bath and today. I'm going to take the day off CT to try and process through it - so I don't know if I gained that much other than the smug satisfaction of knowing I can do it!

### freesia — 2012-04-24T07:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18302>

Current detox symptoms....PIMPLES. Hmmm.

The past 2 days I've had showers starting at the M on WARM (pretty cool really) gradually decreasing to COLD over 4 - 5 minutes then giving myself a full 20 minutes on COLD. From about 8 minutes in to my shower I'm loving the cold. What I like about my shower is luxury of being able to adjust the temp down like that. My soft attitude might change when I'm more adapted?!  Another thing I like is that the daily temperature is coming down as we head into Winter which means COLD in the shower should be coming down fractionally each day too. Yes my shower set-up is like CT for Dummies. I like it. I also like the interesting poses I arrange myself into to get maximum surface coverage. I'll miss that in the bath!

My shower is over the bath. So I plan to keep the plug in tomorrow and shower myself right down into that bath... I'm not promising ice!

Hope everyone elses is doing well.

### freesia — 2012-04-24T07:40:35+0300

### Amazonmagic — 2012-04-24T16:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18353>

> **freesia;18377 wrote:** Oh I envy you your lake!! Nothing like making CT actual recreation to make the time speed by. (Good on the kids!)

I actually live on a peninsula, so I have many glacier fed lakes and the Puget Sound right outside my door. So I did it people, i CT'd in the Puget Sound (inlet off the Pacific Ocean), it was wonderful, I only lasted about 30 minutes though, not because of the cold, but because the theme song of Jaws began going through my head and my flight or fight instincts kicked in, I flew! So I have been getting some detox signs, not much, a few deep pimples on my face, bacne (dang it), and my paitence is thinner, but I just tell myself detox and breathe through it. That was until I did 2 days of open water CTing. I injured my wrist in 2008 trying to lift something heavy( I was a steel carpenter at the time, I could life heavy things). I sprang it pretty bad and it was extrememly weak for a couple of months, it healed and every once in a blue moon my wrist tweeks out on me again for about a week, especially when I tried to jump in to full pushups. Well during open water CT's that wrist has been aching, like I sprang it again, the ache stops after about 15 minutes in the water, but for about 30 minutes after I CT it's as weak as when I sprang it but without the pain. Anyone have any clue what this could be? I just think its so cool! Jusk like Jack said I am curing stuff I didn't even know was a problem from way back when! Also, I must have had a toxic dump, because for the first time I COULD NOT SLEEP, and I woke up swollen like I had gorged on a salt feast (dinner was backed chicken and 3 peices of broccoli), and my blook sugar was 118 and I had maybe 10 carbs, oh well, just tell the liver to heal heal heal!

Legs in 55 degree water for 20 minutes. Skin was 80 deg going in, 58 coming out.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-25T07:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18625>

I had my first private Skype session with Dr. Kruse today. Highly recommend it if you can. It really helps you pinpoint or drill down to what you need personally especially if you have effed up physiology like mine. Going to go straight to icing starting tomorrow after supper. If you do schedule a session with him come prepared. Send him a history of what is going on with you, what has gone on with you. Add what has worked and what hasn't. let him know about your parents' health. Include past and current labs, surgeries, etc.Tell him what you want to accomplish. For me it boils down to being optimal.

One of the takeaways for me is to get more labs done, like my genome, and schedule a sleep study. For people who are really screwed up physiologically you can not beat this one on one to help you get clarity in what direction may be best for you. This process is all about fine tuning and refining for me and he is the best coach for this.

Even one session will save you a lot of time that might be wasted.

### Jane — 2012-04-25T22:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18827>

Hi Folks,

I've been too busy to get to my computer for the last few days, but I've kept up my CT except Monday when I took a day off. Basically, I did my face dunks same as before (a.m.s 50 times). I've moved on to cold showers (65 F) for about 10 to 15 minutes. I've also iced my belly and back for 20-25 minutes e/o day. I think I'm ready for the CT baths.

I've been doing the iodine protocol and am still VLC on the Leptin Reset. Detox is a constant, but I'm determined to get through this. My sleep is crappy, pretty much awake all night after wicked nightmares (probably the Melatonin). I think my main problem trying to sleep is my DH's snoring. It constantly wakes me up and keeps my adrenaline pumping. I'm going to try earplugs. Wish me luck!

The good news is that I was absentmindedly running my fingers over my belly the other night in bed and suddenly noticed that the stretch marks weren't as noticeable! I lost another pound this last week. That makes 25 pounds since the middle of February, a quarter of my weight-loss goal.

Glad to see everyone is doing well. Hello to the newcomers, glad you can join us on this journey to optimal health.

Jane

### Jane — 2012-04-25T22:35:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18832>

> **Amazonmagic;18426 wrote:** I actually live on a peninsula, so I have many glacier fed lakes and the Puget Sound right outside my door. So I did it people, i CT'd in the Puget Sound (inlet off the Pacific Ocean), it was wonderful, I only lasted about 30 minutes though, not because of the cold, but because the theme song of Jaws began going through my head and my flight or fight instincts kicked in, I flew! So I have been getting some detox signs, not much, a few deep pimples on my face, bacne (dang it), and my paitence is thinner, but I just tell myself detox and breathe through it. That was until I did 2 days of open water CTing. I injured my wrist in 2008 trying to lift something heavy( I was a steel carpenter at the time, I could life heavy things). I sprang it pretty bad and it was extrememly weak for a couple of months, it healed and every once in a blue moon my wrist tweeks out on me again for about a week, especially when I tried to jump in to full pushups. Well during open water CT's that wrist has been aching, like I sprang it again, the ache stops after about 15 minutes in the water, but for about 30 minutes after I CT it's as weak as when I sprang it but without the pain. Anyone have any clue what this could be? I just think its so cool! Jusk like Jack said I am curing stuff I didn't even know was a problem from way back when! Also, I must have had a toxic dump, because for the first time I COULD NOT SLEEP, and I woke up swollen like I had gorged on a salt feast (dinner was backed chicken and 3 peices of broccoli), and my blook sugar was 118 and I had maybe 10 carbs, oh well, just tell the liver to heal heal heal!

I too have found that my old injuries are being revisited. I'm thinking it's a part of the healing process. Re: sleep, I've read that a lot of people had sleep issues after starting CT, when their hormones calmed down, sleep returned. Hormones also affect blood sugar, so I assume that the readings will be a bit wacky until our systems heal.

I don't have the Puget Sound (lucky you!) but I do have a river that flows from the bottom of a very deep man-made lake that remains freezing cold throughout our scorching summers here in California's Central Valley. I'm going to take a walk down there today and check out the temp.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-26T13:39:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-18991>

Started with a cold shower today. The water comes out of the tap at 50 degrees. I started warm and turned it down to not all the way cold but cold enough compared to my usual temp. I kept it mostly on my my upper back and neck which is one of the brown fat sites. I stayed for 2 minutes. It is a shock but the gradual cooling made it easier. When I say gradual I mean less than a minute to switch over. I going away for a long weekend today and there won't be ice there so I will be doing cold showers as I can. It will be ice time when I come back.

The session itself does wake me up. I feel very alert.

I told Dr. K that I hope soon to go up into the mountains and start soaking in snow melt in one of the creeks. He said, "as often as you can." This new way of living requires major lifestyle adjustments!!!

### Amazonmagic — 2012-04-26T17:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19031>

SO CT has been normal. On Wednesday I didn't want to! I couldn't do it outside but forced myself to do it in the tub, I was shivering at 64 degrees, crazy. The next day I was down and depressed, just let it flow, and it went away as soon as I did my CT last night, which I did a little differently. I usually lay on my back and my feet, legs, back, arms and shoulders are in the water with an ice pack on my belly, but yesterday I turned over and dunked from the other side. More was covered in water (only butt cheeks and upper back were out and wow was it easy. I did face dunks while soaking in 53 degree water right out of the tap. I'm getting there.

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T02:53:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19202>

20 min, 50 degree water (it was coming out of the tap at 47), legs only.

Skin was 85 degrees going in, 53 coming out. 10 minutes later, skin still feels very cold to the touch but I have no sensation of my legs being cold, if that makes sense.

Oral temp before I went in: 98.6

Oral temp after 12 minutes in water: 99.1

Oral temp at end of 20 min: 99.1

HOLY MOTHER OF SNOWBALLS! Coldest I've done so far. It felt like a million pins and needles stabbing my skin, especially my feet, the whole time. My skin turned lobster-red within 5 minutes. About 10 minutes post-soak, skin is mostly back to normal color even though it's still very cold to the touch.

Really tired as I was getting out of the tub - felt like I'd climbed a mountain and done a bazillion sit-ups.

### Birdy — 2012-04-27T03:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19208>

I was in the soak tub outside for about 20 minutes. After a few minutes, I didn't even really feel it, though parts above water were shivering a little bit at the beginning. I thought a storm was going to pick up, and I didn't want that only because it would wreck my book. Afterwards, I dried off and warmed up by emptying out my tub and watering all my plants. I am still not completely warmed up, but I haven't put all that many clothes back on yet.

### freesia — 2012-04-27T05:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19246>

I've been doing shower --> baths the last three mornings.

It takes a full 10 minutes in the coldest shower setting before I feel my body start to strengthen against the cold. I LOVE it when that kicks in. I sip iced water and rub iceblocks on my face while I'm in the shower and I pretend I'm at some flash spa taking the most expensive treatment on the menu.  I'd love to try this outdoors sometime.

The first time I tried a bath I underestimated how much ice I'd need, so it was a bit disappointing. Yesterday and today I'd made big ice bricks by filling 6 500ml yogurt containers with water and sitting them in the freezer overnight. I used a big bag of regular iceblocks as well. I sat the yogurt containers in the bath when I started and the ice popped right out of them by the time I was ready to sit down. This morning I was rubbing one of those huge iceblocks all over any skin that wasn't underwater (upper arms, chest and face) and it didn't even feel cold there because I'd already been well conditioned in the shower which was running at 57F. I stayed a further 15 minutes and it wasn't uncomfortable anywhere but my feet and hands. My feet are still cold four hours later. I haven't shivered yet, so I guess I'm taking it relatively gently?

The overnight temperature is forecast to go down to 50F here over the next week so my water (from an artesian aquifier) temperature will go down too. I'll just stick with what I'm doing and let nature take me down to the next level.

Detox. Yep. Pimples...pushing through concrete type fatigue, broken sleep, general achiness and some strange digestive upsets....nothing major, just unfamiliar.

### freesia — 2012-04-27T05:15:05+0300

I'd love to do this outside! I'd have thought of the book too  ...If this really takes off there might be a good market for weather proof books!

### ealachan — 2012-04-27T16:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19325>

Lest anyone get the wrong idea, I didn't go straight into 50 degrees. I've been working my way down from 65 degrees since I started doing soaks, and I did a few weeks of spot icing before that. I would never advocate someone going super cold without having worked up to it. Bad idea.

### freesia — 2012-04-29T01:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19688>

I've forgotten what day it is  Week three sometime LOL.

I skipped a day yesterday due to time constraints. Today I had a 20 minute shower, cool --> COLD, getting down for the final 10 minutes to 53%. I shivered for the first time. It was pretty cold. Then 15 minutes in the bath at 50. More shivering but no real discomfort. I added more ice after 5 minutes to keep it at 50.

I got out nice and pink and I've been shivering (gently) the past 30 minutes.

I don't have weight to lose (maybe a few pounds?) but I do need to improve my body composition, plus hopefully reap a few other rewards from CT. Today I weighed to check and I'm down 4 pounds. I stayed completely stable the first 3 weeks of LRx. I can spare another 4 pounds maybe but I might need to look at eating more.

Still shivering, and typing is tricky so I'll sign off wishing everyone a great weekend!

### ealachan — 2012-04-29T03:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-19703>

Holy suffering SeaMonkeys!! Fat girl not only got her fat butt INTO the tub tonight, but she GOT IT OUT. Awwww yeah. Victory points for me! For those who don't recall, the last time I tried sitting in the tub for CT, I had a really hard time getting out because, frankly, I'm too damn heavy to hoist myself back up without grab bars, pulleys, blocks and tackles, a team of draft horses and a suspension of the gravitational force itself. But tonight? TONIGHT...I just gave it the ol' heave-ho at the end of my 20 minutes in the water, and had WAY less struggle than last time. It's not like I weigh significantly less, so I'mma chalk that up to a strength gain and be overjoyed about it.

Anyway...the pertinents from tonight:

Water temp: 52 deg F Duration: 20 min, though it took me a while to actually ease down into the water, so I'll call it 15 minutes of actually sitting in the water fully.

Before CT: oral temp 97.7, skin temp 82

After CT: oral temp 99.0, skin temp 60 on lower legs, 70 on butt where I was touching the sides of the tub / didn't have good water circulation

Notes: Think my O6:O3 is still out of whack because my toe went white again. Just one toe, doesn't hurt, just turns waxy white. It looks pretty zombie-ish still, 15 min later. I did try to keep my toes out of the water once I got settled, but it didn't seem to matter. Tons of goosebumps while I was in the water, but no actual shivering while I was in. Slight amount of shivering started about 10 min post-CT, but not actually noticeable shaking...just muscles feeling tense. Felt like I'd climbed a mountain again (all in my legs) when I got out of the bath.

20 minutes post-bath, oral temp is 98.7...WHEEEEEEEEE!

### freesia — 2012-05-02T02:21:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-20563>

Week three update.

The cold water went down to 55, then 50, then today...46!

Yesterday I skipped CT as I'd had a sinus headache since the day before and it wasn't getting any better. Usually I treat sinus problems with high dose vitamin C, but since I'm doing the Leptin Rx and down to 2 meals, I limited my C intake to mealtimes...and that was pretty ineffective on the sinus front.

I've gone shower --> bath two days, and shower only the other days. The last bath, on Sunday, was 48. I stayed in 15 minutes, after a 20 minute "conditioning shower" and I took 3 hours to warm up.

Today's 46 degree shower was pretty brutal.  The bathroom temperature was only 54, though that felt cosy when I exited the shower! I shivered through most my shower and ended up with blue hands, which returned to normal colour 10 minutes after. Skin was nice and pink and I'm still shivering 1 1/2 hours later.

### freesia — 2012-05-03T00:59:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-20849>

The cold water's at 46 again and I'm putting off getting into my shower.

Yesterday I shivered for SIX hours. The water and air temperature had taken a dive from the previous day and I usually suffer when that happens anyway (no temperature control in the house which is usual here)...but yesterday was a bit extreme.

So anyway....what did I do at 4.30pm? I ran a warm/hot bath, put in mg flakes and soaked for 1/2 hour and warmed up! Felt like a truck had hit me when I got out but I was more functional than before. If I hadn't either have done that or put lots of heat on in the house (which we usually avoid doing) there would have been no dinner on the table last night! I couldn't use my hands properly b/c of the cold.

I'm going to try the same today and hope that my recovery time is shorter.

If not, what can I do to warm up (after several hours) that's more optimal than a hot bath? Excercise? Or is a warm/hot bath at the other end of the day OK?

Thanks if anyone can offer advice

### freesia — 2012-05-06T07:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/come-ct-with-me-for-30-days-start-monday-april-9th.734/post-21741>

I skipped another day, then I've done two in a row.

Both yesterday and today the tap water read at 57. So I showered 15 minutes. then I added ice to the water that had collected in the bathtub and sat there another 15 minutes. Yesterday the bath read at 58 degrees (not enough ice) and today I got it to 54.

I don't know if anyone else has found similar, but a shower at 57 is much, **much** more skin numbing than a bath at 54. I turn around in the shower and make sure I concentrate the blast at my (emerging?) BAT area and my back goes completely numb even at 57.

Tonight I'm making twice the ice because 54 baths aren't really doing it for me. Not after the shower.

Goal for this coming week is to get baths to 50, and to rig up a spot icing garment.

Hope other Newbie CTers are doing well!!

### Glamazon — 2012-05-07T01:28:37+0300

I just want to update y'all on what's happening with me. I had a private consult with Dr. K two weeks ago and I am doing a re-assess of my plans. My physiology is really jacked up which needs some pointed health coaching by Dr. K. I can go deeper now to help myself. I am holding off until I get my labs all drawn so I can have a baseline before I go full bore on this. I sent off my 23andme for DNA/SNP profile. I am awaiting for my salivary endocrine profile test to arrive so I can do that and send it back. (My insurance doesn't cover bio identical type treatments and labs.) I am also having some more labs done through my regular provider and a sleep study done. I am exploring cryotherapy locally.

[http://belowzerocolorado.com/](http://http://belowzerocolorado.com/)

I want to just accelerate what I can do as quick as possible and then look at what I can do at home.

You all sound like you are doing well. I hope to re-join you again soon. Still a newb that's for sure.

### chocolate — 2012-04-08T21:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lactic-acidosis-and-metformin-anaerobic-energy-source-lactic-acid-inhibits-melanin.735/post-12759>

[http://www.livestrong.com/article/373575-lactic-acid-pumping-up-a-muscle/](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/373575-lactic-acid-pumping-up-a-muscle/)

[http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEoQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.skincaretalk.com%2Ft%2F24927%2Fwill-lactic-acid-peel-lighten-past-my-natural-complexion&ei=tdeBT5ziG5HRiALR34n0Ag&usg=AFQjCNH25TlAq1mN-dEs3ZTDhtT9vDHDfQ&sig2=lowHGjAa8N\_JEUixrEf7KA](http://http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEoQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.skincaretalk.com%2Ft%2F24927%2Fwill-lactic-acid-peel-lighten-past-my-natural-complexion&ei=tdeBT5ziG5HRiALR34n0Ag&usg=AFQjCNH25TlAq1mN-dEs3ZTDhtT9vDHDfQ&sig2=lowHGjAa8N_JEUixrEf7KA)

[http://www.metforminguide.com/](http://http://www.metforminguide.com/)

Rare But Serious Metformin Side Effects

There is a very rare, but very serious side effect that can occur while taking metformin. The condition is called Lactic Acidosis. If you are having any of these symptoms: increased sleepiness or unexplained lethargy, weakness, decreased or slowed heart rate, cold sensations, stomach pains, or if you are feeling light headed or have fainted either before or after taking metformin you could be showing some of the symptoms of lactic acidosis. This is a dangerous syndrome and you should seek emergency medical help right away.

Chest pain has been reported and other metformin side effects such as flushing, red face, etc. You should also be concerned if you are allergic to metformin or have allergic reactions after taking it. Allergic reactions can be marked by rashes, hives, itching, difficulties with breathing, or unexplained swelling.

[http://www.merckvetmanual.com/mvm/index.jsp?cfile=htm/bc/21703.htm](http://http://www.merckvetmanual.com/mvm/index.jsp?cfile=htm/bc/21703.htm) lactic acidosis with grain overload

### chocolate — 2012-04-08T23:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lactic-acidosis-and-metformin-anaerobic-energy-source-lactic-acid-inhibits-melanin.735/post-12788>

that glucose was insufficient as an energy carrier during intense synaptic activity and finally, that lactate can be an efficient energy substrate capable of sustaining and enhancing brain aerobic energy metabolism in vitro.\[21\] The paper was positively commented by Kasischke: "The study by Ivanov et al. (2011) also provides novel data on biphasic NAD(P)H fluorescence transients, an important physiological response to neural activation that has been reproduced in many studies and that is believed to originate predominately from activity-induced concentration changes to the cellular NADH pools."\[22\]

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lactic\_acid](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lactic_acid)

Lactic acid is developing brain energy, I guess it would be repairing brain energy also. A lot of thinking has changed about lactic acid in the past couple years. I know that there are a couple of ridiculously muscular women I've seen around that have this all over golden glow and I am sure its the lactic acid causing it. It looks breathtaking.

### chocolate — 2012-04-10T02:06:55+0300

# Thread: Have I had success? Question for experienced CTers.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T05:00:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12889>

Hi Friends,

I have been doing spot ice regularly since March 22, 2012 and can easily do 50 minutes without going below 55F on the spot. I decided it was time to start the baths. I have taken three baths so far. My internal temperature dropped to 95.3F after 20 minutes in 71F water. I know kind of sad performance!

I had my third bath in 68F water and I raise the temperature of the tub water to 70F after 30 minutes. My internal temperature went from 96.8F to 98.6F! Normal body temperature is not "normal" for me. This is the first time my internal temperature has gone up during a cold treatment.

Is this what others have experienced? Should I expect to see more increases in body temperature after cold treatment?

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-09T05:07:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12893>

I started super slow at 80 deg. and went down one degree a day, although by accident it often ended up being a few degrees down. I found that each day even though the water was colder I felt warmer and more comfortable. Finally I just jumped down to 55 and then 50 and feel almost the same.

Just go slow and safe and know that it will all happen sooner than you might think and more comfortably too. I think your core temp going up is a great sign, and don't feel bad about not just diving into 50 deg. water at the start.

### shilohman — 2012-04-09T05:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12895>

>
>
>
> Is this what others have experienced? Should I expect to see more increases in body temperature after cold treatment?

How are you measuring your core temp? Did you take your temp several times to verify the 98.6?

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T16:31:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12964>

I am taking my temperature orally. I use a basal thermometer which is an ultra sensitive thermometer that tracks the body's minutest temperature shifts.

### quelsen — 2012-04-09T16:43:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12968>

mine raised then fell and has never come back up. have no idea why that is

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T16:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12969>

> **PaleoMom;12962 wrote:** I started super slow at 80 deg. and went down one degree a day, although by accident it often ended up being a few degrees down. I found that each day even though the water was colder I felt warmer and more comfortable. Finally I just jumped down to 55 and then 50 and feel almost the same.
>
>
>
> Just go slow and safe and know that it will all happen sooner than you might think and more comfortably too. I think your core temp going up is a great sign, and don't feel bad about not just diving into 50 deg. water at the start.

Yes, I have noticed that too. I am finding that my cold tolerance is increasing surprising quickly. I am still feel "cold" and I am not radiating heat yet. I have a little talk with myself as I watch my internal temperature drop to borderline hypothermia. I say to myself: "cold is my friend" and "I am safe". It seems counter intuitive to get "cold" to get "warm" but at the same time makes sense to my right-brain kind of thinking.

In the last few days I have also increased my coconut cream and coconut oil intake to try to stoke my internal fire without the downsides of stimulants like coffee.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T16:47:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12971>

Have you tried large doses of coconut cream or coconut oil? When I look back at my log this is the one thing I have changed in the last week. (Of course, I am very new to all this.)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T17:04:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-12982>

> **Jack Kruse;13036 wrote:** When cold adaptatipon begins for real you will see that you can raise your core temperature and you can directly alter your cortisol levels if you test for them as I do.......

Hi Dr Kruse,

I would like to do testing. Unfortunately, my family doctor(Dr Kevin Worry) has moved far up north. I have been thinking about the Life Extension's testing protocols, but I live in Canada. (I have been meaning to contact them about blood pulling services. I will put it on my list for today's actions.)

I am sure that there is a way through this problem of testing. I want to learn more about testing protocols too. I have gone way past the point of being a passive patient letting the doctor do all the thinking. (Of course, this will make it difficult to find another doctor.) I truly want to understand rather than blindly following directions without understanding - at least partly - why.

If you have any suggestions I would greatly appreciate it.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-09T19:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-13030>

Hi Dr Kruse,

I just checked out Life Extension and the only test they can do for Canadians is the omega score because it is a prick test:

[http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/ItemLCOMEGA/Omega-Score-Blood-Test.html?source=search&key=omega%20blood%20testing](http://http://www.lef.org/Vitamins-Supplements/ItemLCOMEGA/Omega-Score-Blood-Test.html?source=search&key=omega%20blood%20testing)

All the other tests require I come down to the states and set up a US address for results.

I have looked at the Life Extension list of doctors for Canada:

[http://www.lef.org/Health-Wellness/InnovativeDoctorsInternational/directoryofdoctorsint.html#CANADA](http://http://www.lef.org/Health-Wellness/InnovativeDoctorsInternational/directoryofdoctorsint.html#CANADA)

In general, my experience "interviewing" doctors to find out if our methods will mesh hasn't been a positive experience. Just attempting to find doctors that will accept my SCD/GAPS/modified paleo diet will be a challenge. I wonder how a ketogenic diet combined with cold thermogenesis will go over! I want to work with a doctor that will send me home with my test results so I can learn more about the results in my own time and have intelligent questions for the next consultation.

I really want a doctor for twice a year testing and check-in. I don't go to doctors for little problems. I want a doctor that can see the big picture. I want a doctor that can look at the test results and give me guidance for lifestyle changes. I don't want medication.

Holy, it happened again. My temperature went up from 36.6C(97.8F) to 37.0C(98.6F) while I was in the cold tub for 30 minutes at 20C(68F). Wow. When something works it works fast.

My temperature then dropped after 15 minutes out of the tub. This is very interesting. I have to get cold to get hot.

### Sherry — 2012-04-12T16:37:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-14211>

> **quelsen;13037 wrote:** mine raised then fell and has never come back up. have no idea why that is

The exact same thing is happening to me after being in the 50-55 cold tubs on average of 4X/week for over 7 weeks now. And, I eat a LOT of CO each day. Lately I'm also experiencing no problem with the temperature for the first 30 minutes. I do seem almost warm and then when it gets to about 30 minutes I start shivering and keep myself submerged by pure willpower alone. ### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-13T16:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-had-success-question-for-experienced-cters.737/post-14672>

Hi Friends,

I was in my 21C(70F) tub yesterday and my nine year old daughter decided to come in. She is naturally very cold tolerant and spends much of her day outside. She partly got in but raised the tub temperature to 23C(74F) within about five minutes. She got out and had a hot bath. My temperature was 36.9C before and dropped in the tub to 36.5C. Within 15 minutes of leaving the tub my temperature was 35.5C.

I am working of increasing coconut products in my diet. I feel stuffed this week with two meals. I am working on a recipe using coconut cream. I'm thinking of calling it Dr Kruse's Creamy Coconut Candy, since he inspired this experiment. I will post a link next week.
