Thanks V, it's coming slowly, but steady. I finally KNOW I have a pathway that makes sense.

Today, I have an appointment with a local doc and will try and convince her to rx me T, LDN, and progesterone. Is that too much to ask?! Noooooooooo! I want to give this protocol a good strong dose, so gimme what I want.

### Martin — 2012-09-23T16:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-53582>

Scored.

My great local, integrative doc wrote rx's for everything I wanted. She even suggested an inexpensive source for the rx's and this place had it all! GREAT prices!!! And to top it off, she offers blood testing at her cost with a lab she recently added. A VAP for $39.00?!!! I paid two bills for my first one!

She was interested in my ultimate dx of biotoxin illness as being main issue in my long-standing problems. I had gone to her early this year and I was unsure if what I had going on was Lyme or whatever. Turned out to be mold, but the pathways after the initial exposure are the same, more or less.

So! I scored T. cypionate, Prometrian, and Low Dose Naltrexone. I'm dosing sea salt heavily for aldosterone, and tightening up my light exposure now that the days are getting shorter.

But CTing is getting tough since the cool weather has hit. Working on a better strategy for confronting getting into water that feels like liquid nitrogen. Brutal.

Collards are growing fast! We got 3.5 inches of rain last week so everyone is happy.

Still working on moving from the place. Picking up after thirteen years in a place is daunting.

### Martin — 2012-09-26T16:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-54416>

Still no BRHT delivered yet.

I had a good day this week with less pain and fatigue. Usually I feel good in the mornings for about two hours, but go wonky after 11 am. But the good times held all day and I got some fun in and even some useful work. Cleaned out my woodworking shop and started on finishing up an project that has been in there forever. Good times!

I'd like more than a day of feeling better.

Howza 'bout it, o' Universe?! Gimme a friggin' week! Is that too much to ask?! Maybe after I start jabbin' my leg with T and downing hormone pills, I'll get to see what the old days felt like again.

The rental project is proceeding nicely. Went before the zoning board and they loved the project. After the meeting, several members came up to me and said they really liked the project. I started to slip them a fifty, but decided to wait for the meeting with the mayor and city council. One has to put their graft and corruption money in the right places in these tough economic times.

Even the neighbors voiced no opposition and liked what we are doing.

We have good taste, so what's not to love?!

Attached files [ ](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/55186=475-IMG_6943-1.jpg)[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/55186=476-IMG_6943.jpg)

### Martin — 2012-09-29T16:31:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-55202>

Got a call from the online pharmacy telling me the doc's office screwed up and didn't call in my rx.

Damn.

Goda-mighty, people, can we get something related to my life right the first time? Is that too much to ask as day by day, my life slips by?

And now it's the weekend, so no deliveries.

It seems that every hour I've got to be shoving something down my gullet. If it ain't some supp or chalky-tasting sweet goop (CSM), I've got to pile a meal on that I've just had to prepare. Gone are the days where my "telemetric" youth is getting me by.

Oysters again this fine morn. There is a god and it has provided the world with little shelly-fish so I can sit by an imaginary shore and munch away until my brain plumps up a bit. Smarter for it? I'd settle for less pain and fatigue. But then I'd be bitchin' that I ain't smart enough.

I'm still aghast at the fall in my productivity these past years. I never was a ball of fire, but this last year has really seen a low. I manage, but I need more than feeling like I had a hard day of work just because I put on my shoes and went out to the car to get something I forgot.

My mom always said to count my blessings, so I should shut up and reflect upon my good fortune. I have a pathway. I have answers. I have enough money to take the next step. I do kinda feel a little better these days.

Kinda.

My BRHT came via mail instead of UPS, so I'm saved!

Jab! Jab! Jab! Gulp! Gulp!

Let's see if I go "Boing!" I have really missed Mr. Johnny Wood. He hung(!) out for just so long and then left the building about ten years ago.

### Martin — 2012-10-19T22:23:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-59227>

I've run out of excuses.

"it's too cold!"

"I'm too busy!"

"I need to rest."

All the reasons why not to get into a cold tub of water have been addressed, and there ain't nothing left.

Diet, check.

Amber glasses after dark, check.

Addiction to oysters, yeah baby.

Bio-identical hormones coursing in viens, done.

I have run from cold for my entire life. Bragged about it. But since reading about the Optimal Human, I see where we are creatures forged from cold. It is our history, our legacy, and our natural state of biochemically being.

I have done so many things to get down to the core of why I don't feel well. It has defined my life in many ways. All of it has been a journey to find my way back to what a Human does to thrive.

It's been six days and I've done three CT sessions. I must grab this "low hanging fruit" and set changes in motion. It is not enough to have all kinds of knowledge and books, webinars, blog posts. Then one has to walk the path.

I've suffered so much low-grade chronic pain in this life, what's a little discomfort from freezing one's hiney off? And it's virtually free, rock solid creds, and the data makes sense when you look from above.

Tough to get into cold water? You betcha. My little thyroid gland almost shot outa the top of my head when I finally dipped in. My adrenals hopped around like a cat on that proverbial hot tin roof. Little Johnny ran for cover, vowing never to come out again.

But I survived and used my changed mind to confront it all.

Back on track, pickin' fruit.

Life's good.

"best" side up!!!!

### Martin — 2012-10-22T16:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-59614>

Got back from mold doc with loads of goodies to tack. The protocol is a tough schedule to figure out, but after a few days it gels.

This is the ninth day of less pain, more energy, and greater focus. Not anywhere near "normal", but when one experiences all this it really makes all the efforts and putting up with skeptics a little more worthwhile. More on that in another forum area.

My son has become interested in all this epi-paleo stuff. He's getting fatter by the day and more tired by the minute. At 37, he's at a tipping point of sorts and is perhaps feeling the need to make some changes. I have been careful not to push my new-found way of life on anyone, but totally open to questions and offering guidance. He may be ready.

Tomorrow is huge in my little mountain-town world. I go before the Appeals Board to present our case for rebuilding the ruined rental house downtown.

Collard plants are getting big! They should be ready in 6 weeks and ready to put up for the winter. This year's garden has been my worst in decades due to my inattention, but this crop will add some success to the season.

### Martin — 2012-10-24T15:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-59965>

I hit a wall yesterday. Mold doc gave me some detox stuff and it may have stirred up some crap. I backed off of it to gain back some ground today. GOt to finish drawings for the contractor bids.

Had dinner with our son last night. I had not heard his details about him going paleo two weeks ago and was thrilled to hear that he'd not drunk beer in that time. He cut out starchy carbs and bread and that was the hardest part for him. All the meat and fat is appealing and he's adding more seafood. Can't use coconut oil due to his baby son's allergy to nuts. Crap.

Here's the big news from him: MORE energy, LESS pain, MORE focus, and he's loving it! I told him to take pictures for later when he's ripped so he can appreciate the changes. He has not weighed himself during these last two weeks and is waiting for a big surprise in another week or two.

When we parted, I threw out that there are a couple of more steps that he can take to really get shredded and boost energy and he laughed and mentioned CT. He knows I'm doing it, but not any details on what it can do.

I give him another week or two and he'll be there.

Then, there is the Light....

Heh, heh.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-24T16:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-59976>

Hi Martin, glad to hear your progress and your son getting on board. You are a starfish! Like Dr. Kruse would say. As far as the die off--being a sensitive person myself to all kinds of things. I find that if I go low and slow. Gradually add things in---very tiny amounts. I want to get better now, but if I push the supplements too hard, I can crash easily then have to back off and start again.

### Martin — 2012-10-24T17:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-59982>

Thank you, freshveggies!!! It will be the greatest thing ever if he can really bust out of modern living and soar. My boy!!!! I could cry!!!

I can usually gulp down anything and not feel any difference, but I was warned that discomfort may come up so I'm taking heed. It really sets me back when I crash down after feeling decent. It's happened so many times! But I've learned to forge on ahead and take the gifts in whatever form they take.

I'm re-reading CT 7 and seeing so much more these days. After re-reading any of Doc's work, one starts to get the relationship of one neuropeptide to a hormone to and neurotransmitter, etc. I couldn't necessarily teach a class, but you get an idea why you put your a$$ in a tub 'o cold water. Smash those cytokines!

I was also wondering if I was feeling a crash because of running out of T. I was due for another injection the day after I crashed. I'm tracking to see any patterns. I'm waiting for the day where we plug in our finger to a little device that gives a real-time readout on all our hormones, etc. Hey, a guy can dream!

### Martin — 2012-10-29T17:58:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-60724>

I picked up my seafood order yesterday. There's a local guy who makes runs to the beach and brings back seafood to finance the trip.

I started on my bushel of oysters as soon as I got home. I've had them three times in two days. Slammin' 'em as much as I can for as long as they last.

Also got five more pounds of skrimps. Man, I'm eatin' good.

Mold doc has me on some cleansing stuff and it's kicking my a$$. Seems like every hour I have to drop something or another down my throat. It's all good. One foot in front of the other.

Scratchy throat and feeling very fatigued in the last few days. I did have about eight days in a row of improvement in symptoms. Very nice to get a peek into where I'm headed.

### shilohman — 2012-10-29T18:18:53+0200

### Martin — 2012-11-06T15:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-62355>

Thanks, shiloman. I get closer every day.

It's been about two months on BRHT and I'm lovin' it. I take T, P, heavy sea salt for aldosterone/renin balancing, vasopressin, and sometimes HC on a rotten day. Add in some DHEA, Preg, and some special herbs my mold doc rx''d me. My body tends to react slowly no matter what I take and all these were no exception.

But there is a shift goin' on.

I've noticed a little more zip in my step, a little more focus, and I've begun to eye girls with bad intent. The mental part of my newly re-minted libido is very interesting in that it is exceeding my "normal" male-body's urge to mount everything in sight. My mind is racing, but my body is lagging a bit. Do I care? _Eff_ no! I'll take anything after the years of pseudo-castration brought on by this biotoxin crap!

Momma doesn't know it yet but I've been lurking around the bath door when she showers but I don't want to commit to something I can't finish with, well, a bang.

Anyway, it's all fun. And fun has been a bit lacking lately. I've also noticed less stiffness in my knees and hips when I go to get firewood or work in the collard patch down in the garden. But here's a biggie: less flu-like ache. All this good livin', circadian watching, fat burnin', hormone balancing, and CTin' has gradually eased my pain.

I have more strength, too. I had to move a desk I was restoring and was almost shocked that I hoisted it up and carried it (it's not a big desk!) to the workshop. I pushed into the work rather than retreating from it like I have for years.

Yesterday morning I passed in front of the dressing mirror and decided to give the ol' bod a look and for a guy who does ZERO exercise (save a desk or two), I look kinda muscular. Not ripped, mind you, more like crumpled or wadded up, but with a little work, who knows?

I bought a whole bushel of oysters and have had a fest ever since. And shrimp! God, the shrimp! I have taken over the cooking since last year and I've really enjoyed creating these wonderful meals. And add to the lineup now is 20 pounds of wild-caught salmon that is as red as a stop sign. We get it from a small company that is out of Alaska and North Carolina. Lot cheaper than the market!

Hark?! Is that the sound of the shower running?

Time to go lurk.

### Martin — 2012-11-12T15:23:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-63802>

Getting my CT water temps down a bit. Not much, but it is definitely enough to shiver occasionally while submerged.

Energy is sorta steady. I have good stretches here and there. I had a decent day yesterday despite running a slight fever of 99.5. I took off my shirt when I went to work in the collard patch yesterday and got some sun. Started to drop- trou and get some leg-sun, but I had to walk thru some brush and didn't want to scar up my magnificent legs.

Not much luck lurking these days, but like E said, it's the effort that is appreciated. It's all good. A guy can dream, can't he? If that goes.... don't look for me here. I will have gone on to the Great Brothel in the Sky.

IM injections are a pain in the a$$ - literally. Okay, that was lame, but it's the truth. Kinda freaky sinking an inch and half needle deep into one's hiney, but after the first time it's a cake walk (for our foreign friends, that's slang for "easy").

"Destroys cytokines". Loves me some quotes like that. This is one of the main reasons I do CT. This is exactly the kind of directed, factual, information that inspires me to do the cold. It's so accessible and cheap! My well water is around 65 degrees so no frickin' runs to get ice. I am working up the tolerance to go swimming in the local river, but need a spotter in case I freeze up like a little white popsicle and float away.

### Martin — 2012-11-17T15:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-64925>

Ugh.

For about two weeks now I've been drifting in and out of some sort of cold or bug and have been going down hill. It comes and goes, but I can tell from how my "core" feels. You know, that inner strength that one can pull from to get thru life. My legs went from carrying me thru the day with their little pumping actions to me dragging around meat sacks. My nausea returned and cooking and eating has lost some allure. Libido is fading, and I have made so many mistakes and forgotten so many important things. Arms ache along with the legs, and some joint stiffness. I find it hard to concentrate on important things going on and have to constantly center myself on what is needed next. Don't ask me to fly your airplane or even fix the engine.

It may be some detoxing going on as the mold doc has me on some stuff that is supposed to do that. Still...

I have not tested this place for mold since cleaning it up and remodeling the inside completely. Too many kids, dogs, to get accurate read, and I have nowhere to go if it is positive, so why test? I'm stuck here so maybe I don't want to know. I may be relapsing into that godforsaken funk that I'd been in forever. I'm going to do another (my last in a series of three I bought) and see what has happened to my visual contrast sensitivity. And maybe a TGF b blood test would reveal what is happening.

T shot today. It will be interesting to test in a month to see where my does have taken me. Progesterone, too. The vasopressin is a sleep saver as I can get thru the night without none trips to pee. I sleep like a rock anyway. Usually 9.5 to 10.5 hours a night and I am zonked thru it all, buddy. Lights OUT. Always have.

Today we hammer out details on the rental project. Tuesday is the bank day. Got to get bids from contractors so we can proceed with the bank.

Time to move my meat sacks.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-17T15:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-64928>

Mornin'. You were doing a lot of CT'ing too for a bit. That along with the mold stuff screams detox. In the Spring when I was heavily into CT'ing, I was detoxing like crazy and for weeks at a crack. Short reprieves in between. Wonder if taking Silymarin would help your liver deal with all of this and not be so hard on you. I take that and DIM (high estrogen issues) right before I get into the tub now. So far, so good. I am not going thru any major detox and have gone cold and hard this week. Course, who knows what next week will bring. Are you still CT'ing even though you are feeling like crap?

I wish you achey free joints and happy days!

### freshveggies — 2012-11-17T16:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-64938>

HI Martin, just wanted to say that it is not always a steady climb out of it. I know what you mean by how the core feels. For me, I will have several days of getting better, then it will go back again for a while, then get better. Kind of wax and wane type. I am glad you are sleeping so well, as that is my biggest issue. Can't get any kind of steady sleep. Lets just hope your house is free of mold. I could not go to my mom's summer lake home at all this last summer because of a low mold count. Do you keep a journal of your foods, supplements and how you are feeling? Sometimes it helps to look back and see where you have come from and how you are doing now and also to track anything that might not be working. You have made so much progress.

### Martin — 2012-11-17T18:04:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-64955>

Hi V, good point on the sillymarin. I ran out about an month ago. Thanks for the heads up. Also, I forgot that CT'ing is breaking down fats and the crap gets released. Bring it on, let's get it out. DIM? Is that Diindolylmethane? Sounds like that methylation issue that I don't have any answers for yet. Money is very tight, but I'd love to throw about 5 grand at testing updates and new stuff like the meth thing. I stoked that there are further pathways to pursue!

veggies! Man are we related or something? Long lost sibs? Food diary - I do kinda keep track of what goes in my grassfed seafood-hole, but there are no red flags that I've been able to detect... yet.

Today is okay so far. My fever left about four days ago and the weird thing is, I felt like a million (comparatively speaking) even though I'd run about 99.5 or more.

I really think I have a lot of power and endurance in my body that's underneath this moldy-ass blanket that fell on me. Low .17 HS CRP, lots of labs in mid range, no weight issues, VAP good, handsome good looks, and what an egghead!

Just shot up my T. Let's see what the rest of today bring.

I love this place. Thanks Doc, and to all here.

### Martin — 2012-11-19T15:38:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-65320>

(Just lost all that I'd written by going back a page to check something. G\*d\*\*mn I hate that.What happened to the auto-saved thingy!!!!!!? I've been rooted!)

Here I go again...

Really bad days for the past few. A flu-like ache and bone-tired lethargy had me on the couch and in my robe all day long. I've been taking detox stuff from my mold doc, but I thought it would be collecting stuff rather than chucking it out into my bloodstream.

It's been coming on for a while now and I wondered if it was because I have stopped cholestyramine and I'm still exposed to mold. Shoemaker says "sicker quicker" so I used my last Visual Contrast Sensitivity test and I passed just like the last three I've taken over the previous three months. Even my doc said I'm at least holding the gains I made over the last six months.

Sure didn't feel like it this past week.

I took the VCS test and checked my temp and I passed both. A big relief as it may be an indication that our house is clean enough to live in. But I can never be sure until I do my second spore test. It is not very practical because of pets and children running in and out and this place is small so laying out large sheets of plastic on the floor for a month to collect dust samples is not going well. Too much risk of contamination and skewing the test. And I am waiting on the ductwork to be cleaned of mold and then perhaps test then. But I still can't move to another house!!! Arrgghh!

What will today bring? I am feeling a bit better but it can creep in as the day goes on. I don't wish this s\*\*t on my worst enemy. Anyway, I'm meeting with contractors today to hammer out details on the rental project and I'm not quite ready yet. Yesterday was the "get ready" day and we see what happened to that.

I'll step up and it will be fine.

### Martin — 2012-11-25T17:30:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-65586>

The kids just left and so did a piece o' my heart. The twin girls are getting big and so much fun! Little brother is a joy to watch as he takes in the world. Boy, do I ever remember that feeling as a child.

My son brought up to the farm his continuing paleo dieting habits and we tag-teamed all week long on what to eat and what NOT to eat. It was fun to have a comrade in your camp. The girls look on, and they see changes, but no one is coming on board... yet. Son looked great and the last time he weighed was the last time he was here and announced that he was going pale. He was 180 and lookin' bloated. What a difference!

He lost ten pounds in two months and man did he look great! Ten pounds never did sound like much to me, but when I see it gone, it is something. He's stopped drinking beer, off bread, sweets, and can feel the difference. I'm working on him stepping up to CT'ing and maybe the amber glasses, so we'll see.

I did another VCS test and can see nearly all the test square patterns which means that my visual acuity is steadily improving. I have not done this well on that test in nearly a year! Now if my flu-like symptoms will just go away... It has been rough. I've learned to push thru it all and have a life, but I must admit, it gets me down. Years, decades of... oh never mind! Whiner!!!!

I may re-test TGFb-1 to see how I am doing while I still live in this home. I still can't move, but I just have to know. I'm running outa money and we still have a rental place to build so the next years will be critical. HRT is still going well and smoothly. I will retest in a few weeks to see what all this T, P, salt, LDN, and vasopressin has done.

I'm so thankful I have a trusted pathway for this! So many years in the wilderness just lost as I could be.

No more!!!

### Martin — 2012-12-10T13:37:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-67011>

My cold has abated. Yay! It was bothersome, but the least fun part was how long it persisted.Â 

I've spent the morning reading Martin's journal from the beginning. Very interesting and I like this guy's style and humor, although he tends to whine a lot. But, not as much as he used to!Â 

I feel decent enough today to add a big smile to my sometimes sour puss. While preparing breakfast,Â I actually bounced up and down with glee as the morning wore on.

Less ache, people. It feels so good I'm drawing a CT bath as I write this.

I feel downright HOT today. Never mind that it's kinda warmish today in north Georgia, I'm going with increased metabolic rate as the reason. I just shoved an old merc thermometer in my mouth to check.

Eight months since starting CSM. Two since stopping it. I am prepping for a retest on the mold count and send it in. If it comes back lousy, it will spur me on even more to get this place ready to sell and move to a clean place. It is still a huge deal to confront, but it won't get done unless I take the steps.Â 

Still no word on the bank's decision on the rental house loan. They have all the effing money thanks to their lobbyists and their whores, aka, our elected officials, and they are not loaning it out without putting one thru the wringer. I'd love to go ahead without them and give 'em the finger, but not in the cards... yet. We have a back-up plan forming. Stay tuned.

Still on IM testosterone. Today I'm feeling like a "man". Speaking of which, one of the side affects of T supps is that one's testicles shrink. Last time I hustled them (which can be quite often) I didn't feel nuthin' down there but a little mublety pouch. _wtf?_

"Are they GONE?!" I shrieked to myself.

Have I turned myself into aÂ eunuch?! I got serious and found them hiding up in there all tucked away, albeit a bit smaller than I seem to remember. I had just run outside in the cold rain in just my skivvies to go get chickie food out in the barn so I hope the little huevos just got a little scared of the cold and ran for cover.Â 

Hmm. A "small" price to pay for other bodily bennies? According to my research... yup. I need to find out the protocol regarding testing when on BHRT so I can see where I am.Â 

I need to change my weight from 136 to 139. Tubbin' out. I've really cut down on my carbos going into the short days and we should see some movement.Â 

My temp is 99.3. WTF is that all about? It raises from time to time and it seems to coincide with feeling better. But usually it's 98.6. There were decades that it would not get above 97 not mater what I did.Â 

CT bath is nearly ready and it's time to put on a podcast and get adapted.Â

### Martin — 2012-12-19T18:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-67983>

Caroline, he's my kinda whacko - goes against Conventional Wisdom with both barrels. I've had some fun talking to some friends lately about what they think is the "truth" in healthcare.Â 

Â 

I've had a mystery to solve.

I've been immersed in methylation research and I am really thinking I'm starting to crack open a mystery that has bugged me for a long time. A special thanks to the ones who pointed me in this direction.

Four years ago this spring, I was doing great. Lots of energy, recovered easily, happy, everyone noticed. I even hauled out my weight bench in anticipation of getting some muscle on. I had built myself up thru diet and mindfulness and it felt great to be alive, work hard in my beloved garden and orchard, and play with the beautiful twin grandkids.Â 

Then I got very ill. I didn't think anything about it. As I lay there with the flu (or whatever the eff it was), I said to myself that I could get past this, no problem. I've been doing well so I should bounce right back.Â 

Three and half years later, that never happened. All my pursuits had turned into chores, and then into burdens. I became anti-social and stayed inside instead of going out onto the beautiful mountain that we chose to retire on and play out our lives. Hills that I walked up and down on my land used to be places to get cardio workouts doing useful work instead became dreaded sheer cliffs to be avoided unless I was on a machine with an engine driving it.Â 

The fatigue and ache became nearly unbearable, and some days I would crack and just scream to the sky asking, begging, pleading, cursing, to anyone or thing that would listen and give me an answer as to why I was suffering.Â 

I heard no answers.Â 

Went to two local docs and helped them make some house payments.Â 

I thought is was Lyme, then mold showed up on nearly all my testing and I went to my mold doc and got treatment. Five months on CSM and other herbs and spices from him helped. My VCS improved and I believe I have cleared the biotoxins. I cleaned up my house, spent a small fortune!

Still, I suffer.

I still pass VCS, but the symptoms are back. CFS, fibro, cognitive, achy, blah blah, blah.Â 

But one thing _always puzzled me. What was that illness I had back then? If I could not be around mold, why did I feel so good before crashing with that illness? I asked my mold docÂ several times,Â but got no real answer.

Before I got so sick, I didn't have great_ energy, but I still had enough to work most of the day. Mentally, I had tons of dreams about growing clean market veggies and being a part of the burgeoning farmer's market in our small town. Two workshops full of materials I'd spent decades collecting materials and machines to build the many projects in my head. A truly useful, beautiful dream being fulfilled.

Today, I could give a flying f\*\*k about any of it. I hate to admit that, but that's the state of my mind for the most part.Â 

Brain damage from a bacteria? Virus? Maybe. My testing shows low pituitary output, but not shut off. Low dopamine, to be sure. Lousy IGF, right on. Something is still hanging on when one has decent days and bad days.Â 

But why have I not recovered with all this clean living, organic eating, fresh air sucking, superlative well water guzzling, and retirement living? Why is all that not enough to get some relief?Â 

I started BHRT and it helped, but not like I need or want. Something deeper I'm not seeing.Â 

Enter Methylation.Â 

Ooh boy.

When I heard in a lecture on methylation that a "severe illness" could knock someone down if their methylation pathways are screwed up, I stood up and started taking notice.

Could it be?Â 

It is starting to make some sense the more I read. I ordered the 23andme test and dove into the research that's out there.Â 

Test kit is due any day now.Â 

Stay tuned, kids...

### Martin — 2013-01-02T10:10:20+0200

Well, I've been gone for a bit.

My faithful hound knocked over my laptop and killed the hard drive.

Then both my DW and I got the flu.

Then the dishwasher broke.

Happy Holidaze!

Still waiting on my 23ndme. In the midst of the flu madness, I felt better the day it showed up and in my rush to get it in the pipeline, I didn't register it before sending it off. F\*\*k. That's what I get for rushing. I've not heard from 23amdme yet, but the protocol is that they will send me an email to register it when they get it. I hope not much time is lost due to my ignorance.

Rental unit project is gone. The bank wants too much collateral, more than we are willing to risk in this sh\*\*ty economy. We are looking around for other options, but meanwhile half our fixed income continues to not pour in.

Looking to do a skype consult with a MEHTYLATION doc in Charlotte after I get some more data compiled. I got my sulfate test strips and have done a few, but need to learn the proper way to test.

### Martin — 2013-01-03T09:42:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-69120>

Thanks to all the thoughts above. Like I've said, this is the place.

Just got some bloodwork back to help with my BHRT strategy. I started im T, Prometium, and here are what the results of being on them for four months:

Testosterone 1196 348-1197 ng/dl Aw hell, I was shooting for 1197!
Testosterone, free N/R Great, the important one and something screwed up. More wasted time and money.
Progesterone 2.4 0.1-0.2 There's something goofy here. Either I wrote it down wrong while on the phone or whatever, I'm going to call back and get confirmation and measurements from the nurse at my doc's.

Vit. D 51.5 This is after five months of supping 20 to 24 thousand UI's a day! That's what it took to get it up from a dismal 30.1.

There are others, but I didn't get the measurement values. More laterâ€¦

Did a sulfate strip test this morn and like yesterday, it was above 1600. Not good, from what I'm learning. Give up collards?! Broccoli?! I loves my brassicas so that really sucks big time. And there's a freezer full of local, hormone-free, grass-fed, wild-caught meats and seafoods just waiting for me to prepare and I can't eat them?! This effing sucks!!!

DW may have contracted pneumonia thru the holidays. More sucking. Our flu bouts were terrible with all the hallucinating and weird dreams at night. And nothing like going to bed at 7 pm, writhing, tossing and turning for what seems like an eternity, waking up thinking it's morning and it's time to get up out that rather uncomfortable bed (no place is when you have the flu) and realizing it's only three hours later! Argh!

New laptop is really nice. Did not have the money to spare, but it's essential for my survival. I live out in the sticks and it connects me to the rest of the world.

### Martin — 2013-01-06T09:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-69570>

Let's see if I can get it right... our environment causes epi-genetic mutations that screw up the way certain molecules are processed and the one's with sulphur are biggies.

Meat and "brassicas" ( examples are cabbage, broccoli, collards, kale, brussels sprout, etc.) have a lot of sulphur in them so one who has the CBS mutation needs to avoid them while undergoing treatment.

And I grew the best and biggest crop of organic collards this year! Phooey! If I have the CBS mutation, that is.

Here's an explanation:

MTHFR is a common genetic variant that causes a key enzyme in the body to function at a lower than normal rate. This can lead to a variety of medical problems, when people with MTHFR are exposed to more toxins than their bodies can handle. There are over 50 known MTHFR variants, but the two prime variants are called 677 and 1298, the numbers refer to their location on the gene. The routine lab test for MTHFR variant only reports on 677T and 1298C as these are the most studied.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T11:53:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-69589>

1298 is the big one and less is known about it........677 is the smaller one and a lot is known about it........the key is know what labs to look for when you know one of the two are out of whack.......then marry that info to the clinical picture and go on a treasure hunt for the optimal goal of health

About CBS: If you have it S compounds are not the only big issue! A high protein diet creates more toxic ammonia through the CBS highway (transsulfuration), and burdens the garbage dump further. This is why Epi-paleo is better than paleo for people with it. Seafood has low sulfur compounds while meat does not. It also simultaneously lowere HS CRP because of the iodine and DHA content! Those taking a great deal of supplements, without regard for their genetic makeup, will only add more ammonia & sulfites to the growing burden. This is why I dont recommend supplement to every like some of the paleo leaders do. In fact I will say that most of the paleo leaders have not a clue about methylation and it is one of the reason paleo can faileo some people...........Truth bomb.
Why do I pound you guys on things gut related? Chronic bacterial issues, particularly anyone with ongoing gastrointestinal problems, as they are more likely to have too much intestinal bacteria, which release even more ammonia & toxic byproducts worsening the CBS person.........Their diet needs to be tailored.......but if you dont test and dont know how can you adapt?

When the system gets overwhelmed with free radicals from poor mitochondrial function two things build up in a CBS person: Super oxides and Peroxynitrates. These are the two chemicals we see in post aneurysm bleeds in the head after a SAH in neurosurgery. It causes a deadly reaction in the brain cause vasospasm. Still today neurosurgery does not understand this link. We still have serious morbidity and mortality because people are not realizing that blood products when broken down can overwhelm the methylation system and lead to these two free radicals destroying normal cells. This is why people with CBS often get a diagnosis of fibromyalgia or Chronic fatigue........when the real answer is an altering your epi genetics to match your genetic match..........Super oxides disable the immune system so the ROS cant be cleared and Peroxynitrates are the serial killer of normal healthy cells........the more they get to the less mitochondria are available......to make ATP and once energy starved what do we call a human? A Cadaver.................where you are on this pathway is somewhere in between. The pathway is a continuum.........when you feel better your methylation is OK.......when you feel like shit you got to adapt. I hope this educational consult this AM helps.......My fingers are tied of typing.

### Martin — 2013-01-21T10:39:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-72017>

Great EMF stuff going on. It's something that has been on the radar for a while and it's good to get some reliable info in front of me. I have been fooled so many times by Woo-woo crap, but felt like magnetism is so huge on our planet that some effects had to be happening.

I'll be calling the power company and asking about whether we have a smart meter. I may get out the sledgehammer after hearing the answer. I've not had a cell phone for close to a decade, but recently got one to assist in the rental project. Since that's gone way, so will the phone.

We bought a magnetic mattress pad about ten years ago and put it on the bed, but did't like the way it hurt my back. Pu it back in the box and up into the barn loft.

After watching the Magnetico guy's vids, I got quietly excited about going up into the barn and dragging it out. It was expensive! $1,200 back then. I hope I can find out the gauss rating, but I distinctly remember big numbers and hundreds of frickin' magnets. I know not to put in right under me, but under the mattress. Hell, yeah. I knew I kept it for some reason. Gonna try it out.

I have noticed a change in my sleep patterns. ALL my life, I've not been a morning person and always stayed up late, even as a young child. Dead tired in the morning and slept in whenever I could ALL my life. I've had occasions where I would get out of bed quickly, but it usually involved a Christmas present. Other than that, ONE morning during some BHRT five years ago, about 5 am, my eyes flew open. As I lay there, I thought briefly about staying in my warm bed, but I felt sooooo good that I got up and got on my tractor to do chores.... and watched the sun come up!!! I loved it! This is the way to be! Didn't last.

Until now.

Since wearing the amber glasses, I've started going to bed earlier and earlier. Usually, I stayed up until 10 pm. But for the past two weeks - 8 pm, lights out. I'm gone. And early in the morning - boink! I'm wide awake.... and it's still dark! I get up, and it's 5 am! I'm good to go! LOVE it! I get to experience a whole new time of the day and have reveled in it. Could it be that this circadian thingy actually changes a person?!

Well, YEAH!

### Martin — 2013-02-28T17:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-78311>

Man, it's been a while since I posted here.

But that's the _good_ news!

I mean to say that I've had an increased level of energy and Ive been taking advantage of it by going out and doing chores around the farm and building things in my wood shop. Lots of duties and projects to catch up on and that means less time in front of the computer, aka, EMF Generator.

I think that ridding myself of wireless phones and internet have helped me calm down, while the detoxing that Ive been having after going on the low-sulphur diet had me hammered, it has toned down to a quiet roar and I'm feeling better.

I've taken to going down to the garden and putting my feets in the soil to ground my electron-challenged self. A little warm water poured onto the cold-ass ground helps the process that would otherwise make Pol Pot wince.

I've got to somehow get a meter and measure the house and workshops to see what I'm dealing with here. No guessing. I've gathered info on schematics to make my own Shumann genny-rator and am ordering the parts to put it together.

I saw Dr Kruse's link to the French No-EMF place and saw some great ideas as to what I can do around the farm here to get back to the earth. Deep trench with copper strip in the bottom, hmmm? I have all the parts, a backhoe to dig, what's not to love about really getting a connection going?

Faraday cages, shielding, meters, all have my inquisitive mind in a comfortable (did I mention I was feeling better?) overdrive place where I have some of my best cognitions. Lots of fun, creative, useful ideas have come from this state of mind. My teachers all called it daydreaming, and so it is.

Back to more of me…

I had my consult with Dr Tim Jackson and it went very smoothly. Lots to cram in an hour, and I thought we'd run out of things to say! Silly me. I was proud to declare my .17 HSCRP, 4:1 omega ratio, and my bone broth history as if to say, "I'm on this shit, Dr. Tim, just give me some more direction in which to fine tune!"

Dr Kruse's interview on MTHFR Support was killer. And I'm not just saying that because they lead off with the question that I posted, no, it's because Dr K didn't pull any punches on what he sees. _Loved_ it.

Missed the spreecast! Like I said, I ain't been in front of the computer as much these days.

Good to be back!

P.S. GQ cancelled the shoot. Said I had to work out more.

### Martin — 2013-03-22T17:27:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-81454>

Good two weeks here on the farm. Lots getting done, compared to say, last year at this time when I didn't give a shit about anything. Ditto the year before that, too.

Over the last month, I've felt a movement towards Optimal as these protocols I've learned here have continued to balance me. Dare I say, less pain? More energy? Hell yes, I am.

A day of "more energy" is okay, but in the past it would go away as fast as it came on. The chronic pain is tolerable, but it doesn't help this old boy and his ways. I'm literally a "hands on" kinda guy and I like to build things, fix things, and eat things that I grow. All that wears my hands and feet out pretty quickly and back I go into the house to recover.

Well, kind forums chums, for the last month I've been working on a lifelong project of creating a workshop for my woodworking. I've had ratty little garages here and there that were never quite right, but this two-car garage sized building is da bomb. I've wanted a place to work since I was five. Every place I've lived didn't have the space that I needed to do it, until I moved here. A beautiful 25 by 25 foot building!

But it never got finished.

Typical for ADD-boy here. Start fifty thingsâ€¦ _finish_? WTF is _that_?!

So! Out to the shop again and diving into my dreams of a place in which to create what is on my head.

I could have not done all this without feeling better. The "better" has been steadily improving for over a month now. The mag mattress pad has been in place for nearly two months, grounding at my computer (right out the window into the wet soil), shutting off certain breakers, more sun, methylation strategies, Vit D, iodine, seafood, grass-fed, and all the straight-up things I've learned here are turning my life around. And there's more to go when I correct the wiring in this shack.

So much fun stuff to learn!!!

### Martin — 2013-03-23T15:25:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-81528>

Thanks, girls! (where are the guys?!) Keep the love comin'!

I kinda hit a wall yesterday, well, more like a curb, and quit working on my "new" workshop around 4 pm. Went inside, had a cuppa green tea, and hit the couch for an hour. My rest felt warm and fuzzy rather than my tradition swirling, aching, death-is-imminent sort of reaction when I stop moving and I'm still. It's one of my best tests to monitor how I'm doing when I'm not focused on a project.

This morning I was thinking about the good past few weeks and here is the difference I'm talking about when I say I'm better. You know when you drop something on the floor, and you think twice about expending the energy and effort to stoop down and do something about it? Your knees ache, your back is just daring you to turn just the wrong way, and your attitude is, "Who cares, just leave it."?

Well, my knees don't ache as much, my back's hanging in there, and my attitude is not even in play as I swoop down and pick up the glob of whatever I dropped. And then I'm on to the next fun, productive, life-affirming action that I have in abundance here on my mountain.

I've been reading an EMF remediation book on wiring and have begun to look at the shitty wiring in this shack.

Rut-row.

"Neutral bus strip bonded in the sub-panel" Wrong.

"3-way wire common traveller run independently" Not good.

"Open neutral on outlet near my leg when I'm at my workbench" Sad.

Got some work to do. But now I am beginning to understand why my health really went south when we moved here. It's a mess, and I'm just the guy to fix it. 30+ years fixing houses will come into play here. I have some good electricians to call upon to help if need be.

I've got to get a gauss meter. No guessing, please. My house, my son's, the two big workshops, and any friends who care to look into what Man has done to his environment. Got to measure all of these.

Now, about that photo shootâ€¦.

### Martin — 2013-03-25T17:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-81699>

Okay, after a good month or so of steady pushing, I kinda burned it out a bit. Not bad, but I took the day off yesterday. I actually picked up my guitar after months of neglect and wailed away. Not bad, either. I'm impressed with myself. All that time off and I can still groove back in.

I've noticed some steady improvements that are hanging in since started all this JK stuff:

**Joint Pain** My left knee is so much better! I do lots of stooping and used to dread it every time I had to get something below table height. Now I swoop! Less hand and foot pain. Holding and walking - two things that I do a lot of, or used to. Both are a lot more pleasant.

**Headaches.** Rare, and they used to be daily in some form or another.

**Focus**. Better, but I can still get distracted and lose my way, but recovery is much quicker.

**Attitude** Oh man, sooooo much better! I was in a funky-ass place two months ago. Life seems do-able now! And room to get better and grow.

**Dumping** Instead of a boulder-ridden massive every 2-3 days, we have launch every morn! Boo-yow!

**Libido** Eh, could be a million times better. I went off T about two months ago (money) until I get thru some of these methylation and EMF remediation protocols. Then, if I want to ramp up the hormones, I'm can get back on them. I miss Little Johnny Wood. He was like my best friend for like forever.

A year ago, I was beginning to think that what I was going through was as good as it gets.

I don't think that any more.

### Martin — 2013-03-25T19:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-81739>

Hack away, V. I'd like to know as well.

Inner child? Funny you should mention that. I remember my childhood very well all the way back to age 2.5. Lots of beautiful times just being in the moment and takng in what was in front of me. As I grew older, I spent too much time in my head thinking about what was coming, and when it was gone, how lousy it often was.

Lately, I've been endeavoring to access certain attitudes and emotions I had in those really wonderful times. I must say that they are coming more frequently since I started to detoxify a bit. Inflamed cytokines can screw with your emotions, at least they have with me. Hormones!

Back ten years ago, I was doing some stretches out on the big porch, and right in the middle of some upward dog/downward dog yoga moves, I passed out and slammed my face on the decking, teeth first. Drove them right into my lower lip. As I came to, I didn't know what happened, but noticed all kinds of blood all over the front of my shirt and my head was pounding. Take-away from this tale of woe was that for a brief few seconds between un and conscious, I didn't feel all the crappy chronic body aches that had slowly ramped up over the years until I thought that it was "normal". I'd forgotten what it was like to feel ache-free! That gave me something to gauge my progress by, albeit a painful one.

And yes, I'm smart enough _not_ to repeat the slam-your-face technique.

Life sounds good Martin - and a strong libido is definitely JK approved! Your DW can't wipe the smile off her face???

### Martin — 2013-03-27T16:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-81960>

I tested my sulfate levels with the CT Scientific test strips and it came up above 1600 again. It seems correlate with the general increased sluggishness I've been feeling. Why? I've been re-introducing sulfur-containing foods this past week and have to back off a bit. Good to have a tool to see where the hell I am! Before, I'd just complain, then when that got old to the people around me, I just suffered in silence. No fun!

I've been on wrist band connected to a ground rod driven into the soil whenever I'm on my computer. I used to have it connected into the ground portion of the outlet, but I kept feeling tingly, stinging sensations so I moved the grounding to the real ground - dirt.

I still get the tingling. It's my MacBook shedding electrons into my body and out into the ground. I had the woman come in and hooked her up to the wrist strap and touch my computer - _ZAP!_ She jumped like a frog on a hotplate. After the screeching stopped, I averted her glare and bought up a link where it shows how to make a body-voltage test set-up. "Look at this, Honey!".

She seems to have a new-found interest in this EMF thing that I've been hinting at for weeks now. Let it be _her_ idea to spend the money on meters! Of course, I want the best, like a NFA-1000 Gigahertz Solutions for only $2,396. Uh, huh.

I have some models in mind, but more research is needed. First is the body voltage meter set-up. Off to Radio Shack for a meter and test leads. Support my local businesses! The local Shack has been very helpful and I'm glad to have on in my little town.

It's looking gorgeous outside and it's time to go do something instead of staring at this computer.

### Martin — 2013-03-28T16:00:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-82050>

Yeah, she knows when she has an easily malleable thing. She's the brains behind this operation when it comes to money.

Yesterday, I was taking some sups and forgot to do my follinic acid and grabbed a tiny capsule of it and proceeded to split it in half. I have to do this because of the dose I had to buy it in to get it from one place to save on shipping (see above for source of inspiration). Pulled the cap apart and dumped one half into my mouth. Hmm? Kinda bitter...

Rut-roh.

I looked at the bottle (with glasses this time, in better light) and it's...

_OXBILE!_

_Argh! Blick! Pa-tooey!_

Well, not one to waste anything, I ran for the sink and gulped down some water. Still, the taste was conjuring up all kinds of gruesome images like a big, lumbering oxen barfing into a...

Never mind. Let's move on.

Sinuses are bothering me so much that my teeth hurt. I actually went to the dentist in an emergency last week thinking I had broken a molar and all it was was inflamed sinuses on that side. Good news, bad news. I get to keep my teeth, but they hurt!

The Woman actually brought up the EMF thingy over a year ago and wondered how the breaker box in between both bedrooms (!) was affecting her sleep, etc. Now's the time to move on it now that we have a much clearer, more accurate path to follow to get it changed. I'm preparing a presentation to show her what I want to get meter-wise and what it will do for us in the way toward removing all the magnetic fields we can.

Now, if she'll give up the cell phone...

### Martin — 2013-04-08T17:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-83359>

I've been testing with the sulfate strips in an effort to get my ammonia down due to CBS snip and it has consistently been above 1,600. Ugh.

I've cut way back on sulfur foods like onions and garlic (whaaah!) and various other condiments and foods that I ate with wild abandon. Eggs are kinda high in sulfur and I cut them out, but not for long after hearing another forum member got into trouble from going too low in sulfur, I added them babies back. I have six free-ranging chickens, for god's sake!

Months of this and....

I tested this morn at 200!!!

Just another brick in this wall o' health I'm building. I love it when you can test your own body and see where it is and not be on a bell curve where what you take and do might or might not help you. Like we all know, we're all different in the details and expression.

I could have _not_ gotten to where I am without testing.

Lost in a swirling whirlwind of strategies, plans, protocols, and suggestions, I would just pick one and do it, most of the time to no avail. It wasn't until getting to this site and learning what labs to go for, did I start to get somewhere. HS CRP, D, IGF-1, and the list goes on.

Time to go do life!

Marty

### Martin — 2013-04-09T23:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-83523>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=83483)
>
> So IGF-1 at the top of range is a good thing?

I found this:

"And to clear the record: I still recommend a range of IGF-1 for optimal replacement of 300-500. The absolute minimum effective level is probably ~225 in males and ~275 in females. With levels over 500, you dramatically increase side-effects, and don't really gain any more advantage. I still recommend daily (or twice daily) injections. The 3 times/week regimen gives you more of a sensation because the levels fluctuate so much. In reality, you have peak IGF-1's above 500, and troughs below 250. There is substantial negative feedback with this regimen and higher levels of somatostatin.

Live and learn, long and well!

James R Hughes, MD"

### Martin — 2013-04-14T18:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-84070>

Kinda slow around here on the farm these days. Pollen is so thick it's like a yellow fog. But I did get out and till and sow my main garden plot in a cover crop. And I did it barefooted! I thought I would have to crawl back up to the house on bloody stumps, but after a few minutes of feets-on-the-ground it started to feel downright wonderful. Off came the shirt... ah photons. Squish my feet into the mud and seeds and compost. My feet are warm! It's okay to get them into the earth!

Okay, kinda dramatic, but I've really come to enjoy bare-footedness and look for more when I can.

Modifying a UBS cable to be a ground wire for my MacBook.

Still cleaning up the shops a bit. But the garden has my attention. Spring is here. I must do things on some sort of timetable if I'm to have a decent crop.

The storms knocked me off the EMF webinar .....for the second time!!! I did get to hear the recorded version, but wanted to be there and ask questions. I'll be at the next one, by gum.

I have read alot about root canal - but that was last year - so don't remember a whole lot except root canals are bad news.

I had an abscess under a root canal - which apparently is very, very common. Mine is complicated by a bridge and no money! I was given antibiotics by my dentist and he sent me to a specialist. The best option is to remove the tooth and clean it all up and then an implant and $10,000.00 + later.....

I haven't had a problem since the antibiotics and my Dentist said he is a fan of - if it isn't broken - don't fix it.

I guess, for you tho - You definitely don't need extra bacteria in your mouth attacking you ..... good luck ...keeping my fingers crossed ....just imagine how great you are going to feel when you get this sorted!

### JanSz — 2013-05-09T16:36:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-85735>

My notes, while perusing your journal.
Way with words, I am jealous.

\========================================

I did 24 hr adrenal saliva and it's low, but always in the bottom of the range. Thyroid is low
Headaches
CSM (cholestyramine)
My T is 420 in a range of about 300 to 800

I took my second Visual Contrast Sensitivity test yesterday.... and passed!

About five years ago, I figured I was stage 4 to 5. (AF, adrenal fatigue.

low aMSH

24 hour saliva tests and they were about the same - start out on the low edge of the scale and then uptick in the evening, but still low-ish.

Shoemaker's book, "Surviving Mold"

IV glutathione

ADH - anti diuretic hormone - low so

I'm taking vasopressin

Two months on cholestyramine so far. No major improvement, but the doc says his patients take 3 to 6 months to start showing improvement.
\-----------------------------
DHEA-S 123.7 ug/dl 38 - 318
Estradiol 20 pg/ml 1 - 47
PSA 1.91 ng/dl 0.00 - 4.00

BG upon rising/fasting and it's been 83, 87, 83, 84

Doc says to limit coffee due to my low-ish iron.

Passed VCS test again.

So another $200 for CSM (Im almost out) and another $150 for mold test.

progesterone =19 in a range of 22-100 pg/ml
HS CRP is now .17. Down from 3.57 from 12/1/11.

testosterone, aldosterone (florinef??), Isocort, Prometrium

Ordered DHEA and lipid pregnenalone from NutriCology. I'm doing the progesterone boost with those because the mold doc is hesitant to prescribe prometrian despite my FULL-ON lab-rat style attitude.

My doc is sending me a RX for T, but much less than I wanted - 75mg a week instead of my consult amount of 300mg a week.

Got my micronized lipid matrix Pregnenelone and micro DHEA yesterday to start building up my tanked progesterone. I am researching Jansz postings on this as well as Dr Kruse's. I really like to see the pathway in my head when I do a particular action. Helps me stick to it

John Roman Romanello 2.0 Rita Cosby

JanSz, here's the best I have along those lipid lines...

GeneSmart test 1/3/2012

Omega 6 to Omega 3 ratio: **4.1 to 1**

HS Omega-3 Index: 7.2% range 2% to 10%

From their test literature: "The HS-Omega-3 Index measures the amount of EPA and DHA omega-3s in red blood cells and is expressed as a percentage of total fatty acids. They go on to recommend increasing EPA and DHA by consuming oily fish or a supplement.

I've got chickens! I got to use 'em! Two a day, and I'm reducing other foods to get it down. If I don't in a week or two, no eggs. Groan.

I may have skewed my testing because of inconsistent times I would do it. Jackson first thing in morning would do. I'm in the middle of rechecking to see where I am. At least it's not practically bleached out like it was when I started! Most of what I ate was sulphur foods.

Are you experiencing any detox effects? I did, I believe. Itching legs, wicked headaches, "propellers" in my peripheral vision.

At this point I am anxious to move on to the next step. Patience... I must have patience.

### Martin — 2013-05-15T14:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-86082>

I've been reading where people here have gone back and re-read blog entries and have been getting around to doing this. I figured that it would be "boring" and "stale" 'cause hey, I've read them dozens of times a while back in my quest to get an understanding about Humans.

Boring it ain't.

With all that I'd read, and all that has come since, it's amazing how you can go back and this rather complex range of information takes on a clearer meaning. Words and concepts that were like another planet's language now have relevance to one another and I see so much more.

And the "gold" in the questions are frickin' priceless! The complex scientific explanations in the blog entry can get overwhelming to the newbie, but when Doc answers the questioner in a more streamlined, concise way in the Q&A , all that science takes on a clearer meaning.

Try it.

### caroline — 2013-05-16T00:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-86136>

Totally agree Matin ... there is so much more gold in the comments sections and like you said ...the science takes on a clearer meaning ..........no doubt about it - I need it clear and simple!
It does seem tho - a year and a bit on .....things are coming together much better - I just can't regurgitate it in any meaningful way to someone else. I have tried writing things out in a simplified form for my grandson and it has helped me tremendously!

### Martin — 2013-08-04T17:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-91492>

Ol' Marty's back writing here today. Lurking away here on the forum all these months just trying to take it all in, process what's the most relevant to him this week, applying it, all while staying off the computer as much as practical and having a life outside in the sun, or in our case this year, clouds and rain.

I ate butternut squash by the ton last winter and decided to grow them because A: easy to grow, B: big harvest, C: they keep all winter, C: nutritious, D: Delish!
Easy to grow them until it rains every damn day and it's cool and cloudy. Then the bacterial blights bombard the plants until there's nothing but black, withered reminders that gardening is tough, even in a good year. It's now spreading to my acorn squash, and I expect it will do my cantaloupes in as they are starting to turn that ugly yellow.

Lots of cleaning out my life still going on. Dusting out my mind from all the programming I received in this life, as well as resting this well-tanned, sinewy, rangy, hunk o' man-flesh body. Catching up on many unfinished projects by moving up in sequence from the oldest one to the newest. Now I figure I'm only nine years behind schedule. Life is good.

"Dang, Marty, where'd all this energy come from?" you ask. "We thought you had about nine tootsies in the grave last we heard." I have to say, I finally found a little semi-consistent CF/FM relief from doing the Mitochondrial RX. It took about three weeks to start seeing a difference. The RX not only cuts the chronic ache a bit, it gives me a little boost in energy to get going on my daily dreams. I do about four scoops a day of ribose, along with 200 Life Ext. CoQ10, 1500 mg L-Carnitine, taurine, and three kinds of magnesium. Lots of water. Sleep is good, but not restful. It's never been restful in this lifetime. At least I don't wake up and stay up. Ugh.

Phase two of the house wiring correction is commencing soon. So far it's been relatively straightforward corrections of some incorrect wiring at a sub panel. I've ditched so many devices that emit EMFs. NO wireless anything.

I'll order lamb from our local meat processor this week to give us another option for dinner. More nutritious goodies from the animal kingdom. Bought a shrimp de-veiner yesterday. Tonight, we feast!

I counted my mattress pad magnets and I have 450! Many more than I thought and I checked each one(!) for polarity and they are all oriented the right way. Yay me!

Glad you are doing so well. Get out there and save those crops!

what a positive description ...well-tanned, sinewy, rangy, hunk of manhood ....can't wait to see this in person one day

So the magnetic pad isn't helping you alot? Does your DW notice an improvement?

I am so glad that "life is good"

I want to say something really risque .... but I got nothin....loosing my edge \[maybe you have been gone too long and I am out of practise???\]

### Martin — 2013-08-08T18:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-91829>

Hi all, Big Giant Head here.

I got a 55 gallon plastic barrel to make a CT tub for down in that garden. I need a change of place while doing it. Cold water from the well @62 degrees for the CTing, then drained in to the collard patch. No waste! I'm going to lay the barrel on it's side and plug the bungholes (!) with draincocks (more !), cut the side open so I can get in and lay down in it. Maybe a small deck around it? I want to say something really risque .... but I got nothin....loosing my edge...

Three months into the Mito RX. Good stuff. Really helps me. All steps to improve my environment are bricks in the wall.

Every day I get something caught up on the farm here. Spending more time outside.

It's hard to say about the mag pad specifically. I know that I'm so glad I didn't sell it years ago. Blessed to have it, and glad it's made correctly.

Thinking of getting TGFb-1 run to see where I am with the mold exposure. My mold doc uses it to monitor patient progress.

Giant Head, signing off.

### Hope — 2013-10-26T22:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-99092>

Hi Martin,

just discovered - again - your journal after reading recently again in Jack's blog on Lyme and Leptin. Got the Mold book, had a consult with Dr Shoemaker.....and after reading your journal, looks like the protocol really really helped you? Is that so? Have you had any follow up testing? I am doing my blood work Monday and Tuesday and can't wait for results. Actually may ask my MD for that CST drug before I get tests back. Failed the VSP test. I think mold has been an underlying issue in me for the past 16 years.....and I always knew it in my gut but never had a way to really check it until now except for a mold company that found nothing. Can't wait to get results from ERMI.

Has your aMSH come up yet? I cannot wait to see those results. I'm so bummed Dr Shoemaker retired but so thankful still that he still does consults...and my MD knows of him and vice versa.

I'm fascinated by your home results too. Places I've lived in in the past I am certain have made me sick; in fact some hotels I used to travel in made me feel exhausted and I got a stuffy nose; I could smell all kinds of stuff. Yuck. Sucks that I am so sensitive.

Anyway...not sure when you will read this but wanted to thank you for your great journal.....it's so inspiring! And interesting that you have mold too despite no water damage; I think that is what is going on in our rental right now. We have so much humidity (I finally got a dehumidifier this summer) I am sure that has affected stuff. ANd I just got a moisture meter too, but not sure how good it is.

Oh and thanks for your HUMOR too. I have laughed out loud and that is not a thing that happens often when on this forum and trying to soak up as much knowledge as possible. Thanks for helping make it fun

Got the Mold book, had a consult with Dr Shoemaker.....and after reading your journal, looks like the protocol really really helped you? Is that so? Have you had any follow up testing? I am doing my blood work Monday and Tuesday and can't wait for results. Actually may ask my MD for that CST drug before I get tests back. Failed the VSP test. I think mold has been an underlying issue in me for the past 16 years.....and I always knew it in my gut but never had a way to really check it until now except for a mold company that found nothing. Can't wait to get results from ERMI.
>
> Has your aMSH come up yet? I cannot wait to see those results. I'm so bummed Dr Shoemaker retired but so thankful still that he still does consults...and my MD knows of him and vice versa.
>
> I'm fascinated by your home results too. Places I've lived in in the past I am certain have made me sick; in fact some hotels I used to travel in made me feel exhausted and I got a stuffy nose; I could smell all kinds of stuff. Yuck. Sucks that I am so sensitive.
>
> Anyway...not sure when you will read this but wanted to thank you for your great journal.....it's so inspiring! And interesting that you have mold too despite no water damage; I think that is what is going on in our rental right now. We have so much humidity (I finally got a dehumidifier this summer) I am sure that has affected stuff. ANd I just got a moisture meter too, but not sure how good it is.
>
> Oh and thanks for your HUMOR too. I have laughed out loud and that is not a thing that happens often when on this forum and trying to soak up as much knowledge as possible. Thanks for helping make it fun

Hope, I did five months of CSM and saw no huge benefit like some people experienced. I've not re-tested my home after the remediation mainly because I'm here to stay. If I'd gotten negative feedback from inspectors along with my own observations about this place being water-damaged, I'd move. It's dry, sealed up, and vacuumed to hell and back. Big and little HEPA filters running a lot. I did several glutathione IV's (I think this is what you mean by "GSH IV ") but no results. I'm a tough nut to crack! WTF!?

I've got to get to a place where I'm innately able to handle the mold and other bad actors in my environment. That's always been my goal in any health endeavors. I believe this site is the place.

aMSH test is kinda expensive and difficult to get locally. I use my symptoms to gauge whether I've improved or not. Pain is a good one. I'm very sensitive to pain, so I figure it's still low. I want to continue my CT and LR, then test when things really improve to the point where I just can't stand it - I gotta test it!

Some illness really hammered me five years ago. I fell off a metabolic cliff after that illness event. I want to use what I've learned to reconstruct what happened then. I am just _curious_ about how things break and falter, always have been. So I'm studying these blogs for a pathway that I can understand what happened and what to do next. I get closer every day.

I had a crazy thought yesterday _(excited cannabinoid system induced?)_ about learning so much about Chronic Fatigue and Fibromyalgia here that I'd do a guest blog post on them. Sorta like Inger and Gretchen did. Smart, articulate women, those two. And some others here - wow! - infinitely more qualified to do one than I.

I love to learn stuff and how things work. And, I love to tell people about it. Part of that is an egoic, "Look at how smart I am!", and nothing wrong with that (healthy ego!) but mostly I want to share things that maybe, just _maybe_ might favorably impact someone's life. Trouble is, I have so much trouble stringing things together in some coherent fashion that it just feels overwhelming. The Kraken, I ain't.

So, I stick to reading and listening, hoping to find clues that give me a direction to go in. We don't need to know how the ship works as long as we can sail to our destination, right?

Thank you Hope, for your very kind words about my journal. Traditionally, writing has not been my strongest talent, but I've been inspired by those who are good at it to hone my thoughts down into something that doesn't bore somebody to tears. Adding humor to everything is just what I do these years. Humor, for me, has been elusive, but like anything it can be cultivated enough to maybe get a chuckle or two outa somebody. My litmus test is, if I laugh at what I just wrote - print it!

Big Giant Egoic Head

embs2001, sorry I missed this earlier, but sounds like you got a good answer. I checked with a friend about this and he said the same thing as Julie5 did.

Big and little HEPA filters running a lot. I did several glutathione IV's (I think this is what you mean by "GSH IV ") but no results. I'm a tough nut to crack! WTF!?

I want to continue my CT and LR, then test when things really improve to the point where I just can't stand it - I gotta test it!
>
> Some illness really hammered me five years ago. I fell off a metabolic cliff after that illness event. I want to use what I've learned to reconstruct what happened then. I am just _curious_ about how things break and falter, always have been. So I'm studying these blogs for a pathway that I can understand what happened and what to do next. I get closer every day.

hi Martin,

thanks so much, truly! So you didn't feel much difference from CSM. Hm. I was hoping you did. Do you know anyone who has? I am going to look for FVeggies' posts. Did you read the Shoemaker book? I know the testing is so expensive. It stinks, but I am so convinced my aMSH is low...I hope they do the testing correctly, ugh.

I feel tired tonight but had to respond as soon as I saw this from you: that u are thinking of writing a guest blog. I hope you do!! I would love that, indeed. Why? I was wishing that, reading your journal and a lot of your comments to some of Jack's blogs.....I keep thinking, "Oh, I wish he'd talk more about this. And that. And what he did. And how he is now." No kidding.

Do you feel better? From your journal it seems like you do, but it's more/something other than CSM? Perhaps it's hard to specify one magic thing since that's not it (though Dr Shoe talks about it like that in his book, and I've learned that is their n=1)

I wonder too...did you ever do the low-amylose diet? I'm thinking about it....though I am already eating so strictly, not sure cutting out beets will do that much...but we'll see. I finally got the sulphur sticks....want to test for that issue also.

Anyway thank you SO much. And I hear you on the event 5 years ago. Something similar happened to me too. I think the body tries to stay afloat for so long and if it can't anymore, bammmm....oh well.

Thanks for all your inspiration....I so wish - still - we could meet in person, all of us....some day....

PLEASE consider writing that blog.....guest or your own on your own site  I think even Jack in the comments said that you are 'the man' on mold around here...

Hope, I remember my mold doc saying how his patients are a mixed bag when it came to CSM. Figures. All N=1's are different, like you mentioned! This is a great community and I often wonder about many members' journey.

No, I didn't do the low amylase diet. Kinda forgot about it and need to brush up on it. I read Shoe's book, "Mold Warriors" and learned some things, but my default venue is here at Optimal.

I have been keeping tabs on my sulfate levels with the test strips. Doing well there and have been able to incorporate some sulfur-containing veggies back into my mouth. Broccoli! My collard patch this fall looks like some freak-a-zoid, mutant garden due to years of soil amending. Lots of well water on them often has really gotten them huge!

Yes, I do feel better, compared to the first couple of years after the Illness Event. I've got a ways to go, of course. It's been a huge challenge to turn this vessel around on a different course. I've made gains that have persisted, and that's a win!

I'm re-reading the Quilt after not looking at it for quite some time. I see big words - and I actually have a clue what they mean.

BGH

### Martin — 2013-11-07T00:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100019>

Vague symptoms that defy explanation.

Tired all the time.

Chronic low-grade pain.

Unmotivated, lethargic, and just plain depressed.

And I thought that getting retired and moving to the mountains would allow me to heal and prosper. I've been here for nearly 14 years and have had a really tough time of it all.

Is it nutrition? Since we moved here, we've done so much to improve even our already tight organic diet from back in Atlanta. I've done numerous protocols and tried various techniques. Sometimes I'd get a bump up in health, but this coconut is hard to crack (I'll let you come up with your own "big giant head" quip here). This farm is old and needed lots of work, so I did some myself and hired out some. But mostly I left the infrastructure in place to save time and money. The outlets worked, right? No need to change anything, I surmised. And quite frankly, everything was just getting too big for me to confront doing a total upgrade.

I have a house and three out buildings. I knew they needed updating and corrections even before I learned about EMF's. Now that I've gotten a good set of meters and some reliable info, I've set about finding what's going on regarding my wiring.

_Good god almighty._

I nearly dropped my gaussmeter when I hovered it around the woodshop's panel. **80 gauss!!!** and when I check each individual machine, I discovered that the old and new wiring power feeds are crossed up somewhere. And that created a magnetic field next to the wire feeding one particular machine that is over **300 gauss!!!** Granted, that was only two inches from the wire's surface, and it would drop down to about **6 gauss** where I would stand and operate this particular sander. Still, six gauss is enough to fry anybody's balls into little cinders.

Sigh. Got some work to do. Still cheaper than moving.

I NEVER suspected how electricity could debilitate your ass until I followed Doc here on the path from low-carb to bacon to oysters to Quantum Biology. I woudn't have believed this EMF stuff if you'd had come at me with it two years ago. It had to be _FOOD!_ It had to be _EXERCISE!_ How can something I can't _see,_ hurt my big head?!

I've got to continue to get this place right. The tub where I currently CT is over 3.5 volts when I measure with my Body Voltage Meter. Who the f\*\*k _knew!_ ARGGH!!!

My bed is decent. Magnetic field is **0.00**, even with all breakers on, and even the electrical field is low at **0.20**. When I turn off the breakers - **0.04** volts. But other parts of the house are over **3.0 and 4.0** volts, so it pays to test with a good instrument.

Big Giant Kentucky Fried Coconut

### caroline — 2013-11-07T01:25:33+0200
