Thanks for the update Martin ......... Is C/T helping? I say ..... more hugging etc. etc. etc.

Has my cabin been upgraded????

How are the power lines overhead? Have you read Dr. Merino's book?

### Martin — 2013-11-07T21:36:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100120>

Hi Caroline! Yes, CT helps with my fibromyalgia a lot for a few hours. Raises aMSH, methinks, among many other things. But I feel that I have to do something about the high electrical field in the bathroom before I do it there much anymore. The electrical wiring my main focus these days.

Your cabin is ready anytime you are. No need to call ahead, I'm at your beck and call.

Yes, the power lines are overhead, but the magnetic fields are low. I checked the electrical fields and they were not high, but can't remember the result. I need to record all my EMF measurements on my floor plan drawing.

I would love to read the book! Sneaking up on it. Lots of stuff to do before this choirboy gets more preaching.

Caroline's Fav Big Head Guy

### caroline — 2013-11-07T22:59:34+0200

Martin - you are my very fav Big Head Guy ..... absolutely no question - but then again - I don't know many people with that size head! I feel very lucky indeed ....

The only problem being .... you would be hiding that delectable body.......

### Martin — 2013-11-11T01:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100362>

I have just re-read parts of EMF-5 and I cannot stop pondering the fate of Humans. _What hath we wrought?!_

Going back to older blog posts is like having a thousand IQ point rise. I am beginning to see the _enormity_ of what's going on, and I just get so sad and the tears start.

I know what to do - just keep on improving, moving, seeking, sharing, trying, and find some people to love and share the last leg of my trip in this life.

At least I won't die wondering wtf happened. _That_ would be hell!

Big Sad Head, asking, "Where is my Caroline? She always makes it better"

### caroline — 2013-11-11T02:20:02+0200

I am still here Martin - I am sticking like glue ......like you said - we are on the back end of our journey and, at least, we will know what happened.

JK has showed us the writing on the wall and we can't look away..... not pretty...I am glad that I have your pretty picture to look at

in Jack's last podcast he said several times that he is an older guy ..... So, Martin, he is our people!! everyone is pretty darn young in Paleo land.

I am so glad that your IQ is rising .... I feel like I am drowning lately - even more so. I think Jack's blogs are getting much more technical .... and I fully understand that they have to be. I just hate feeling so stupid!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100457>

>
> BGH

Hi Martin,

wow. I have looked more into the amylose diet and in fact we are doing a lot of it already with EP/L Rx. The only other difference is, he says no beets and carrots bc of the high glycemic index. Trouble is I have some beets left that are amazing.

I am totally overwhelmed now; we do have mold in the place and I am STILL trying to convince my DH to move. So far no luck. Will hire an expert to review our house and see what he says. I got a HEPA filter but not sure if it's enough. I know we need remediating. Did you do any cleaning yourself? I was doing a LOT of decluttering this summer, mostly in the basement - where I think our issues start, besides the bathroom....I actually think the HVAC is an issue though it's new and we had it maintained etc (means nothing I know). The HERTSMI-2 I did show (if I calculated right) we have a score of 16, not good. But no surprise. So between August 20 and mid-October my health steadily worsened it seems plus I was definitely exposed in an apt I stayed at for three nights. I thought all that extra time spent decluttering was stressing me out - so much work - but now I think it's the mold. I felt SO good and strong this summer, would walk 20,000 steps some days or even a whole hour. Now, I am just exhausted.

My local MD tested me in his special physical testing and said I do have mold too, but no lyme's now. My labs for Dr Shoe - suck. MMP high, TGF beta 1 high....MSH 11. UGH. But, I'm totally confused about leptin - normal is 4.7 to 20something I think, and I am at 4.8. Whoa! I expected it to be way high. I wonder if it means my body is totally shutting down something? I don't know. My TSH has gotten worse too....though my T3 and 4 free got better.

Anyway - my question if you don't mind, what filter do you use? Are you taking anything to support your ASI? Mine is worse there too....something is clearly affecting me.

I SO wanna move. We 'only' rent but DH likes it here, and keeps telling me I picked the place. Well they were running the AC at 58 degrees, and I did NOT see the major power lines behind the large trees nor knew all the wifi was here. But we'll see. I am cleaning like a master - oh - what did you use for that? I can't handle a lot of chemicals. I will try essential oils....have to get a lot of it but I love Thieves. When I diffuse it I don't smell mold.

My MD said to take melatonin, which I am not a fan of. This past week I've woken up way early. so frustrating. Must keep on keeping on.

Anyway. I see here your post from 11/6 - SO hope you will feel better ASAP. ARe you getting enough daytime light? My light box is helping some. I am seriously considering that red nasal light for the brain. I need major help with that for sure.

Oh and I met a Klinghardt patient at my chiro's, she goes over twice a year....she lives in the basement of a church with her husband. Left her home and belongings bc of mold. Wow.

ANyway, thanks so much for sharing all you do....so appreciate it!

### Inger — 2013-11-12T08:50:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100487>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=100362)
>
> I have just re-read parts of EMF-5 and I cannot stop pondering the fate of Humans. _What hath we wrought?!_

>
> At least I won't die wondering wtf happened. _That_ would be hell!
>
>
>
>
>
> Big Sad Head, asking, "Where is my Caroline? She always makes it better"

This touched me Martin. I so feel with you... exactly how I often feel like too... The good thing is, there are always people to love around us... actually people cry for it. When we start to see... it is pretty heart breaking. I feel sad too and sometimes I cry. Because of it. Because I see how they suffer. But it always passes and then the sun comes out of the sky  and the moon and the stars.. I often stay in the yard watching the lambs eat.. then look up in the sky and I kind of get lost in it. often when it starts to get dark/the sun sets. That is an amazing feeling! Maybe do that when times get rough

@ Hope,
I am so sorry you have a mold issue. I bet your hubs will move with you soon! But until then, here some things you could do, what I have found helpful. Because here is a mold issue too most probably (some in my family get symptoms)

I air out as much as possible = keep the window in your bedroom at least fully open all the time. The more fresh air you get in the less mold you will have in the air. I have all doors and windows open in summer! But then I am much outside too.. so winter is the real issue. But airing excessively will help a lot.

Then, keep lots of green plants. They clear the air from toxins too. And they are pretty . Some are better than other in clearing mold toxins, you can research about it if you like.

Then, CT helps I am 100% sure. At least to dip in the river (earthing + CT, double good effect?). Because my moms bad asthma healed after she started to take the dips every morning. I am pretty sure her asthma was because of mold. I am not 100% sure, but that is my guess. Also my other siblings that lived here that got symptoms did not dip in the river at all. And also not keeping windows open like I do.

No sugars! I am crazy sensitive to them. I bet it is the mold issue? I cannot even eat apples from our garden in summer! I get candida between my toes. Not bad but it will itch in the night. almost no carbs will agree with me. The very safe carbs so far for me are wild berries.. and maybe cucumber and then for sure wild greens.. anything wild. Wild foods never bothers me in any way. Do not ask why but they are magic..lol So I need to be very strict with my foods. My toes will tell if I have not been that..  But I can eat dirty carrots from the garden and other small amounts of veggies usually... like some raw red pepper here and there. Fruits are the dangerous stuff.

Maybe try to stay as much outside as you can? The thought of being locked in a mold house can help your will power... Look, you will get many other benefits too, daylight, fresh are, exercise...

after a while your hubby will realize you take great pain with the issue and his heart will melt.......

Not bad but it will itch in the night. almost no carbs will agree with me. The very safe carbs so far for me are wild berries.. and maybe cucumber and then for sure wild greens.. anything wild. Wild foods never bothers me in any way. Do not ask why but they are magic..lol So I need to be very strict with my foods. My toes will tell if I have not been that..  But I can eat dirty carrots from the garden and other small amounts of veggies usually... like some raw red pepper here and there. Fruits are the dangerous stuff.

Thanks, Inger, I have been meaning to write you on your journal. you are 100% correct, with the food, I am the same way. I let some tiny amount of fruit slip into my diet for a couple days and bam, no good. So appreciate your input, and encouragement. It helps me stay motivated to stay strict, it's just not worth it at all!! I am having a hard time convincing DH to move; it's been over two years now. I am certain he is sick too, but denies it. But I know he should have more energy for his age. He is too tired to recognize how tired he is. UGH. We need to move asap but also finding the right place with less mold and less wifi around - my MD said the building codes here guarantee mold almost, bc air stays trapped inside. Thankfully, bc growing up we always had the window open, I am used to that. There is some kind of mold source in the house though. I definitely cannot stand the windows not being open. When I am gone - and I now mostly stay with a friend's house in the country (no wifi!) a couple days during the week...and when I come home, the air inside is always worse....bc DH never opens the window. He is very skinny and gets cold easily too. I keep telling him to wear more clothes but when I'm not here, and blasts the heat, another source for mold.

But I'm not giving up. Your mom - I am certain you are correct with asthma. a quarter of asthma cases are bc of mold is what I read. It just makes sense. it is a huge issue and most MD's don't know about it.

Anyway. so glad you are doing well. I do try to walk, an hour each day, morning and lunchtime. It is so good. Best in the woods. GREAT BIG hug to you, my dear ~

### sooperb — 2013-11-12T13:17:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100496>

My daughter has asthma and she ended up living in house that unknown to her was full of mould, she was always sick, the girls were always sick. When she moved out, she found mould all over the wall and under one of her girl's beds and the carpet too. A cupboard in her room always smelled awful, dank and mouldy. Since she moved her health has been better but there are issues with mould in her new place too. She lives in Scotland, plenty of rain lol and she has a leak that they can't seem to fix. She was amazed when I pointed her at Martin's journal, she said it was like a Eureka moment

For cleaning I avoid any chemicals and use bicarbonate of soda, it's cheap, it works very well on almost everything. To make it a little stronger you can make a paste using lemon juice and apply and leave it, fetches off limescale and toilet stains and stubborn grease off cooker tops.

> I am totally overwhelmed now; we do have mold in the place and I am STILL trying to convince my DH to move. So far no luck. Will hire an expert to review our house and see what he says. I got a HEPA filter but not sure if it's enough. I know we need remediating. Did you do any cleaning yourself? I was doing a LOT of decluttering this summer, mostly in the basement - where I think our issues start, besides the bathroom....I actually think the HVAC is an issue though it's new and we had it maintained etc (means nothing I know). The HERTSMI-2 I did show (if I calculated right) we have a score of 16, not good. But no surprise. So between August 20 and mid-October my health steadily worsened it seems plus I was definitely exposed in an apt I stayed at for three nights. I thought all that extra time spent decluttering was stressing me out - so much work - but now I think it's the mold. I felt SO good and strong this summer, would walk 20,000 steps some days or even a whole hour. Now, I am just exhausted.
>
> My local MD tested me in his special physical testing and said I do have mold too, but no lyme's now. My labs for Dr Shoe - suck. MMP high, TGF beta 1 high....MSH 11. UGH. But, I'm totally confused about leptin - normal is 4.7 to 20something I think, and I am at 4.8. Whoa! I expected it to be way high. I wonder if it means my body is totally shutting down something? I don't know. My TSH has gotten worse too....though my T3 and 4 free got better.
>
> Anyway - my question if you don't mind, what filter do you use? Are you taking anything to support your ASI? Mine is worse there too....something is clearly affecting me.
>
> I SO wanna move. We 'only' rent but DH likes it here, and keeps telling me I picked the place. Well they were running the AC at 58 degrees, and I did NOT see the major power lines behind the large trees nor knew all the wifi was here. But we'll see. I am cleaning like a master - oh - what did you use for that? I can't handle a lot of chemicals. I will try essential oils....have to get a lot of it but I love Thieves. When I diffuse it I don't smell mold.
>
> My MD said to take melatonin, which I am not a fan of. This past week I've woken up way early. so frustrating. Must keep on keeping on.
>
> Anyway. I see here your post from 11/6 - SO hope you will feel better ASAP. ARe you getting enough daytime light? My light box is helping some. I am seriously considering that red nasal light for the brain. I need major help with that for sure.
>
> Oh and I met a Klinghardt patient at my chiro's, she goes over twice a year....she lives in the basement of a church with her husband. Left her home and belongings bc of mold. Wow.
>
> ANyway, thanks so much for sharing all you do....so appreciate it!

Hi Hope, sorry about the mold in your space. Ugh. If you hire a mold person to check out your space, I would try and get a reference from either Dr Shoemaker(good luck, he's got a rep for not very responsive) or from Mycometrics. There's a lot of "experts" out there so it's tough to find a reputable person.

Did a ton of research and got a Blueair [https://www.google.com/#q=blueair](https://www.google.com/#q=blueair) and like it a lot. My mold doc has the same brand in his office! Pricey, but well made and quiet on the lower settings. Top rated.

My HERTSMI-2 was **44**, so your 16 is much better. **Desired: below 11**. I haven't retested.

My TGF-b1 was **10820**, yours?
MMP-9 **263** range <984
Leptin? My mold doc didn't check. He likes to look at TGF-b1 as a re-test marker for improvement. I have not retested yet. I've been putting my money into EMF remediation.

I ran a huge HEPA filter while the remodeling went on, and cleaned ALL furniture by vac-ing and using thieves oil . I also use the diffuser with thieve's oil in it - Dr Jackson rec'd it as well.

ASI - I've done HC a coupe of times and it helped more than anything I've ever done. But it doesn't last. Trying to guess what the body needs on a hourly basis is tough and I would ween off when I got weirded out by it. Steroids! But when you're in that sweet spot, it really gave me a glimpse of how I used to be many decades ago.

Stay tuned Hope. I'm measuring my RF signals now that my meter is finally here. I rented one from Magnetics Sciences - $48 for two weeks, reads up to 8 GHZ.

Martin

### Martin — 2013-11-14T22:28:54+0200

**@ Inger**, thanks for the wonderful words of encouragement. I remember how I used to get lost in the sky as a child. I call upon that as much as I can. There's solace in Nature. If we take the time to look.

**@sooperb**, this made my week! I try and write about things that can offer up a clue into a pathway that I've learned about. We learn from one another all the time! Like baking soda!

Martin

### Martin — 2013-11-14T22:48:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-100733>

I rented a RF detector - Acoustimeter - and was delighted to see a VERY low reading while standing on the south-facing porch where I can see through the mountains for 20 miles or so. I figured that this was the most logical direction for a test and sure enough. Out there, it barely registered on the meter, and would go to 0.0 when I went inside. The meter has sound and lights to let you know the levels. Green is good, yellow not so, and red is bashing your brain cells. I love measuring stuff!!!

I powered my little trace-phone and _wham!_ Up to the top red range, and the speaker screamed out, _"Warning: your mitochondria are in grave danger."_

Swear to god.

When DW gets home with her new Razor _(her old phone got left on the hood and fell off in a parking lot and run over so she bought her friend's old one - less than a year old - $100!)_, I'm going to show her some cold hard measurements. I'm offering my friends a test while I have the thing, and then send it to our son in Atlanta to measure his home. When that gets measured, it's going to freak his baby momma out.

When you can measure better, you do better.

Giant Informed Head

Hi Martin,

thanks so much for sharing all that. So glad you like the Blueair. So our Hertsmi2 is 20, I calculated wrong  but oh well. we had a guy here today recommended by Dr Shoe (i had a consult with him) and he found mold in the HVAC for sure, we could see microbial stuff in the coils and then he had a probe that we could see worse crap further down. THEN we learned having an open sump pump = very bad. We have it right next to the drier = also very bad bc it's not sealed off right, so helps to create more humidity = mold. Yuck. And the bathtub was remodeled wrong, the guy thinks that the modeled over mold issues. YUCK. And we learned a non-functioning bathroom fan is a code violation. Landlord told us to just take the fan to Home Depot. That really bothers me.

Guy said to leave is best, but we can stay and remediate. Did you take out all your stuff to have it cleaned or clean it yourself? I am looking at getting rid of a lot more stuff, especially books - I have many still.

it stinks but it also answers a lot of questions. like why I would feel so yucky after a lot of basement decluttering this summer with newfound energy. Ha! How can i be so very stupid?

This man was very good; he also suggested DH is likely affected. I measured his wingspan and he meets that criteria Dr Shoe mentioned in the book. Plus he is always out of it to me, and doesn't notice.

We are renting so we can leave...but finding a place is hard. But I am putting that intention out there. there are too many reasons NOT to stay here. Not just EMF, but mold. The bathroom should be redone, and I am certain the landlord won't go for it.

When you remediated, did you get rid of a lot of stuff, like books, or did you clean them all?

I am praying DH will do the HLA test. Must find out where to do it on our own. Can you tell me how you did it? I don't wanna go through an MD - bc DH won't take the time to go see him and ask for it. So sad.

My MMP says it should be 86 but is 454, and my TGFb1 is 9960 and should be in the 2300s. So yucko.

Here's the fascinating thing -- this guy looked a lot like me. He was 48, reddish hair (and NOT a single strand of gray in it either, like me (though I'm 40), and I don't think he colors it LOL), fair complexion (except that he looks healthy and has a lot of energy! He knows a lot about supplements and works out, HIIT). It's like we shared some kind of blood line. Of course we don't. But, he knows about the HLA and we share it. Wow. The same sucky ones - that one I figured out OK. I just thought it was amazing. What's your HLA BTW? I was so encouraged talking to him. There is hope

This genetic stuff = wow. Though I am now seriously considering totally giving up having my own baby. It would be guaranteed at least one set of sucky genes, if not two.

Dr Shoe said to do a month or so of CSM. My EDS practitioner gave me homeopathic stuff to help with the mold. I also learned today from her, I have Roundup toxicity. Which I didn't know was Agent Orange. Yucky! A Klinghardt patient I met said she had it too per Dr K so I asked my lady to test me, and sure enough, bam.

The mold guy said some fascinating stuff -- like VIP, which mine is low normal, is raised by cocoa, and B12. If I got it right, must read notes. I wonder if the cocoa is really helping me with that.

The guy lives in the DC area and you know what? He is a contractor and rebuilt his condo. Wow.

Thanks for sharing about the steroids; I'm not planning to take them again, just the adrenal supplement support. Something IS working; Dr Shoe said some of my labs are pretty good; not just the leptin, but some other set of immunity. So he said some of my labs are super scary, some are in great shape. So that is good.

Anyway I am rambling. I am so so tired. But thanks for sharing. And how cool about your measurements. That is so great.

Mold guy said no air filters are really great. He had a video he did that shows they don't take out smoke even. It made me think. I've thought of the blue air but also read about the IQAir but so expensive, have to save for it and wouldn't get it now, while in the mold bath. We do have a couple cheapie but pretty well-rated HEPA filters, and I feel like they help some. Mold guy said, run it near the bed so you breathe good air while you sleep but I wouldn't, EMF stuff. Just keep one there in the daytime.

So now we're waiting to see what our LL will say. And I'm immediately looking to rent another place. Wish Christi had another house on her property

Thanks so much for your inspiration

### Martin — 2014-02-14T17:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-110202>

A few months ago I got a call from one of my brothers asking for some help in moving his business to another city. We are close, he and I, a little band of brothers that played, fought, dreamed, schemed, and share a love of building things from the raw materials in this world. We both loved cars, but I burned out and chose another path to take in this life. I restored old houses and built wooden things, he went on to restore cars and build custom high-performance hot rods. He constantly acquired machines and tools that furthered his passion for creating things from metal, while I did the same with wood.

So when he asked for help, I knew what I had to do - get the move done. He's in a large city - the city where we grew up as kids - so I knew I would be heading into a caldron of non-native EMF. As I drove up I-85, I noticed more and more cell towers had sprouted up since plying that highway all these years when I would go see my family. When I pulled into his warehouse parking lot, I noticed a huge cell tower about fifty yards from his building. Crap. It's interesting when you become aware of what technology does to humans and how you used to think those big antenna-studded beasties were benign. I pulled up my shirt collar and turned my back on the thing trying to imagine that would somehow ward off cooking my mitochondria. Uh, huh.

As we loaded up the trucks and headed south to a smaller town, I imagined a more rural life for my brother in the "run to the end", as he calls our final years on this planet. We drove into a nice clean industrial area and lo! A f\*\*king cell tower a hundred yards from his new digs. We just looked at each other, and nodded an unspoken recognition that without lots of money, we have to do the best we can and not stress out over it. I took some solace, misplaced I'm sure, that he was so close to the damn tower that the majority of the radiation was being sent out over his head. I seemed to remember reading a reference about that, and I'm going with it.

It was great fun for me, despite the radiation. We work well together and I got to contribute to his survival. I felt like I was on a vacation, weird, I know, but that's me.

I spent a week in this environment. I knew what I was exposing myself to, but sometimes we have to charge that proverbial machine gun nest when it comes to family. He would do the same for me.

I got home, upped my CT, drank rivers of my sweet well water, and got back to my life here on this mountain.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-15T23:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-110482>

Martin.......you mentioned EMF 5. I think that blog "scared"many people aware.......because they saw something between my words they were not ready to hear. It was not food. It was not lifestyle. It was something they had little control over.

Well here is a cipher to consider: As civilization has grown we gave up chanting, meditation, and "monkdom" to use our time creatively. The more creative we became........we lost sleep. We stayed up late creating. The Renaissance is where our modern ills began. When you begin losing sleep it was because we were dreaming about the things that we could do better and become. We wondered could we beat mother nature at her own game. When humans creation begins to really take hold the its pace quickens exponentially, just like epigenetics did in mammalian evolution. Here we see the congruence of mathematic power laws that support fractal geometry.........but then we feel something change. When we outpace the speed of our design (circadian biology) We begin to have "a sense" that something that seemed "so right" on the surface could be a big impediment. What brought the "sense" on? Using creation to build things to allows us to easily do the wrong thing consistently chronically. When it becomes in grained in our culture, no one seems to see where it came from. And they are less willing to examine why they have strayed from their blueprint. But the irony we begin to feel something is so wrong while we are doing the "right thing" by our cultural and social standards. We try to lie to ourselves.......but in the end our neolithic diseases show us we could not lie to ourselves forever because it finally dawns on us that everything that kills us faster also happens to makes us feel alive. That is the ultimate irony and counter-intuitiveness of QED and mother nature. In this way, social and technologic creation have brought us to the brink of our extinction while we all have been entertained by the “man made asteroid” coming at us from the electromagnetic forces we have harnessed with our creative abilities.

I dont fear this.........I rather like its challenge. It requires good thinking to solve. Some are up to it........while others cower and fold their tents. Stress is not something to run from. Our response to it is what defines us .

I am good at holding toxins though." This defines a poor redox potential. And maybe now you see why I said some of the things I did to you.........why I suggested you not do some things your heart was driving you to do..............The environment is the key......not your food.

### Martin — 2014-04-16T18:30:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-121892>

The Jeremy/tensegrity blog post was reeling through my mind as I headed out a couple of weeks ago to go help my brother move his hot rod shop. We moved literally thousands of pounds of his world - tools, parts, cars, supercharger manifolds - and I wondered how I'd do with the huge increase in pushing my body to it's limit every day trying to beat a deadline. How was my body going to do with full-bore activity and long hours of picking up heavy objects and moving them about? This was going to be a good n=1 on the state of redox in me.

Well, I did just fine.

NO shutdown. NO agonizing fatigue. Tired at the end of the day, for sure, but the next day we'd get up and do it again. And after I got home, I wasn't a basket case. Instead, I'd felt what I read on the Jeremy blog - the quantum nature of water and collagen! Who knew?!

Here's my brother with the greatest car in the world - his hemi-powered '32 Ford that he built.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I have trouble connecting dots with the complex science, and in the past, I've have thrown in the towel when it came to difficult subjects. But I have found the Rosetta Stone for my health here and I knew I had to tough it out and learn this science and the innovations being put forth. It's been said," The way out is the way through."

I've been feeling a bit less fibro and fatigue in the past months and have cautiously taken on more and more around this beautiful place. After a few years of doing next to nothing, I've decided to have a big garden again. On so many levels, I KNOW that gardening is going to help me. If it ain't the grounding, the photons, the fresh air and birdies singing, it's the fact that I'm planting seeds, growing local, picking up tools, moving dirt, pulling weeds, building structures, pruning apple trees, all those things that get my body working and moving. I'm even thinking of building some monkey bars!

My garden shed built with old building materials.

Just working on the dreams that I've had since a child!

I do have some sad news. I've lost my favorite hen to the wild creatures around me. She was the first to come up to me as a chick and let me pick her up. As the biggest and bravest, she soon became the alpha hen in the flock and keep everyone in line. Her voice was so deep that sometimes she sounded rather like a mourning dove than a squawky chicken. There's something about a bird that will come up to you, peck at your clothes for attention, and allow you to reach down and pick them up. She would then settle down in my arms and stay forever. A bird in the hand.

The old guy and his chick.

Glad to hear you're feeling less fibro stuff. Outside, working in the garden will be good for you. Do you have a watering trough you can fill with cold water and soak in? I'm picturing you relaxing in it and enjoying the scenery!

### sooperb — 2014-04-16T18:47:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-121895>

Hens, thought by some to be silly and stupid. I love ours. We currently have a brown one who just wants to be a house hen, door to the garden open, cue brown hen to come running either in the expectation of food waste or to get into the house and with luck, make her way to the dog's bowl to see if he's left any! The black hen is the complete opposite, step out into the garden and she lifts up her skirt and runs round looking to escape, even though no one goes near her. She's the bottom of the pecking order of the three we have.

Our Bengal is afraid of the hens and with good reason, they see him coming and start puffing up and looking threatening, if he gets too close he gets the full angry hen treatment, the Maran jumps on him and he collapses like a paper bag, I never tire of watching them. The brown hen eyes him up and puts her head down to charge him if steps out too far, he tries rolling on his back in his best friendly mode but they don't fall for it, so he just gets up and tries to walk around them in a big arc. Even my Coonie who's a big, big cat, keeps out of their way, yet either cat could almost certainly pin a hen down if they wanted to.

You look a lot like your brother Martin and not so much like your avatar lol. Still chuckling about that

### Martin — 2015-10-31T22:57:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180853>

It's been nearly two years since I last posted! Let's reflect...

I started this journey to better health nearly six years ago when I crashed after a flu-like bug. Took a week to get out of bed. Felt a fundamentally bad change in energy. I figured it would pass, but it persisted. Always the researcher, I looked up my symptoms and it led me to investigating infections, Lyme, mold, and it was Doc's "What to do about peripheral neuropathy" blog that first got my attention because my hands were hurting constantly, and my feet not far behind.
Doc's Lyme blog was chock full of pertinent info and I was sure I had it, but after a few key tests and further investigation, it didn't indicate so strongly. Whew.

Mold was next, as they share a similar pathway in the body. More testing produced mixed results, and after gobs (literally) of cholestyramine, not much had improved. Sure, my TGFb1 was down, but VEGF was always below what Shoemaker's markers showed, passed VCS many times more than not (tip: I'm very visual and Shoe says we artists have a stronger than average acuity), MMP9 not in danger zone, no MARCONS, and no water-damaged home conditions. In fact, I'd get around mold and never feel any difference. Same house for ten years, and felt great right up until the flu incident.

It wasn't until I got some further tweaking and guidance from Doc and Dr Jackson that I even had a glimmer of change for the better. Then I slip back down. Then up again. Down, up, you know the drill. Healing is not linear... where did I learn that?!
I believe now that it's not mold or Lyme, but the lifelong imbalance of modern living coupled with a shitty redox download from my mom.

But I held steady cuz I was in the _best place ever_ to get through this mess - Doc's site here. I knew that damn purple pony was in there somewhere! As I started to improve my living space and everyday habits, I began to wear blu-blockers and stood fast against the ribbing and rolled eyes. I cleaned up my magnetic fields, watched how I exposed myself to fake light, practiced grounding, relaxing, and ate seafood more and more. We were already into local food and meats so that was never a problem, but it's nice to get some data here that you can trust to guide you better.

CT was tough. I'm a skinny guy with both adrenal and thyroid insufficiencies so getting into cold water was a bitch. I used to shiver uncontrollably if my shoulders or back got a chill. I'd bundle up at the first sign of cold weather and wouldn't come out of them until the dead of summer. But reading the how's and why's help me commit to keep going with getting cold exposure.

This year, I was determined to get into the mid-fifties water temp so I constructed a barrel rig, added a little beach chair, a ground wire, and would get in at my well water temp of 65. Breakthough!
"Hell, this is easy", I thought to myself. But gotta get to the magic of 50-55 degrees. I got into the filled barrel, and then dragged in two big bags of ice before I changed my mind. My feet lasted about three minutes and I pulled them just above the water level. I watched as the temp gauge settled in at 53 degrees - I win! After the initial shock, which lasts about fifteen seconds, I'd settle in to a pleasant 45-50 minute soak and not feel the least bit uncomfy.

I need to do more CT. There, I admit it. But it's not the "death sentence" I used to conjure up.

More from me later, but it's always a good indication when I feel good enough to open up to the world here. I get very reclusive when I feel bad and this really helps get me through it.

Martin

### Martin — 2015-11-01T17:04:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180897>

I can stretch out and dip my knees but usually I just leave them out. The barrel? I assume it's a 55 gallon?

I did mold therapy with a doc in Atlanta for about nine months. Five buckets of CSM at $210 each and took it religiously. Not one bit of difference I could put a finger on, but I learned a lot and helped people steer their path to wellness in perhaps a different direction. I know people who defiantly get affected by going into a water-damaged building but I seemed to not react. I spent four days in my brother's warehouse suite helping him move tons (literally) of hot rod cars and parts before I asked him what that funky-ass smell was. He said it was where the roof leaked and soaked carpet and ceiling insulation. We worked for two more days and no symptoms of mold exposure. When I set out for home after spending nearly a week with my brother, I actually felt good. Why? The movement and lifting/walking/climbing helped my body's communication system work better. Where did I learn that? Tensegrity Series. HUGE revelation. Get out and move though your environment, people.

I couldn't get some of Doc's blog posts and answers to questions about mold out of my head. Mold is everywhere and I've been around it all my life. Change me or my environment? I'm doing both because from what I've learned here is that I need to improve how I handle naturally occurring pathogens. I did many changes to my house and workshops.

About six months ago I felt a subtle but persistent change in my CFS/ME, and a slight up-tic in my ability to handle just doing simple activities each day. My arms and legs ached a little less and I was able to do more work and planted a fall garden. More pics on that later (hint: ask Caroline!).

My mental processes are the other part of this big story. You know how, like after five years of feeling like shit and chronic pain, you eventually don't give a fuck about much? Things that were a once a beautiful, rewarding part of life are burdens and of no value? Your once eternal creativity dulled to practically nothing? Grumpy, pessimistic thoughts abound?

Well, after learning about neurotransmitters and hormones 101 here on Optimal, I was able to crack a slight smile and push onward through it all because I realized that my disconnection from Nature's Laws were affecting my ability to give a shit.

Martin

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-01T17:23:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180898>

Nice, made me smile this AM. Key is not to procrastinate because mediocrity is all that brings.

The best way to avoid procrastinating is to begin. If you're stuck in your past, you go forward in reverse and nothing changes. We may delay, but time won't. The most pernicious aspect of procrastination is that it can become a habit. A habit institutionalizes us to mediocrity. Action allows us to step out of the crowd of average people. Enter that game and alter the numbers on the scoreboard. Mediocrity is what's left when vision is absent.

### Martin — 2015-11-01T17:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180901>

When I learned about the Magnetico, I immediately set about learning how they were constructed and planned to build one. I'm a recovering DIY'er and this was right up my alley. After many months of some trials and errors, and a cost analysis, I said screw it and started saving, selling, and scraping up money to buy one. And not just a wimpy one, but the maxed-out Super Sleep king-sized pads. Took delivery a few months ago. DONE.

And about after watching me parade around the house in amber-tinted glasses for nearly three years, I put it to my DW that she needed to wear them cuz it would help her sleep. I guess seeing me get better impressed her, but the promise of sleeping through the night had a certain appeal as well. She's had a chronic post nasal drip for nearly ten years and drives her a little batty. But to her credit, she handles it very well and doesn't sink into despair. She is quite adept at calming the shrieking voices we create in our heads to tear ourselves down. I also got her a RubtLux light to hold against her face and it is so cool to see her with this erie glow on her adorable face. Big wins for me to see her embrace some changes. Thanks, Doc, for the circadian info and direction.

Most mornings, we would both head down to the pasture next to the gardens and catch some rays . Best tan ever. My grandkids stopped asking, "Why are your legs yellow, Grandpa?" Then I'd hear her talking to her friends about light and blue blocking and how they affect us. Be still, my aorta. Things are a'changing.

But when she says that she's sleeping better and dreaming more, man I gotta tell ya, that rocks. And her sleep apnea seems to have improved some. She's more refreshed upon waking than in many years. And that's another HUGE change in me as well. I have ALWAYS felt like crap when I got up in the morning. For the last six months or more, I've noticed that once I'm up, I quickly gather steam and do things like clean up the kitchen or run out to the shop or car for something that I need to do.... in my slippers! AMAZING! Most days I feel the best when I first get up, then gradually wind down. More autophagy! Decent cortisol rise! Even some AM sexy dreams and woody-ness! How do I know all that? The Sleep webinar and blogs.

Martin

### Jack Kruse — 2015-11-01T17:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180903>

Think of each thing that happens in life as an information upload to your mind. It is neither good or bad, just information. Then use the information to adjust. Consider that before becoming more aware you wouldn't have even noticed how bright the lights were or made the connection between the artificial lights (and EMF) and the quality of your sleep that night. It is funny how what looks like an initial setback, might even be considered a step forward in retrospect.

### Martin — 2015-11-01T18:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-180906>

When I learned about paleo, I embraced my inner lard-eater with renewed enthusiasm and ate Amish butter like it was ice-cream. But this neurosurgeon guy keep nudging us towards seafood to really up our game. Why? DHA, baby. What the fuck good is all that high-quality animal fat if we can't catch light to use it?! Ask Jimmie Moore.

So DW searches out sources and comes up with a wild-caught salmon source near us. My local Wally-mart has oysters at the best prices around. Yum. This salmon is caught by a family who has a boat in Alaska and it's been a family tradition for decades. Blood red, sweet, it's the first raw salmon I've ever tried. Oh, man! We bought twenty pounds the first year, ran out. Thirty the next, and bought some from a friend after we ran out. This year forty pounds of salmon and ten pounds of cod from the same Alaskan waters. How can we afford this? I bought less goddamn supplements this year!!! Check out [https://www.facebook.com/Heidis-Wild-Salmon-270656416343588/?fref=ts](https://www.facebook.com/Heidis-Wild-Salmon-270656416343588/?fref=ts)

This DHA source, and the improvement in light exposure, is turning this little tub around. Doc, I read in one of your early blogs that some maladies (metabolic syndrome, CFS/ME) can take 18 to 36 months to get turned around in the right direction.

I groaned, but I knew that years down the pike, I'd be glad I embraced discomfort and changed my conditions of existence and I've read and re-read everything Optimal. I know a pathway through when I see one. Knowing that you, Dr Jack Kruse, are looking at Nature's principles kept me on this track. We can trust Mother Nature.

Oysters are not keep frozen, just there for effect.

>
> Think of each thing that happens in life as an information upload to your mind. It is neither good or bad, just information. Then use the information to adjust. Consider that before becoming more aware you wouldn't have even noticed how bright the lights were or made the connection between the artificial lights (and EMF) and the quality of your sleep that night. It is funny how what looks like an initial setback, might even be considered a step forward in retrospect.

Oh yeah. When I get blasted by night lighting it's shocking. I look at NOTHING the same way these days. The fall colors on a maple, turning first up at the top and less as you look at the lower leaves. All this has been right in front of us.

I am so very happy Martin to read all the above .....

Thanks for all the support you always give me my dear friend ..... and all the pics!

### Martin — 2015-11-05T17:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-181205>

Whether my legs and arms ache is one of my leading indicators as to how I'm doing. It's good day if I'm not dragging around meat stumps trying to have a life. Every day I can get up from sleeping and not feel hammered is a win for me these days.

In the past three months, I've felt a change into a more relaxed body. I'm more willing to get going on activities, and even to take a break. And it’s been consistent. I’ve rollercoaster-ed many times in the past years, it and was always a sign to tighten up my environment and look for un-natural, non-native conditions around me. And eat more seafood and pork.

A delightful change has been these spontaneous thought around what drives Martin. Some of the thoughts feeling and urges from way back are returning. Hard to describe because to the casual observer, they seem trivial and pedestrian. But I know what gets my creative juices flowing and what calms me down into a pleasant reverie.

Both have their dopamine aspects. And when I start eyeing women with bad intent, well, that's just plain fun. Good sign! More dopamine. More testosterone. More (or less) of everything floating around in my body.

Time to go get labs updated. I've endeavored to get my sun this past season to see where I could get my vit. D level to with no supplementation. All that sun has turned me into a bronzed rockstar. Calm down, girls. Except for you, Caroline. Please feel free to swoon away.

Just scored fifteen pounds of local ham hocks to season my upcoming collard greens fall crop. Thank you, Ms. Piggy, wherever you are.

Great to hear of your changes Martin. So inspiring! Keep up the good work and fish eatin'.

### Martin — 2015-11-08T17:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-181396>

Day 92 (more or less, of course) of waking up a _bit_ more refreshed and alert. Once I get vertical and sit on the edge of the bed for a few moments, I'm ready to go do stuff. _NO_ dopiness and lethargy dragging me back onto that mattress. I'm dreaming with a bit more clarity and remembering them better. Morning hunger is increasing, and the urge to get outside has become welcome.

Since I've been on this site, I've learned things that offer explanations to what's screwing up our health. Conversations with friends would invariably bring about discussions around not only health, but human nature as well. My friends and family are starting to ask more and more why I wear amber glasses at night and why morning UV light is so important. Even a small tip such as "not snacking ever" can pique someone's interest to the point where they have a realization around it and want more. It's interesting when the people around you no longer think you're frickin' nuts and then want to incorporate better habits. This world becomes clearer when you follow Nature.

I've begun to look at how long and deeply I've been in a chronic, low-grade ME/CFS condition, and I'm beginning to see how being a lifelong night owl sugar-eater has affected me. I've always had a low pain tolerance perhaps due to a susceptibility to a low a-MSH level as revealed by a DRBQ test I did a few years back. Now I have ways to ameliorate those types of imbalances.

Yesterday, I picked some rose hips from my wild rose garden and brewed up some tea. Small pleasures. I'm a lucky man. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Martin — 2015-11-17T18:10:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-182191>

Healing is not linear. I learned that here. This last week has been a little rougher than the previous ones. Increased aches and a bit less energy.

Mentally, I really take a hit when I get more tired. Things that have been traditionally bright and inviting become dull and uninteresting. Sometimes even worthless. But I have answers and terms that explain what's going on and I can take some degree of comfort in, even if I feel like shit on a stick.

I'm looking into maximizing DHA absorption now that I have this massive amount of fish in the freezer. I know, fresh is best, but moving to Alaska is currently on hold. HCL/betaine really helps me maintain bowel regularity and breaking down the fishy goodies. Ramping up bacon fat to cook with and tapering off coconut oils heading into winter. Investigating a potential source of local lard and need to follow up. Some tallow would be nice, too.

Continuing with stretching and yoga to keep collagen pathways connected. Walking on a new path a friend cut through the woods. Nothing like moving through one's environment to get some Hebbian learning on.

Added Duracell UV lights around the house. Ceramic bulb sockets needed insulating with tape to cover exposed wire terminals but have handy-dandy mounting lugs so they can be attached to a surface easily. I've even got some friends interested in UV lights cuz they have figured out that I'm a freak when it comes to light, water, and magnetism.

### Martin — 2015-12-02T19:54:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-183075>

Got through a rough week with a slight fever and body aches. Oysters from market are really getting good this time of year. More people around me are asking some great questions about light and health. Good times. Sunny tomorrow after nearly five days of cold, dark rain.

Just got back from the dentist and all I could think about was the blue LED headlight on the doc and how intense the beam was. Wow. And the overhead T-8 fluorescent light fixtures flickering away. Nothing is the same in this modern world when you know better.

Forty-two plants! Lots of blood meal, bone meal, kelp meal, and good well water. This should give you something to shoot for, Krista.

Martin

### Martin — 2015-12-09T20:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-183808>

With my dwindling UV index heading towards the winter solstice, I have about a half an hour at 1 pm to catch some rays. And who comes out these days to join me?! DW! I love that she is becoming more curious about Light and the crazy shit I peddle around the house. DW ordered her some lblueblockers and is quite proud of them. My DIL is getting more savvy as well. Both are smart, articulate, funny, inquisitive women so I guess it was inevitable.

FM a bit better this morning. I had a good run of energy (remember, it's all relative) yesterday afternoon and did some handwork out in the shop redoing an entry door. Felt good to work. Then crash! A recurring pattern of afternoon dip in energy gets me heading for a place to lay down. It recharges me a bit, but not fun to have to do this so often.

I lifted a 50 pound (22.5 kilos) of birdseed and really tweaked my lower back. On the bright side, it didn't go out immediately and it's healing faster then a few years ago when it would just explode and put me down for a week. Collagen, baby. One of the things I've really improved on since all things Optimal. I like that. Doing yoga every day and walking more. Weight hovers at 143.

Mentally, I'm experiencing no interest or enthusiasm. Nothing has been the same since the illness event. I go through the motions (fake it till ya make it ) and get some good thoughts, but something has been _very_ different. Things I've loved forever - building, growing, music and art activities - are all just kind of not there. Low dopamine, baby. I see/feel glimpses of those endeavors, but the unbridled excitement ain't happening. But knowing about brain activities and neurotransmitters really help me take it in a more calm way. I don't feel lost because of the things I'm learning here. I know what to do in a broad sense, but would like a sharper, more detailed approach to my N=1.

Measured sulfites with test strip yesterday and it was a bit high - +800. Less eggs, pork? I don't do NAC or other sulfury sups.

Been on this mountain at 1800 feet (GPS!) for over 15 years. Lots going for it in so many ways. Altitude ain't one of them. Water is crystal-clear, sweet, and clean right out of the earth. Soil is poor (geopathically stressed), but the garden spots have been organically amended for over thirty years. The house is very comfortable and workable kitchen that I remodeled is very efficient and beautiful to boot. Nearest cell tower is nearly two miles away and signal is very weak, but present. Our area was the last to be serviced for cell usage and we used to complain about it... until we knew better. Lots of range bulbs and I just scored more anger bulbs at the local hardware store. Dumb meter on the house. I've done lots of EMF remediation but what troubles me are the electrical fields. They vary widely and I can reduce them at night, but the wiring is a hodgepodge. No big magnetic fields anywhere except around the mains panel, freezer, and the hot water heater. I've shielded them, but it always makes me wonder what the difference if they were zero and it makes me think of Feynman's "toaster" quote. The south-facing big porch is good in the fall after the leaves start dropping in order to catch some sun. But we usually go to the pasture to sun and ground. We're so private (getting less so), a quick nude sunbath can be done often. Can I leave this? Is the gulf coast calling me and my woman? She's up for a change and we can go spend a few weeks at some different locales to see how it fits. Really thinking about this change. But to where?

Martin

### caroline — 2015-12-10T10:45:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-183833>

Martin ..... What happens when you and lynne sleep in my little cabin?

So many things to think about isn't there -for all of us.

I used to sleep great in my apartment on the third floor and tons of wifi signals surrounded me.

Now - we sleep on the ground floor, just a dirt crawl space underneath, hardly any wifi signals here, huge double lot sized back yard, very old area with lots of trees, bushes, flowers, grass. I get lots more grounding and sunshine here.
I am really struggling to get a good nites sleep .....can't work it out.

### Inger — 2015-12-10T12:29:40+0200

That is sad you cant sleep Audrey

I can think about a few things..... could it be a geopatic stressor? That the spot you sleep on is not good? There are ways to measure it I think. Or you could try to sleep in another room?
Or in a tent in the backyard?
... how is it with late eating... coffee and alcohol? Fake lights? I assume you have none... do you watch TV in the evenings?
Do you have it as cold as it gets in your bedroom? What mattresses you use? Hopefully no metal in bed or mattress?
Have you measured the house with an EMF meter?

I sleep so well in my horse barn........ it is totally dark there except when moonlight and I still sleep well the moon shining on me .. and it is very cold..lol I cant sleep in warm bedrooms anymore no way
Do you have a magnetico? It really helps with sleep too

I recall you do had some sleeping struggles in your apartment once in a while too

Generally I sleep very well no matter where I am and have since I can remember. That's not to say my brain is running it's programs to it's best ability though. But at least I'm not _overtly_ suffering from some of the problems of staying asleep I see others experience. My DW has had sleep issues for a while but I gotta tell you, since going with the blueblockers and 20 gauss Magnetico, she has really improved.

And another couple that we are close to did the same and the feedback makes all of us here on Optimal smile. This stuff works! We had a decent magnetic mattress pad with 450 correctly-pointing ceramic magnets just like Magnetico's and I gave that to them. Good feedback coming from them seems to be consistent.

Audrey, I've tried sleeping in there for a month and didn't notice any difference, but I've considered it again lately. No Magnetico and the mattress sucks for my back. But it's becoming intriguing to hack the space again.

Martin

### Martin — 2015-12-10T15:45:07+0200

It's really on our minds, Inger. We've heard it here many times - it's the environment. All the remediation and adjustments have helped, but there is more to go. I have to confront what I've learned here. One must act and change one's conditions of existence. We've been here since 1998 and had a blast, but now we are considering a change.... a BIG one. The gulf shore will be many hours further from my son and his family and that troubles me. They are already too far away for my liking and I'm missing so much around their children growing up.

Apalachicola seems to be the closest to them and still be on the upper gulf shore. It has a strong oyster/seafood industry and has a quiet, less touristy reputation. We need to go there and check out various nnEMF conditions.

### Martin — 2015-12-15T18:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-184207>

This past week has been a mixed bag of progress and pain. I've noticed an internal tenderness next to my larynx that goes down to the center/right of my chest that coincided with a drop in energy and increase in FM. I thought it was a sore throat, but no irritation or typical symptoms with one. Swallowing is okay, but it can irritate the throat area if enough throat movement happens. Flared for three days and then overnight, it reduced significantly. Hell yeah. My energy and stamina came up and I have a slightly rosier outlook on life. Dopamine, baby.

Overall body muscle stiffness and pain over these months has increased slightly. Wrists are a little tender, and an old sartorial muscle injury has reared it's little head. Tore it several decades ago on a water slide when one leg went one way and... you get the picture. Upped my walking and moving. Neck gets stiff and I push and manipulate and it pops like crazy and then settles down until the next time. Increased yoga and stretching. Overall pain tolerance is poor and any little bonk or jab really hurts. Low aMSH and myelin sheath issues abound. I've always had a low tolerance for pain, temp extremes, and activity. I exhibit Shoemaker's gene marker for low aMSH.

Tinnitus increased about a year ago. I realized that it's always been present throat my life, albeit at a much lower level then this past year. Fairly consistent and will occasionally increase in intensity, but rare. I remember telling a childhood friend about this "sound" in my hearing when there was no ambient sounds that sounded like a high-pitched whine. He made a comment about it being "utter silence" and I shrugged it off and figured everyone is that way. Silly me.

My family and close friends continue to embrace what I've been harping on for years and it does this old guy good to see my DIL getting my precious grandchildren to try on blue blockers. And my precious DW online ordering BB glasses for everyone. Quite a change from just a year ago. Other friends can get rather irritated when I offer some nature-based quantum information, say for instance, on why deciding to be a goddamn vegan has a downside. "Psycology of previous investment" is at play here. We humans are good at staking off a position and fighting tooth and nail to defend it, sometimes to our own demise. Ego, baby. I've done it. But I endeavor to listen to another viewpoint because I realize that it may save me some trouble, let alone my life. Don't be too proud and cocky to embrace change.

Heading into the winter solstice my UV index is nil. I must head over to local doc to get some labs drawn to see where I am. I did some decent sun exposure this past summer and really miss being in the garden bare foot and butt naked (pictures upon request). And lately, DW has joined me in sitting in the sun around 1 pm just cuz it feels so damn good. And first thing this morning, she beat me outside, standing there facing the sunrise. I almost cried. You go, woman. She is good at embracing change, sometimes better than me. I have wondered if doing CT in my little blue barrel in the sun is an optimal idea. I thought a Fournier effect would be beneficial, but something I heard or read lately has me wondering if there is a mismatch while doing that. I would mainly do it after 4 pm, but later changed the location of the tub to a more shaded location. Can't remember why.

My lab has been a great teacher for me. When she was ill, she would stand at the front door and bolt for the outside and lay in the sun while asleep on the ground. Even in 18 degree weather. She'd sack out for hours. I had friends give me grief about "not taking care of my dog" by letting her do that. More human, big-brain bullshit. She's teaching me! Now at nearly sixteen years old, she spends all her time outside and won't come in.

She's trying to tell me something.

Martin

"Jenna"

### Martin — 2015-12-20T16:02:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-184581>

Guilty as teased. Back when the forum was going through some changes, we had to put a human picture up and I chose this one for fun (it's probably the _best_ picture of me ever and it still surprises me when I look at it) and intended to post a current picture of myself that didn't suck. Haven't found one yet.

Then all the girls went nuts over it. I'm certainly not used to that! Hmm? What's a shy, clueless, reclusive boy to do?

So, said "ego" kept it up, all the while planning to get into the 21st century with a current photo. Another forum change enlarged the picture so it was bigger than anyone else's, so my ego really got pumped up and that's where the "Big Giant Head" meme came along. Milked that for what it was worth.

Another forum change reduced The Head back down to the size of the other mere mortals here and I just left it up as it had become a "thing" and had a life of it's own.

So! What's an egoic Ex Big Giant Head to do?! Continue on this path of self-delusion? Continue to long for those halcyon days of yore? Go snap a pic and get fucking real?

Let's vote! Anyone in favor of taking this false idol down, cast your ballot here.

Ex Big Giant Headed Ego

### Martin — 2015-12-30T17:33:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-185119>

Leg cramps in the evening have increased a bit. Upped mg malate and threonate. Morning headaches increased in the last week, but nothing for the last two days. Yay. My wrist and hand joints are a bit more sensitive and it will surprise me, unpleasantly, that a heavy pan full of hot food needs some extra care. My damn sartorial muscle tear injury from way back is hurting again. Tinnitus still ringing away, albeit not as bad as I've read about others experiencing. Still, it'b be nice to get rid of that crap forever. Not a healthy sign.

Listening to older webinars again. I feel like I'm at the head of the class on the front row and one of the teacher's pets. Going back to older releases is like getting another vacation or birthday - you get to experience revelations, and the concepts are clearer. I look for Light clues in these and apply them to my life. Long history of bad circadian mashups going back to early childhood. I remember a lot from that time and I made staying up late reading and watching TV a personal goal. My mother called me a "night owl" and I continued to get up late and go to bed late for the next forty-eight years. My first clue back in my 20's was a 2nd shift job that wrecked havoc on my life. Weird sleep patterns showed me that I needed to bail and never did that kind of job again.

I have a friend whose VERY mold sensitive come spend the night here and he had no issues. He's mentioned many instances where he has been hammered within minutes upon entering certain buildings. It's happened to him many times and lives on the edge. I've been in water-damaged buildings for days and nothing unusual. I've been in crawlspaces this year that would put some people in their grave... and not even a stuffy nose. All clues to my case.

I had a run-in with another friend who brought up her "candida elimination diet" and I offered up some clarity only to be shot down with a wall of denial. I tried to say that cheese and peanut butter are not worthy substitutes for high quality animal fats. Being vegetarian fits her love of animals and I get that, but why sacrifice your own body on that alter? Got to let it go.

Still gotta update my mugshot. That guy in the upper left doesn't exist anymore.

Martin

Morning body temp: 98, afternoon 98. I had a fever 3 weeks ago and started testing more often. Stealth infection? I have a clinic a few hours away where I got two UVBI treatments early last spring. Didn't noticed much difference.
Sulfite test: 600
I seem to take about an hour or more to go to sleep after going to bed around 9:30.
Apetite good, FM a little less yesterday, raw salmon for lunch.

### Martin — 2016-01-03T17:57:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-185357>

**Sweaty Palms**

"Palmar hyperhidrosis" has been increasing this past year. Seems to be worse in the morning? Really makes it tough to hold my guitar pick. Another clue as to why I never could hold the damn thing well while I play. I ruined my new Mac wired mouse's roller ball with my gummy residue. Jeez. Need to find a quantum connection to this malady just for my own curiosity. I don't sweat _profusely_ out in hot weather, but do seem to readily in a sauna. DW takes forever to sweat in same, but has improved.

**"Dude... 'shrooms!**

Listened to the webinar with Doc and Erwan La Corre and was wondering how one's redox potential would affect psilocybin usage. Alkaloids are heavy duty stuff, but the idea of "refocusing" one's thought habits is intriguing. Reading about chronic pain being partly a function of the brain being stuck "in gear?" Biohack coming up in the spring? I have a beautiful spot on my land that is quite tranquil and have a good friend who's studied with Stanislov Grof many times and has facilitated many journeys along this line of expanded thinking.

**Pop Quiz**

Gearing up for labs this month. Got to find out where I stand. I have a doc an hour away who let's her patients get labs at near her cost.
Vit D
HSCRP
Thyroid Panel with antibodies, Reverse T3
Prolactin
IGF-1
Free Testosterone, Total Testosterone
SHBG
Estradiol Ratio
PSA
DHT
Chemistry Panel and Complete Blood Count

And then order a Salivary Cortisol, Melatonin/DHEA panel. And then I'll share my results with some family members/friends and perhaps inspire them to get off their hineys and turn over a lab rock or two.

Martin

I want to know more about shrooms too!!

### Snowyky — 2016-01-15T16:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-186359>

Hello Martin, read your journal last night and finished up this morning, bio-toxin is a hateful thing and am glad to see you're coming out the other end slowly but surely. I also used to get terrible cramps whenever I switched over to paleo/low carb woe and found that I had to make sure I drank over 64oz of water daily or they would show up and are excruciating, I was getting them everywhere not just my calves, the inner thigh ones were the worst. I also take mag malate daily as well, maybe this will help you get rid of them. Good luck. Snowy

### Martin — 2016-01-15T17:45:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-186367>

Thanks snowyky, it's been a rough seven (!) years. Over six years ago, "Biotoxin Illness" became the closest description for my symptoms and I did a boatload towards that end, but I've come to believe that it's not the predominate factor in what kicked off all this MCS/CFS/ME/PTSD/LSMFT. Martin Pall's NO/ONOO cycle research (and others) describe the symptoms and perhaps some of the pathways well enough to give some additional clarity on what's going on, but I see where the deepest effects and remedies are being shown to be the electro-magnetic spectrum.

I _may_ have severe enough brain damage from that badass illness event back when to make certain symptoms persist. Maybe not. I have had many of the same problems since I was a very young boy. C'est la vie. But I've enjoyed the learning about what goes on in our biology and always have. The science here is deep, but attainable. But most importantly, I TRUST what Doc has to say. That's HUGE in my world.

Bottom line: keep going forward towards living with Nature and perhaps plugging in a Quantlet or UVA bulb or two to help the process. And don't forget to balance with red. There's tons of free stuff to do to ease the pain and I learned it ALL here on Optimal.

Martin

PS did we correspond a few years back? your name is familiar.

### Snowyky — 2016-01-15T23:14:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-186413>

Yes we did, I haven't been to the forum for quite some time, since 2012 I think, gave up for a while since it didn't seem to be doing what it should but when I look back at things, I see most of the problems were stemming from myself not following the protocol properly. I had problems for a while too from mold and finally seem to have gotten past that, I noticed the other day that my breathing was a whole lot better and I'm finding myself having more energy and notice a few of the LS symptoms are showing up.

I haven't started CT yet, just sticking to the leptin reset but adding as much seafood as I can afford. Just can't make myself eat oysters but I've read that your tastes change eventually so we'll see. Meanwhile I'll stick to what I know and as long as I feel better and the weight keeps coming off that it's all good. I don't have super high expectations anymore, as long as I'm down a few pounds a week I'm happy with it, of course it's usually more then that so I'm more then happy with it

### Martin — 2016-02-07T22:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188487>

Ran some labs in January. Blood drawn in AM around 10.
Pile on with opinions and suggestions, folks.

HSCRP .20 mg/dL _Been low for a while now. Yay!_
Vit D 28.3 ng/mL _Crappy as can be. No supplementation this year, only daily sun exposure._
PSA 3.58 0.00 - 4.00 ng/mL
Free T4 0.97 0.59 - 1.17 ng/mL
Free Testosterone 10.2 6-.6 - 18.1 pg/mL Let's bump that up, please.
Serum Testosterone 674 348 - 1197 ng/dL
SHBG 102.1 19.0 - 76.4 nmol/L _Good god. Up from around 86.0 from the last time. Looking for good info._
Estradiol 10.7 7.6 - 42.6 pg/mL
Prolactin 4.5 4.0 - 15.2 ng/mL I'm thinking melatonin release at night is keeping this down properly?
Free T3 2.9 2.0 - 4.0 pgmL

Glucose a little high.
BUN/Cr ratio sucks ass. Have not gotten it below 10.0 ever.
Would like C02 to be higher.
Decent Trigs, but HDL?
LDL is High, but HSCRP is low.
Low Alk Phos... again.

### Martin — 2016-02-07T23:10:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188489>

A few days ago, I did **60 drops** of MB that was equivalent to **120 mcg** when I mixed it according to the info on MB on the self-hacked site . I spread it out in three doses after not getting much of a boost from the first dose. Then I felt a little less FM/CFS symptoms and upped it a bit to the 120 dose. But after reading how much higher doses have therapeutic effects, I'm looking to do a **"mg"** dose to see what happens. Continued this for two days. Added nicotine gum each day dosing from 1 to 2 mg. Slight woozy feeling gave way to somewhat less ache and a tad more energy in AM, but couldn't maintain in afternoon after adding 120 mcg of MB.

Today, I did 240 mcg MB in AM with 2 mg nicotine gum to up the previous days dosing. Ugh. Didn't give a boost and eventually got more tired. Variables include working unusually hard the day before which tends to burn through my energy stores. I may go to higher doses to see if it improves. Then back down to 120 mcg or lower to see what happens.

Leg cramps are getting a bit worse at night. Taking Mg Threonate in AM, 1200 mg malate at night and have for over a year. Helped but didn't eliminate. Muscles and joints are stiff and sore constantly, but sometimes I get some small relief in the afternoon when my cortisol tends to raise (according to the two saliva cortisol tests I've had through the years) in the afternoon.

Tinnitus is constant and a bit troubling. Sensitive to loud noises, have been for years (decades, really). Applying cold to carotid artery.

### Martin — 2016-02-07T23:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188491>

Back to being "Diaper Dan" in the past few months. I think winter is when I get leaky, but need to confirm. Going to my doc and request vasopressin hormone treatment to possibly help and up my BUN/Creatine ratio. Also talk to her about some bioidentical hormone treatment again. I did well on T and Progesterone two years ago but tapered off to see how I fared.

Found high electrical fields around my home and while I shield and moved things around, the high fields were an obvious concern. I had power company come test the ohms resistance value of my ground rod stuck in my shallow soil and the technician said it was the highest he'd ever measure 200 ohms (it needs to be 25 or lower). I have very shallow soil, so an 8 foot ground rod cannot be driven in either vertically or at the maximum of 45 degrees. What to do? There is a concrete product that conducts current and can be laid down in a trench and surrounds the ground wire. When when it sets up, it turns the whole trench bottom into a bigger conductor. [http://www.sankosha-usa.com/sanearth.asp](http://www.sankosha-usa.com/sanearth.asp)

Herbert IIRC Jack has stated that SHGB for men should be 25nmol/L. so you want to bring that down. Prolactin is low - how's your ASI looking particularly in the AM? Are mornings get up and go? or dragging? For thyroid I like my Free T4 to be over 1. I feel better there. I also like my Free T3 btwn 3-3.5. but I think you may have a conversion issue - to support hormone cascade looking at T and your LDL. Remember LDL converts to pregnenolone which coupled wiht Vitamin A and T3. = hormones. with your D so low, you've definitely got an issue with Vitamin A conversion... Beyond supplementation have you looked at a vitamin D light?
