### Shijin13 — 2016-02-08T00:35:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188500>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=188495)
>
> ...and this one:
>
>
> Thanks, you gorgeous hunk of femininity, and so fast!
>
> I've been using a Sperti D light for two weeks now, ramping up exposure each day or two. I raised my D with micellized D over a year ago from 30 to 70's but we know about sups. Did a ton of sun this year but didn't get around to testing until this January and not sure how long it takes to "burn" through one's stores.
>
> I've had the SHBG thing for a while now and don't know what to do other than endeavor to get as optimal as I can.

You know I love you Herbert!!! I will say I didn't supplement since Fall of 2014. b/c we should supplement what nature makes. However, my N=1 of not supplementing for a whole year resulted in lower redox over all. I got bronchitis 3xs, pneumonia 1x, an ear infection (Seriously, who gets ear infections in their 40s???) Strep (road it out w/o abx b/c the test came back 2 weeks after I had the test run - Labcorp is failing badly) and 2x cellulitis on two different fingers. I'm still recovering from the pneumonia (still coughing almost 2 mths later) but when I got the pneumonia I loaded up on D3 10K IU /day with every 3rd day taking 15K. my D3 in the summer was in the 60's and it probably fell during the fall. within 2 weeks of dosing it shot up into the 90's. So for me. I'm supplementing D3, b/c its's what works for me. And yes. I get that I'm doing it wrong, but my N=1 says supplementing is the best for me, until I can get a job teaching snowboarding that pays as much as I make now (ain't every gonna happen)

As for SHGB - @[Josh (Paleo Osteo)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9815/) and @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) have me researching aspirin as a way to mitigate excess estrogen.... I haven't looked at how it impacts SHGB - but my research is limited b/c most of my free time is focusing on pre-reqs for nursing school.

> [lilreddgirl said:](/goto/post?id=188496)
>
> How would someone go about raising prolactin?

To raise prolactin you have to raise melatonin. Your melatonin status determines how much prolactin is released. When you're releasing melatonin at the right time, you release adequate prolactin. Prolactin should be lowest in the morning and should peak during peak sleep, approximately 4 hrs after melatonin has peaked. Peak time for prolactin secretion is 2-4am.

### Martin — 2016-02-08T23:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188564>

From Doc:
"If your SHB is high and estrogen levels normal then you can localize your electron steal syndrome to either the plasma or the liver. Based upon your other labs you appear to have a mixed pattern of both. Your DHEA level tells us that the IL6 in your CSF is likely higher than you want it to be and this could directly be affecting your liver via the vagus nerve. This picture you have painted here is one that is mixed. But make no mistake you have a significant loss of electrons occurring to ionize SHBG to lower its charge. This depletes your plasma of electrons and the SHBG rises because it can not do its job with a lower level of electrons present within the blood. This is concerning because it also means you might be at risk for clotting or DVT 's with excessive airplane travel. The water issue must be a player here. I think you need to move to abetter source like Spring water or RO water. I also think you could help yourself with significantly adding ubiquinol not ubiquinone to your regimen. Resveratrol of polyphenols from organic blueberries would also help."

Sounds like a situation for the Quantlet's abilities. Just checked my UV index for 34 degrees latitude and it's not happening yet. I have some decent ubiquinol to take. Liver, huh? Not planning any airplane travel soon. Elevation is 1800 feet where I live.

**Pain Dept.**
More leg cramps last night. Caught it quickly and stretched it out, but this morning my right calf muscle is bitching at me.

Progesterone came back: **0.7** 0.2 - 1.4 ng/mL

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=185386)
>
> I want to know more about shrooms too!!

Still researching cubensis.

**Rattle those pots and pans!**
My morning energy is decent, actually. In cortisol saliva tests before it starts out at the bottom of the range and continues at or below the range until the afternoon and evening where it raises slightly into the middle of the range for that time of the day. It's been a positive change in the traditional pattern of my life where mornings sucked. Now when I get up, within minutes I'm wide awake and will actually do dishes and clean the carnage from the kitchen from the party-night before. I move around and will even go outside in my pajamas to check on things, like feeding the pooch. But within an hour I can feel the ache and fatigue creep back in.

**Sleep, ya little dummy.**
My sleep has never seemed to be an issue in my life, at least as far as sleeping soundly and dreaming freely goes. I rarely toss and turn, although some nights it takes me about an hour to fall asleep. I may get up to pee once if I've drunk a lot. When I start waking up, I can really feel it happening and don't feel too groggy like I have so many mornings in the decades of my life.

### Martin — 2016-02-09T19:11:15+0200

-   Maternal Haplotype info:
-   Haplogroup: U2e, a subgroup of U2
-   Age: 45,000 years
-   Region: Near East, India, Europe
-   Example Populations: Gujarati, Sindhi, Kalash
-   Highlight: The mitochondrial DNA of some of Europe's earliest inhabitants belonged to U2.

-   Paternal Haps:
-   Haplogroup: R1b1b2, a subgroup of R1b1
-   Age: 17,000 years
-   Region: Europe
-   Example Populations: Irish, Basques, British, French
-   Highlight: R1b1b2 is the most common haplogroup in western Europe, with distinct branches in specific regions.

### Martin — 2016-02-09T19:46:38+0200

A doc put me on vasopressin in 2012 to help with what he thought were mold issues and went by Anti-diuretic Hormone levels (ADH) **2** (1-13 pg/mL). I was splitting tablets and going very cautiously. I use a LOT of salt in many ways, but my BUN/Cre. ratio has been hovering between 12 and 15.

I think my problems are deeper and while _possibly_ influenced by mold, but I have few symptoms and labs to show me that it is the overarching cause.

Having said all that, I agree, it may do nothing for my Diaper Dan Syndrome. But increasing my hydration, perhaps in a more overall way, will.

Labcorp values, yes.

### Martin — 2016-02-13T19:30:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-188972>

**Altered World End Run**
Saw my local doc yesterday and we went for vasopressin hormone replacement. My sad Anti-diuretic Hormone levels (ADH) **2 (1-13 pg/mL)** and creeping-upward BUN/Cr ratio will be hacked for the next month or two.

**Blue Brew, who knew?**
Last week, I did **120 to 240 mcg** of methylene blue and felt slight increase in clarity and energy. Got some sun in AM and again at noon. Left out nico gum to isolate effects.

This week, did **4mg to 6mg (yes, mg's)** per day and my head didn't come flying off. Noticed a slight uptick in energy and clarity, slight head pressure _(the effects of a zillion mitochondria actually getting electrons?)_, and an increasing willingness to go do important things.

Today, I'm inviting Mr. Nick O'Teen to the Blue Room party. Did face dunks in 50 degree water from my well and DW suggested skin brushing my nakedness and I didn't blow her off cuz I have learned things about Skin being not just a sack for meat and coconuts.

Martin

### Martin — 2016-02-16T00:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-189131>

**Binding Goblins!**
Took 8 mg MB split into two doses, morn and afternoon. Working on SBGH (102.1 !) with Janz's suggestions and Doc's, too. My DHEA and IGF-1 should be in this week. Talked to my doc about light and vit. D and she was not aware of UVB lights. She's a big proponent of supplementing D and I'm working on showing her what's really going on.

**To Pee or not to Pee**
Took 1 mg desmopressin last night before bed and felt woozy while I lay there. Backing off to .5mg tonight. This stuff ain't cheap, but nothing but the best for me!

**No more Mr. Night Owl**
Slept like a rock. I awoke around 5am(?) to pee. I've stayed up late all might life. It all changed with the information I learned here. Had good dreams about weird friends I know. Dozed until my usual get-up get up time 7:20 am. Man, it's like clockwork! It's become a subject of amusement around my house. I noticed DW is snoring less since the Magnetico and after she finally got on board with blue blocker/amber lights in the evening. I worry about her chronic sinus drippage interfering with her REM sleep. Doc's blogs and webinars kick ass on why this is so friggin' important. I see her doing the VieLight some days.

Martin

### Martin — 2016-02-17T19:35:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-story.870/post-189253>

**Labs Jan. 2016**
HSCRP .20 mg/dL _Been low for a while now. Yay!_
Vit D **28.3** ng/mL _Crappy as can be. No supplementation this year, only daily sun exposure. Should have checked it in September for better picture._
PSA **3.58** 0.00 - 4.00 ng/mL
Free T4 **0.97** 0.59 - 1.17 ng/mL
Free Testosterone **10.2** 6-.6 - 18.1 pg/mL Let's bump that up, please.
Serum Testosterone **674** 348 - 1197 ng/dL
SHBG **102.1** 19.0 - 76.4 nmol/L _Good god. Up from around 86.0 from the last time. Looking for good info._
Estradiol **10.7** 7.6 - 42.6 pg/mL
Prolactin **4.5** 4.0 - 15.2 ng/mL I'm thinking melatonin release at night is keeping this down properly?
Free T3 **2.9** 2.0 - 4.0 pgmL

Got two more labs today:
DHEA **195.2** 48.9 - 344.2 ug/dL
IGF-1 **77** 49 - 188 ng/mL

**Breakfast on the Wing**
Did **5 mgs MB** first thing this morn before heading out to see what Mr. Sun had to say on this overcast cold morn. Watched a hawk swoop down and bag a hapless vole that was foraging in my field. MB so far has been very subtle. This week, adding nico will be interesting.

**Your face hurts, but it's killing me."**
Slept soundly. Continuing to have vascular irritation on my left temple and keep a slight headache constantly. Applied cold pack to temple and alternated that with my carotid artery to work on this damn tinnitus, aka, "huge swarm of crickets being ingested and spit out by a Pratt & Whitney J-57 jet engine". I'm overstating it a _bit_, but making shit up like this helps me forget the noise.

**Healthy Home Shopping**
Kids are looking at getting another house and asked us to come give our learned opinions... and bring my meters! The agent smiled as I opened my aluminum case and started pulling all sorts of gizmos out and waving them around. Unusually curious, he asked about what a gauss meter did, and had heard about living around "power lines" not being healthy. A quick primer had him listening attentively and that impressed me. House was really good except for around the refrigerator (duh) and right up on the electric panel box.

Re-checked lab sheet on my serum sodium levels after re-reading my vasopressin info. **142** MMoL/L 136 - 145 MMoL/L

# Thread: Do fat cells live and die based on diet/environment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-fat-cells-live-and-die-based-on-diet-environment.872/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-04-13T22:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-fat-cells-live-and-die-based-on-diet-environment.872/post-14851>

Creating a parallel thread here for MJ's AskJack question to honor the notion that only Jack should respond over there to keep things clear. However, I had a bit of info on this that is worth sharing....

> **MJ\*;14881 wrote:** Back in my days of Bio, I remember learning that fat cells were formed when we are babies and that throughout life we do not get more of them, we just expand the ones we have when we gain weight. Dr. Kruse in the quick start guide one the the benefits of CT is listed as permanent fat loss. I have a couple of questions on this...does CT destroy fat cells never to return again? Is it possible to destroy all the fat in your body with CT, or are we just swapping white for brown? If so, don't we need some white fat for regulatory purposes?
>
>
> It's no longer believed that fat cells only expand and contract. They are born and die as well. I believe that the rapid death (apoptosis) of fat cells actually causes a large immune reaction which is inflammatory, perhaps leading to the "low carb flu" and the "CT detox" effects (pure conjecture). OK onto the scienc-y stuff.
>
>
>
> From the Journal Of Nutritional Biochemistry: [http://missclasses.com/mp3s/Prize%20CD%202010/Previous%20years/Obesity/phytochemicals.pdf](http://http://missclasses.com/mp3s/Prize%20CD%202010/Previous%20years/Obesity/phytochemicals.pdf)
>
>
>
>
> > Adipocyte number increases not only as a result of increased preadipocyte proliferation but also due to differ-
> >
> > entiation \[102\]. Induction of differentiation stimulates clonal expansion resulting in doubling of the cell number
> >
> > > A number of studies have demonstrated that natural compounds like EGCG, genistein, esculetin, DHA, berber- ine, resveratrol, guggulsterone, CLA, capsaicin, baicalein and procyanidins inhibited adipogenesis
> > > ompounds that induce adipocyte apoptosis can reduce body fat content, and the effect has the potential to last much longer than body fat reduction caused by lipid mobilization alone. Apoptosis is a form of cell suicide that plays an important role in maintaining cellular homeostasis, and at times, it is necessary to eliminate excessive cells and cells that hinder development
> >
> > Green tea extracts \[17\], soy isoflavones \[26,27\], CLA \[130,136,137\] and garlic compounds \[18\] were shown to reduce body fat in experimental animals, but the mechanisms of action in inducing adipocyte apoptosis with these compounds has been investigated only recently. Although the effect of CLA on body fat is not completely understood, it is thought that a marked increase of TNFα mRNA with an increase of uncoupling protein 2 (UCP2) in adipocytes caused CLA- induced apoptosis \[137\]. In contrast, EGCG-induced apop- tosis is mediated by activator protein-1, nuclear factor kappa B and p53 \[138\] and increased caspase 3 activity \[139\].
>
> Genistein, EGCG and capsaicin stimulated intracellular ROS release, which activated AMPK rapidly leading to apoptosis

I found this by googling EGCG adipocyte apoptosis, there may be better results but I had heard about this effect of EGCG from Tim Ferris' 4-Hour Body book....

jesse

### chocolate — 2012-04-14T20:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-fat-cells-live-and-die-based-on-diet-environment.872/post-15100>

Now I'm wondering if taking that stuff will screw with the brown fat. You make me read.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-14T22:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-fat-cells-live-and-die-based-on-diet-environment.872/post-15127>

Good point chocolate. You're an aggressive googler so let us know what you find. I'm not taking EGCG but I took it for a while, not sure if it had any effect or not on my adipocyte apoptosis. I forgot to take roll count before and after

# Thread: Nutrigenomics... I find two different definitions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrigenomics-i-find-two-different-definitions.879/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-14T03:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrigenomics-i-find-two-different-definitions.879/post-14974>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nutrigenomics](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nutrigenomics)

Nutrigenomics is the study of the effects of foods and food constituents on gene expression. It is about how our DNA is transcribed into mRNA and then to proteins and provides a basis for understanding the biological activity of food components.\[1\] Nutrigenomics has also been described by the influence of genetic variation on nutrition by correlating gene expression or single-nucleotide polymorphisms with a nutrient's absorption, metabolism, elimination or biological effects. By doing so, nutrigenomics aims to develop rational means to optimise nutrition, with respect to the subject's genotype.

### chocolate — 2012-04-15T00:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrigenomics-i-find-two-different-definitions.879/post-15150>

[http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0013087](http://http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0013087)

So its not the fermented food that helps the insulin resistance, its the lacto . The micro flora themselves help with the insulin resistance. Its not just the absence of sugar.

# Thread: How do YOU relieve stress after one of THOSE days?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taryn.ourso@gmail.com — 2012-04-14T04:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-14986>

Just wanted to comment that sometimes after a very stressful day (like today), I seem to CRAVE ice on the drive home. It used to be that on a day like today I would cry into a bottle of wine and wake up a hot mess, but this time I fought the urge to buy the wine and just packed myself in ice when I got home. The cold really helps with my emotional stress!

What are some other optimally aligned ways that you alleviate stress besides the ice? I'm looking to build up a good arsenal. I'm a high school teacher and I foresee more of these days between now and May 31st!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-14T04:18:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-14987>

today was a pretty bad day in out OR......and I am playing a Pandora list that I never listen to.........cause a day like this never happens........except in nightmares.

### taryn.ourso@gmail.com — 2012-04-14T04:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-14988>

Sorry to hear about that! I'm sure a bad day in the OR is probably worse than a bad day at the high school! Let's just rock out, learn, and move forward!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-14T04:38:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-14991>

We had a suicide in surgery this AM. Not a good day at all. Walking into the hospital today was a surreal experience all day.

### taryn.ourso@gmail.com — 2012-04-14T05:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-14994>

Oh man. I can't think of much to say except that my thoughts are with all involved! In the end, the only actions we can really be responsible for are our own, even when our professions require us to care for the wellbeing of others. But things like this can always teach us SOMETHING if we are willing to reflect. If you really think about your own deepest thoughts, there is no way that anyone else in the world could 100% fully understand you. So we can never 100% fully understand the hows and whys of why people might choose to end their lives. Life does continue to move forward, and we must embrace that fact while honoring those who have passed on and learning from their journey.

### caroline — 2012-04-14T05:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-15000>

ohmigosh I am so sorry to hear that, the OR seems like it would be a stressful place in itself, I can't imagine something like that on top of it all.

### LinD — 2012-04-14T06:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-relieve-stress-after-one-of-those-days.880/post-15008>

So sorry, Dr. K. Very haunting/disturbing, indeed.

Sprint Samsung Epic

# Thread: Resveratrol absorbtion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-absorbtion.883/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2012-04-14T15:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-absorbtion.883/post-15035>

There has been some mention of which red wines contain the highest amount of resveratrol. I came across the following information in [Wikipedia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wine#Health_effects), that indicates that _how_ the wine is consumed is an even more important factor:

> To fully get the benefits of resveratrol in wines, it is recommended to sip slowly when drinking wines. Due to inactivation in the gut and liver, most of the resveratrol in imbibed red wine does not reach the blood circulation. However, when sipping slowly, absorption via the mucous membranes in the mouth can result in up to around 100 times the blood levels of resveratrol.

The Wikipedia entry references an article at Forbes.com that is no longer available, unfortunately.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-14T15:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-absorbtion.883/post-15037>

Nice. A good wine should be enjoyed anyway.

### Optimalbound — 2012-04-14T15:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-absorbtion.883/post-15041>

Absolutely, vkiernan. The same goes for chocolate, I suppose.

### Souldanzer — 2012-04-14T18:43:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-absorbtion.883/post-15074>

Wonder if that means I should take my resveratrol supplement sublingually. Tastes disgusting though.

# Thread: You will notice a change in your sweating pattern   . . . How so?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### August — 2012-04-14T17:28:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15060>

Dr Kruse says that one of the signs that you are becoming Leptin Sensitive again is that "You will notice a change in your sweating pattern".

Source: MY LEPTIN PRESCRIPTION [http://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](http://http://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/)

Please tell me what exactly you experienced re: change in sweat patterns . . . so that I might know what to look for.

Or maybe I ought to phase the question this way: What is typical for sweat patterns for someone who is not Leptin Sensitive? . . . And What is typical for sweat patterns for someone who is Leptin Resistance?

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-14T17:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15063>

From what I understand, leptin resistant people sweat a lot.... As you become sensitive you sweat less and different pattern. At least my observations and I could swear I've seen jack comment on it as well .. Your body works more efficiently and you tend to sweat AFTER exercise to cool not all the time. I have noticed this in myself.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-14T17:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15067>

LS people have total autonomic control over sweating.......that is a function of ACH and the parasympathetic nervous system which is controlled by cortisol outflows from the brain and begun in the brainstem close to the floor of the fourth ventricle. Leptin controls this entire process........the more LR the worse control you have over the proper sweating responses to environmental triggers.......

### AKMan — 2012-04-14T19:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15078>

My personal experience; I used to sweat profusely. Embarassingly so. After about a year of paleo and the leptin reset, I barely sweat at all. Used to be, when I'd work out on a piece of equipment, like treadmill or elliptical, it would be a spattered mess when I finished. Now, not a drop. I can workout at lunch and wear the same clothes the rest of the day.

I was worried that I'd broke something, but when I went to Florida a few weeks ago, I worked out in the hotel gym where it was about 80 degrees and humid--there it was! Sweat! So, it's definitely still in me.

If you are in a chronic hot/humid environment, sweat is normal with exertion. But if you sweat like a dog all the time with minimal exertion--not so normal, methinks.

### BJK77 — 2012-04-14T19:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15082>

This is reassuring to hear as I definitely don't sweat nearly as much as I used to and I've never been one to sweat a lot anyway. I read that it could be a sign of my body's inability to properly detox, but now I'm going to look at it as a positive and assume my leptin is finally resetting itself. After 11 weeks on the rest I would hope so anyway!

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-14T19:40:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15084>

AKman, that's it exactly...I did a 2-mile hike recently on VERY steep terrain (didn't think I'd make it up some hills...). I noticed I was barely sweating even though prior to the reset and CT, I'd sweat on a simple walk around the block.

But there I was, stopping to suck wind, feeling muscles burn I didn't know I had, in 82 sunny weather (not humid), and my armpits were barely damp after an hour, much less the profuse leg sweating (more crotch really!) I was used to getting from my half-marathon days (you of course won't catch me doing endurance sports again!!)

I still am 5'6" and 235 so it's not like I'm svelte. Just 7 weeks into the reset (25 lbs gone) and a couple weeks into Ct (dunks, spot icing, some 62-degree pool submersion, but no ice baths yet)

It's really liberating. I didn't even notice I wasn't sweating that much til I was on my way back down and saw a real heavy guy sweating through his shirt and realized I wasn't doing the same, and was amazed!!!

God bless Dr Kruse for sharing with us...!

### donkjellberg — 2012-04-15T10:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-will-notice-a-change-in-your-sweating-pattern-how-so.885/post-15262>

I don't sweat much but where I do, its is in my pits. Usually when I get nervous, stressed or hot. I don't notice sweating anywhere else.

# Thread: dry, dead skin on edge of lower lips, use CO, rest of skin perfect even in winter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-dead-skin-on-edge-of-lower-lips-use-co-rest-of-skin-perfect-even-in-winter.891/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thyme — 2012-04-14T23:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-dead-skin-on-edge-of-lower-lips-use-co-rest-of-skin-perfect-even-in-winter.891/post-15138>

I've just finished my first winter without moisturizer in my life. No dry skin, no itchy skin--nada. Unbelievable.

But the edges of my lower lips are still trouble. They don't bother me, but they are dry and flaky--my partner reminds me once a day, more or less, to scrape them with a toothbrush as they've turned a most unattractive color with whatever we've been eating or drinking. I use CO on them and my O-3 O-6 consumption has been as recommended for more than a year--and I don't have any other signs of possible O-6 overload--but the continued dry flakiness on this one bit of my body is driving me BATTY!!!!

help, please???

### Souldanzer — 2012-04-15T00:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-dead-skin-on-edge-of-lower-lips-use-co-rest-of-skin-perfect-even-in-winter.891/post-15149>

I have the same... my entire lower lip peels about once a day. No idea what's going on. Tried b complex but didn't help. No dehydration as well, I drink way too much fluids.

CO does nothing for me. The only thing that appears to be helpful is a beeswax lip balm my beekeeper friend gifted me with. It doesn't stop my lip from peeling but it makes it look a lot better.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-15T15:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin.895/post-15288>

Melatonin is not a good supplement. Why? Melatonin can affect prolactin levels. Go re read CT 7 now to see what i say about Prl!. Prl stimulates the breasts to make milk. It also effects thyroid hormone production bigtime, as well as estrogen and progesterone which is why I HATE it for menstruating females.......but I am OK with it in Menopausal females after I hack their labs. There are some studies that show long term (two months) use of melatonin are a problem

### kathylu — 2012-04-18T05:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melatonin.895/post-16124>

[http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1467-789X.2010.00756.x/abstract](http://http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.1467-789X.2010.00756.x/abstract)

"The physiology of BAT is readily regulated by melatonin, which not only increases recruitment of brown adipocytes but also elevates their metabolic activity in mammals"

I have to admit...I love melatonin, I have never slept so well...only take 1 mg at night, but it really helps!

Another cite on the BAT topic:

Obes Rev. 2011 Mar;12(3):167-88. doi: 10.1111/j.1467-789X.2010.00756.x.

Significance and application of melatonin in the regulation of brown adipose tissue metabolism: relation to human obesity.

Tan DX, Manchester LC, Fuentes-Broto L, Paredes SD, Reiter RJ.

Source

Department of Cellular and Structural Biology, the University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio, San Antonio, TX 78229, USA.

Abstract

A worldwide increase in the incidence of obesity indicates the unsuccessful battle against this disorder. Obesity and the associated health problems urgently require effective strategies of treatment. The new discovery that a substantial amount of functional brown adipose tissue (BAT) is retained in adult humans provides a potential target for treatment of human obesity. BAT is active metabolically and disposes of extra energy via generation of heat through uncoupling oxidative phosphorylation in mitochondria. The physiology of BAT is readily regulated by melatonin, which not only increases recruitment of brown adipocytes but also elevates their metabolic activity in mammals. It is speculated that the hypertrophic effect and functional activation of BAT induced by melatonin may likely apply to the human. Thus, melatonin, a naturally occurring substance with no reported toxicity, may serve as a novel approach for treatment of obesity. Conversely, because of the availability of artificial light sources, excessive light exposure after darkness onset in modern societies should be considered a potential contributory factor to human obesity as light at night dramatically reduces endogenous melatonin production. In the current article, the potential associations of melatonin, BAT, obesity and the medical implications are discussed.

Â© 2010 The Authors. obesity reviews Â© 2010 International Association for the Study of Obesity.

# Thread: leptin rx and carbs

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4

### sisterclare — 2012-04-15T07:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-and-carbs.898/post-15242>

I have not started the leptin rx yet as I am still getting used to the paleo diet for now. I noticed a note on facebook today from Jack saying that he has started eating carbs, and will continue until mid-July. I need to know if the carb thing happens from mid April until mid July no matter what climate you are in. I live in the high desert in southern california. Also, for someone just starting out with paleo, and about to do the leptin rx, does that mean that I should not omit carbs now that it is "carb season", and not worry about the leptin rx until August? I am confused here. I aim on doing well with the leptin rx before even attempting the ct. I also plan on getting my optimized blood tests and salivary tests done within the next two weeks. Thanks for any info you can give me!

### Chrisgraff — 2012-04-15T17:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-and-carbs.898/post-15304>

Dr. Kruse limited his carbs, (a la LeptinRx guidelines), for the first couple years of this diet. Now that he's leptin sensitive & cold adapted, he allows 10%-30% of his diet to be carbs _depending on the season_. He doesn't eat carbs during the cold seasons.

All that to say, the "carb-rules" he follows now are not for those of us getting started. ;-)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-15T17:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-and-carbs.898/post-15309>

Chriss is correct. It depends upon you current situation

### sisterclare — 2012-04-15T22:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-and-carbs.898/post-15374>

> **August;15318 wrote:** Would you mind saying if weight loss is one of your goals . . and perhaps how much weight you want to lose (only if you want to say) . . . that might be one factor to consider . . . re: consumption of carbs . . .

### BJK77 — 2012-04-15T18:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-dos-and-donts.899/post-15319>

I was hoping we could get a thread started explaining what is and isn't ok to eat. It gets confusing when you read about some having dairy (HWC, butter, etc), eggs, tomatoes, etc and then read elsewhere that those with AI/leaky gut shouldn't consume them.

Please chime in with any input.

As of now, I'm hoping the following is accurate:

**EAT

\- Respect circadian cycles and eat according to seasonality

\- Lots of good quality proteins (grass-fed meats, wild seafood)

\- Lots of good quality fats (grass-fed/pastured animal fats - lard, tallow), coconut oil

\- Bone broth made from grass-fed animals (to heal the gut)

\- Fermented vegetables and/or probiotics (to repopulate the gut with micro-flora)

**AVOID**

\- Grains, legumes, starchy veggies

\- High omega 6 nuts/seeds (few macadamia nuts may be ok)

\- Vegetable / nut / seed oils

\- Fruit (strictly when leptin resistant, selectively once leptin sensitive & cold adapted)

\*\*Note: Avocado is ok

\- Sweeteners

Nightshades**

\- Do not consume if HDL is

I can't answer if "too sweet" makes the reset take longer, but I can say for me it induces cravings so for now it's best to keep most of those things out of my diet. My meals are definitely boring and lack variety, BUT I'm finding progress and willing to deal with boring for now if I can see optimal in my future : )

> I was being super strict for so long and just snapped. I think my diet was too narrow with all the AI stuff thrown in. I need ideas for more variety that is on the plan. I've added in 99% or 100% cocoa chocolate and given myself more "permission" with coconut butter and milk. But trying to keep things not too sweet...
>
> Does too sweet (as in stevia, coconut manna) make the reset take longer?
>
>
>
> Just made a smoothie with an avocado this AM and some fruit and coconut manna with Upgraded Whey.

I feel your pain! I've been doing keto-paleo, following AI protocols, since October, and I've been feeling pretty down on my diet lately with no variety. When I get that way, I add in some kind of veggie to mix things up. I got in the jar of manna last night again, so it's officially out of the house. No more!

I do avocados on occasion...when I'm making paleo tacos or beef brisket (I season the meat with garlic and lime juice). Avos are a spring veggie, too, so you would be eating within season. Otherwise, I like mushrooms, asparagus and canned pumpkin. Too much sweet makes me want more sweets and carbs, so I've quit using stevia. Also, I dropped caffeine and chocolate to work on my adrenals...so really my vices are all but eliminated. But, I totally no what you mean about snapping! When in doubt, eat more fat. Put bacon on everything. Try new seasonings.

### Susan M. — 2012-04-28T23:05:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-dos-and-donts.899/post-19651>

> **ealachan;19206 wrote:** I eat a little bit of cheese...we're talking like, a couple servings a week, tops...on the reset. The only other dairy I have is Kerrygold butter, and maybe once a week or less I have yogurt. I don't know if that's "correct" or not, but that's what I've been doing. FWIW, I'm not doing AI or anything other than the basic reset along with CT.
>
>
>
> Some people (myself included) have mentioned that too much sweet stuff makes them crave more carbs, which can be a slippery slope.

I am one of those people that has to be very careful with the taste of sweetness. Once I found out that 99% cocoa was a go on the diet, I started eating World Market's brand of chocolate bar that has a hint of current in it. It is 99% cocoa liqueur, but it is soooo good and is actually sweet enough to the point that I find myself thinking on it way too much during the day and straying from plan. I know that it is supposed to help lower LDL, but I don't want to risk undoing all that I have accomplished so far.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-29T01:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-dos-and-donts.899/post-19685>

> **Susan M.;19724 wrote:** I am one of those people that has to be very careful with the taste of sweetness. Once I found out that 99% cocoa was a go on the diet, I started eating World Market's brand of chocolate bar that has a hint of current in it. It is 99% cocoa liqueur, but it is soooo good and is actually sweet enough to the point that I find myself thinking on it way too much during the day and straying from plan. I know that it is supposed to help lower LDL, but I don't want to risk undoing all that I have accomplished so far.

I have some concerns about accuracy in reporting on those damn bars. I usually go buy out the World Market every 2-3 weeks. My family goes through like 50 bars a month. That being said, the first 2 or three batches it was good, liked it, but didn't taste sweet. . .then we got a batch that tasted SUPER sweet, like 75% sweet. We started putting aside and collecting the "sweet" ones and did a taste test. I also had friends who have no idea the importance of this topic do some tasting.

I was left with little doubt that all their bars are not created equal. They clearly have no quality control and the nutritional info is not accurate. Some batches are SIGNIFICANTLY sweeter than others. I am 100% sure they are not all 99%.

>

> What is the time frame for not eating saturated fat with seafood, not in the same meal, not in the same day, ever?

From Brain Gut 6, under fats:

3\. When you eat non seafood protein this is when you should add your saturated fats to your diet to the greatest degree!! Grass fed red meat and offal come packed with saturated fats by nature and fish do not, by design as well as I laid out in the webinar. When I eat non seafood protein and saturated fats, I tend to also add sea vegetables to the meal. What are the Epi-paleo sea vegetables? That comes later in the blog. In the healthy human colon complex carbohydrates get converted to SCFA to increase our omega three content in our colon to diminish the risk of diverticulitis and colon cancer. This is only done when the gut microflora is functioning well. This can be re engineered by evolutionary dietary modifications to be covered in later blog in this series.

Does this help?

Marcia

# Thread: Mark Sisson on CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mark-sisson-on-ct.902/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-04-15T19:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mark-sisson-on-ct.902/post-15335>

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/cold-water-therapy/#axzz1s7vWHYg1](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/cold-water-therapy/#axzz1s7vWHYg1)

Read this, then check the date!

Excerpt:

The benefits of cold water therapy appear to depend on the subjectâ€™s adaptation over time. In other words, regular polar dips seem to enhance long term health, but a single cold burst in the shower wonâ€™t offer much beyond a good wake-up jolt. The power of cold water therapy, it seems, is in the habituation itself.

In studies comparing regular winter swimmers with subjects not adapted to cold immersion, winter swimmers showed an ability â€œto survive a significantly greater temperature gradient between body and environment than non-cold-adapted subjects.â€￾ Their advantage over the non-adapted subjects was a modification of the â€œsensory functions of hypothalamic thermoregulatory centres to lower heat loss and produce less heat during cold exposure.â€￾ The researchers concluded that regular winter swimmers show â€œmetabolic, hypothermic and insulativeâ€￾ kinds of adaptation to cold temperatures.

Read more: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/cold-water-therapy/#ixzz1s7xouIVo](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/cold-water-therapy/#ixzz1s7xouIVo)

### skline@peak.org — 2012-04-15T19:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mark-sisson-on-ct.902/post-15337>

Good for Mark.

But "hypothermic and insulative" doesn't seem to be what is happening in cold adaptation, from the experiences related here. Hyperthermic from burning a lot more fat seems to be what is happening.

# Thread: Warming up After CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pamela — 2012-04-15T19:50:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15341>

The actual CT makes me feel very good after the first 30 second plunge, I feel the coldness putting a calm over my body that really feels good, really gives me a sense that I am changing some sort of cellular structure because I can feel the "edge" being taken off my mood. The hard part is the hour or so after I am done, man am I cold. What is your experience with the after CT bath? Does the warm up get better over time?

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-15T21:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15369>

I think quite a few people found that if you stay naked after CT you'll warm up so much faster than if you put on clothes or cover yourself in bed.  For me it takes just about 10 minutes before my body return to normal color and I feel comfortable, then I put my clothes on.  It takes longer to warm up feet and hands, for that I use a little exercise - walk a mile, or do some leg lifts with ankle weights.

### primat — 2012-04-15T22:44:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15380>

I also find getting properly dry before dressing - and then walking a mile'ish - helps for me. This gets me to 40-45 min bath - 10 min to dry and a 15 min walk.

### donkjellberg — 2012-04-15T22:47:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15381>

I'm not in any hurry to warm up. I try to stay cooler longer when possible. After CT, I usually let my body dry through evaporation and when done, wear light clothing.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-16T00:51:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15406>

I drip dry......just did three hours in pool and im sundrying as we speak

### Pamela — 2012-04-16T03:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15466>

I did find that doing a little exercise helped not to shiver so I'll do all that you suggest. The weird thing is that now I'm feeling like I want to CT every day instead of every other day and the good feeling the cold bath gives me is starting to be really desirous. What's that all about?

### JC2K — 2012-04-16T23:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15714>

> **Pamela;15535 wrote:** I did find that doing a little exercise helped not to shiver so I'll do all that you suggest. The weird thing is that now I'm feeling like I want to CT every day instead of every other day and the good feeling the cold bath gives me is starting to be really desirous. What's that all about?

The cold helps release endorphins. They're causing the good feeling. Runners get it as "Runner's high". It can contribute to overexercising. Not sure on the CT side, but I would assume it can contribute to overdoing it there as well.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-04-16T23:58:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15726>

Usually I have to get dry and dressed right away after CT. I have found the couple of times I was able to hang out outside next to the pool after getting out, it has been easier. I started CT about 6 weeks ago, and just the last few times I've warmed up more quickly, so I'm inclined to believe that the warm up process does get faster.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-04-17T11:06:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warming-up-after-ct.903/post-15837>

I try to make sure that I can "drip dry" (not always possible when children are home), and then get dressed and take the dogs for a walk to warm up - it doesn't always seem to work but, oddly, the colder the bath the shorter the warm up time, which I seriously don't understand.

# Thread: I have golf carts that have onboard desulfators...human equivalent
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-golf-carts-that-have-onboard-desulfators-human-equivalent.904/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-15T21:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-have-golf-carts-that-have-onboard-desulfators-human-equivalent.904/post-15367>

So lets go with the body really should be pretty much a closed and self-tuning machine with outside fuel to feed its internal processes. Most stuff can get recycled, but we seem to be adding unnecessary food for the body to scrounge out nutrients and store the waste in fat. What are we doing that is making us need to replenish sulfur? At some point shouldn't we reasonably expect to reach saturation? shouldn't we have an onboard desulfator? the one thing I know from my doc is that we are carbon burning. Spent fuel is carbon dioxide and water. What's with all the turds? I know good dog foods' bragging rights is that its full of protein and less waste to clean up.

# Thread: pain relief, global or local?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-04-15T22:51:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-15384>

Had an interesting experience since I mildly sprained my ankle last tuesday. While doing CT \_with my feet out of the water\_ the pain in my ankle went away. It was gone for several hours.... I had always thought the pain reduction from ice therapy was because it numbed the location and temporarily decreased the inflammation on the joint. But maybe if you are becoming cold adapted then the pain is decreased globally instead of locally?

I can't discount what I felt, there was no numbing involved, it just didn't hurt for a while. I'm not judging his as necessarily a good thing either, just a thing.

jesse

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-16T00:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-15404>

Globally.....but it does follow a dermotermal pattern wired into the spinothalamic tract. I found this out jan 9th. I talk about it in the book

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-04-17T00:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-15727>

Can't wait to read all of the good stuff in the book! How much longer must we wait?!

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-19T00:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16360>

Right now I'm only getting relief in places that go into the cold water (joint aches and pains from heavy gardening and some arthritis in hands).

Will this change into a global relief as I become more adapted or does the type of/ cause of pain dictate this?

### hellojtm — 2012-04-19T01:02:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16368>

Hi PaleoMom,

You prompted me to post this as I was thinking about it anyway. For me it's gone global. I don't have to ice the area or even get it wet at all. If I run cold water on my chest for 5 minutes then the pain in my ankle goes away for a while. I'm taking this as a strong indication that I'm currently cold adapted.

jesse

p.s. I am thinking of testing this by just icing my neck or belly or lower back and see if the pain goes away, but the injury itself is healing now and in fact is not even bothering me right now... so I think that window has closed. Since I play in two soccer leagues I'm sure a new "opportunity" will arise soon...

### PaleoMom — 2012-04-19T02:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16401>

Jesse,

How long have you been at now? Just curious to compare when this switch is happening for people. I think I started March 12. I have lots of other signs of adaptation so far, but not this. Can't wait though!!

I am going deeper and deeper in the tub each time (I've got a stock tank now) so parts of me not being in the water is less of a problem, but it would still be nice to be able to read, yet my hands are the primary problem area. I've been putting them in for part of the time (about 10-15 minutes) and then read the rest of the time.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-19T04:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16458>

I pulled my calf muscle today. Icing it as we speak!

### hellojtm — 2012-04-19T04:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16459>

I started with mild exposure around first week of March. Face dunking, getting rained on and living in San Francisco with just shorts/t-shirt. My first cold bath was around 3/25 and then I did one nearly every day until a few days ago. So it didn't take long and I skipped the ice phase. However I went on a heat fast and had no external forms of heat other than food during that time.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-19T05:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16474>

I also went form face dunks to baths, skipping the ice, as I do not have much fat to lose. started at 65F and now down to 56-58F for 30 min.

### Birdy — 2012-04-19T07:46:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16516>

Around here, three weeks of cold showers (only 2 minutes) for general pain.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-19T20:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16656>

PaleoDentist,

If your calf is still strained can you try icing only your neck and shoulders or try face dunks or something else and see if it eliminates the pain.

Jesse

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-04-20T03:43:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relief-global-or-local.905/post-16851>

When I first started I couldn't really tell if there was a global impact, but now the inflammation recedes from my core to the tips of my fingers and toes. I can literally watch the wave of normal skin color push down my fingers and toes while I sit in cold water. As the inflammation comes back it follows the opposite pattern. I have RA so inflammation is easy to spot - red, hot, puffy joints. I get a similar effect just from icing my core, but that is not always as effective for the lower extremities.

# Thread: HCG/CT/FAT - what are you doing and what are your results

What are you eating?

Fat?

CT?

how often?

Results?

thanks!!!

### ChimpChick — 2012-04-16T05:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15508>

I am having awesome results with HCG, oil and CT.. I have none of the negative side effects those who are strictly HCG protocol plus CT are experiencing. Doc said CT requires fat, so I went with it.. Dr. S protocol has been SO beaten into our brains that we all seem afraid to take a chance.. Stabilizing has nothing to do with what you eat during P2.. It is how you live your life post HCG.. There have been days that all I have is CO oil (in various forms) I lose 3x the amount and have kept it off.. I am in p3 now and 2 lbs under LIW. The last half of my p2 I had CO, butter, I did a few fat fasts and mixed in a couple protocol days just for the dogma of it all.. Best part, unlike last year when I followed strict protocol and got where I am now, but sickly looking, I have not lost any hair.. Last year I lost 1/3 of my hair.. So I am all for fat + HCG..

You just gotta take a chance.. Doc has not failed us yet =)

### momto2 — 2012-04-16T07:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15518>

### MaggieC — 2012-04-16T09:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15531>

What type of HCG? I do injections rx, 175.

What are you eating? Mostly protocol, CO with cocoa and the dreaded artificial sweeteners and diet soda. CO doesn't seem to slow me down. I don't eat the fruit, traded it in for a protein breakfast, and no grissini. Occasionally I'll add 1/2 C raspberries. Some rounds I've done half and half in my coffee, not this round though.

Fat? Just what's in the meat, sometimes I saute celery in CO for a change.

CT? Not yet.

Results? Great first week, then my shots gave out. I didn't put it together until last night, and this morning I mixed a new batch. Back to no hunger, and I'm freezing again, so it's all good. Let's just pretend last week didn't happen! I lost 10 lbs first week, best first week ever. Now I'm making up for lost time.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-16T16:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15562>

We're not seeing that success with coconut oil with very many people, chimpchick.  Glad it's worked for you though. I never lost hair in any of my rounds that were strict protocol. I suspect your hormonal issues have been fixed by CT and it has nothing to do with the oil. And in the past what you ate on P2 DID affect stabilization. I've seen it zillions of times on the hcg forum. But hopefully CT is fixing that.

### ChimpChick — 2012-04-16T19:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15629>

Colleen,

Last year I did a perfect p2, even wore gloves anytime I touched oil.. My hormones were and are A-OK.. The hair loss was due to the unhealthy 500 cal diet. I also regained all the weight from the start of p3 on.. I believe it was do to lack of nutrients including fat and protein. My dose was perfect, I have never had hunger on any p2, I have no food sensitivities, all cleared by NAET so there is really no other explanation for it. So far I am between 2-3 lbs below LIW and I feel and look great.. If people are not stabilizing in P3 it has to be due to what they are eating in P3. I can't see how you could eat oil then 2 weeks later gain from it, it is not physically possible.

I am sure CT has helped my stabilization and because I followed CT protocol by eating fat before CT sessions (while in p2) I also experienced none of the negative side effects everyone who is doing HCG plus CT with Dr. S protocol seems to be having trouble with..This tells me CT shouldn't be done on such a restrictive diet..

It may not work for everyone, but it worked for me.. You never know unless you give it a whirl!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-16T20:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15649>

Even Doc is now saying coconut oil doesn't seem to work well for HCGers. You had your hormones checked including thyroid T3 and T4? It's very unusual to regain in P3 unless there are hormonal problems or food issues. I've never lost hair in any of my 5 rounds so it has nothing to do with the 500 calories. It is NOT an unhealthy diet when the dose is right. Everyone says I look younger and better than I have ever looked.

The hair loss is hormonal. Hcg elevates your thyroid and female hormones and then after you go off, the hair loss usually starts because the hormones plummet again.

And it is physically possible to gain on oil in P2 then not gain in P3. I'm a prime example.  I have seen thousands and thousands of hcgers on the other forum in 3 years and have seen this over and over.

But I'm glad it's worked for you--truly! It just doesn't work for everyone so it's dangerous to say it will work if you are brave enough to try it. I've been hearing from many who are reporting bad stalls and gains on coconut oil so they stopped using it. I wish we could get to the bottom of why it works for some and not for others. I WISH it worked for me. I hate giving up my cream when on hcg. That's the only hardship I have. LOL Nothing else bothers me at all.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-16T21:22:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15667>

It's very unusual to regain in P3 unless there are hormonal problems or food issues. I've never lost hair in any of my 5 rounds so it has nothing to do with the 500 calories. It is NOT an unhealthy diet when the dose is right. Everyone says I look younger and better than I have ever looked.
>
>
>
> The hair loss is hormonal. Hcg elevates your thyroid and female hormones and then after you go off, the hair loss usually starts because the hormones plummet again.
>
>
>
> And it is physically possible to gain on oil in P2 then not gain in P3. I'm a prime example.  I have seen thousands and thousands of hcgers on the other forum in 3 years and have seen this over and over.
>
>
>
> But I'm glad it's worked for you--truly! It just doesn't work for everyone so it's dangerous to say it will work if you are brave enough to try it. I've been hearing from many who are reporting bad stalls and gains on coconut oil so they stopped using it. I wish we could get to the bottom of why it works for some and not for others. I WISH it worked for me. I hate giving up my cream when on hcg. That's the only hardship I have. LOL Nothing else bothers me at all.

oh...HCG upregulates thyroid hormone? seriously? I didnt realize that...does it affect other hormones as well?

### ChimpChick — 2012-04-17T06:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15818>

> **colleencoble;15719 wrote:** Even Doc is now saying coconut oil doesn't seem to work well for HCGers. You had your hormones checked including thyroid T3 and T4? It's very unusual to regain in P3 unless there are hormonal problems or food issues. I've never lost hair in any of my 5 rounds so it has nothing to do with the 500 calories. It is NOT an unhealthy diet when the dose is right. Everyone says I look younger and better than I have ever looked.
>
>
>
> The hair loss is hormonal. Hcg elevates your thyroid and female hormones and then after you go off, the hair loss usually starts because the hormones plummet again.
>
>
>
> And it is physically possible to gain on oil in P2 then not gain in P3. I'm a prime example.  I have seen thousands and thousands of hcgers on the other forum in 3 years and have seen this over and over.
>
>
>
> But I'm glad it's worked for you--truly! It just doesn't work for everyone so it's dangerous to say it will work if you are brave enough to try it. I've been hearing from many who are reporting bad stalls and gains on coconut oil so they stopped using it. I wish we could get to the bottom of why it works for some and not for others. I WISH it worked for me. I hate giving up my cream when on hcg. That's the only hardship I have. LOL Nothing else bothers me at all.

Hey Colleen,

There are a ton of people over at low carb friends who are having fantastic results with fat + HCG.. I thought you were doing a round with fat included for Doc's data?

My hair loss started while still on P2, so it wasn't hormonal. My hormones (all of them) are fine. My hair loss was due to lack of protein and probably eating the required fruit out of season, which is not healthy.

It is impossible to eat oil in p2 and have it cause a gain 3 weeks later in p3.. It does not stay in you system that long. If I ate oil in p2 and lost more then the normal amount of weight, it will not cause me to gain weeks later.

I am not telling anyone to do anything dangerous. We are all 'guinea pigs' who are supposed to be trying out different approaches and reporting back to Doc our results. I said it works for me, may not work for everyone but you never know unless you try. If people chose to experiment that is their choice.

I left the 'huge HCG forum' because anyone who dared to think beyond 1950 was ridiculed and told they were wrong and/or a failure. I thought we were beyond that type of thinking/dogma/CW here in the land of N-1?

There are plenty of people on HCG who are having great results adding fat to their menu's. There are even more who want/wanted to try it but get scared off by something that was written in the time of the 'food pyramid' AND we all know how true that fairy tale turned out...

I know you are very knowledgeable about HCG and give great advice about strict protocol. I have always appreciated your helpfulness and wisdom. I do feel that over here in Kruseville, most want to go forward in our healing and we cannot do that if we live in the past and are unwilling to take a chance and change. Doc is a perfect example for all of us to follow..

### colleencoble — 2012-04-17T17:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15887>

actually Doc, no, most people keep it off. When someone doesn't, there is an underlying hormonal issue or food intolerance. I'm basing that not just on the thousands I've seen on the big forum but on the +70 that I've personally helped face to face here in my town. I can actually SEE that they've kept it off and not rely just on what I've been told. I have 2 here who have struggled out of that 70 and those folks have insulin issues and can't stay out of the carbs. They go on binges with carbs and that's pretty fatal when trying to lose weight in any manner.

I wish those who are having great results adding fat to the protocol would speak up here. I've been hearing from those who tried it then stopped when they began to gain even with adding CT. Quite honestly, I hope it IS true. But it's something I see on the big forum all the time. When something works for a few people, it's assumed it works for everyone and it just doesn't. I wish it did.  And I agree with everyone being willing to experiment. I'm just reporting that others of those who DID try the fats found it didn't work for them. We need all the information not just part of it if we are going to figure this out.

We can't be dogmatic in either direction. The fact is we just don't know yet. We've got three or so on here who have added fats and had great losses. Will they stabilize? I don't know. I do know that many who add fats in P2 without CT do NOT stabilize. The fat derails the pathway to the hypothalamus. I've seen it hundreds of times. I've heard from about 8 people who tried adding fats but then started gaining and stopped the fats to continue on the strict protocol + CT. Will they stabilize better? We don't know yet. Everything is theory right now.

And yes, I was going to try a round with fat + CT but my round got delayed when my aunt died and I had to fly back to Phoenix. Then the reports started coming back to me about the problems so I began to rethink that. Especially considering what I know for sure about fat and hcg. I could still try it and just drop the fats if I start gaining as I expect I will. My body is VERY sensitive to fats in hcg. I think part of what is the difference here is that the hcg protocol was designed for obesity. Not for an extra 30 pounds but for those of us who have truly struggled with obesity. Pounds and Inches defines what that looks like and it's not an extra 30 pounds someone could lose with low carb. They are two very different things. And the strict protocol works for those of us like that. Many are jumping on the hcg bandwagon who don't really need hcg.

### MartiD — 2012-04-17T18:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15906>

Believe me, I'm happy, althought when looking at my progress over last 3 months it has slowed way down. Most of that 14 lbs was lost in first 3 months after last round with LRX. I am only down about another 2-3 since doing CT. My original goal was was 15 lbs higher than I am now! My "ideal weight" for my height is 20 lighter although I don't want to get to a weight I struggle to maintain either. I'd rather find a happy medium! I think that is 10 lbs lighter than I am now...just not sure how to get that 10 lbs off since I seem to have stalled! Ugh...decisions!

I am really looking forward to our pool opening this Summer and getting lots of swimming laps in since it is a good, non-stress workout. Plus, our pool will be really cold for first month! In past I would never get in until late June, now I can't wait for the first, really cold water day!

### Knittycat — 2012-04-17T20:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-15940>

Hi all,

I'm joining the guinea pig club, loading for my HCG round 5 today. I was a frequent reader and occasional poster at the "big hcg forum". Nice to see a group over here at Kruseville.

I have been doing the leptin reset since November, hoping I would be one of the ones who could get through the reset and start losing weight. It looks like that will not be the case for me, although I feel fantastic on the reset and I can see that it will be the way for me to maintain my weight. Sadly, I start this round at the same weight as I started my first round, due to several week long carb binges which brought my weight back up at an alarming rate. This is totally my fault, so maybe I would lose weight in the reset if I could get past my carb binges. For now though, I'm tired of feeling like crap from being fat... So back to hcg.

Amazingly, I have no desire to binge on sugar during my load days this time. Prior loads were full of junk food and ice cream.... Last night I started to eat ice cream and realized I just don't want it. This is HUGE progress for me. I think the leptin reset and CT are helping me recover from sugar addiction. I'm so grateful!

I'm still trying to decide how to incorporate CT into my round this time. Looking forward to hearing how you all are doing.

Rachael

\---

I am here: [http://tapatalk.com/map.php?fok4ag](http://http://tapatalk.com/map.php?fok4ag)

Thanks Doc, I will keep you posted. I hope others who are having the same success do as well. Once the reports of HCGers having negative reactions to adding CT -while following strict HCG protocol- started coming in, I knew you were on to something. I added fat, had no bad CT reactions while others who did not add fat did.

The thing with forums is that you tend to only hear from those who have succeeded. Those who don't seem to no longer post. I find the Low Carb Friends site much more informative and accepting of people who do not do everything by an ancient book. While I have only been a 'lurker' over there (I only have time for you) I haven't seen as much opposition to trying new and different things. Not to mention the level of support the members have for each other is amazing and refreshing. You should check it out. It also seems like they don't know about you and your discovery's over there, I am sure if they did they would be all over it!

On a forum we tend more to see the problems. Those who have struggles are usually the ones posting. And everyone falls off when they are done losing weight. I get emails though so that helps. If you read a forum, you'd think that everyone is having problems. LOL The reality is that most don't. We just hear from the ones who have a wrong dose or have encountered a problem of some kind. Which is why we have 800 posters at any one time. the bigger number (didn't know it was 600k now. I haven't looked in a while) is the TOTAL who have joined the forum in all this time. The CO worked great for me but I am not sure what the rules are on the type of fat required for CT. I guess I would start out slow, with a small amount and see how you do. ### colleencoble — 2012-04-19T05:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-16462>

It's actually 2 days of loading per Dr. S but 3 won't hurt you.  I see better stability with 30 days or more. Some stabilize fine on 23 days but if someone is going to struggle, it's most often those on a short round. So I usually recommend 30 days and just staying on the hcg and upping your calories so you stop losing then riding it out to the 30 days. 28 days is close.

And yes you could have your omelet on Mother's Day.

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-04-21T19:09:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-17461>

> **Fara;17359 wrote:** Colleen,
>
> What do you recommend for loose/saggy skin. My sister has lost 75 pounds and needs a rebound. any suggestions? Thank you

Here's the Dr. K quotes I've saved about skin:

After my patients lose their weight we use a few things to tighten skin over about 12 months…..high dose Vit K2 and teprenone (Oral and topical), caprylic acid (oral and topical) and high dose dietary palm and coconut oil. It does amazing things to adipocytes and the skin once the weight is gone.

I also add a couple of high dose supplements too….NAC and R-lipoic acid and resveratrol. This is not for a leptin reset…….this is when you are looking to lose the last twenty pounds after your weight loss.

If there is a lot of cellulite marks I also ask patients to consider high dose super curcumin as well

Why wrap? When someone is morbidly obese they have two major problems when they successfully lose weight. Obesity causes a stretching of the skin to cover the adipocyte mass but the second point is more problematic. Obesity causes adipocyte cell division. To effectively recover from the effects of obesity requires two major things. For the skin high doses of coconut oil to stimulate continued fat burning with a normal weight to force replacement of excess PUFA incorporated in the skin with saturated fats. Vitamin K2 is also a co factor in skin metabolism and skin tightening via teprenone conversion. This takes 18-36 months in my own experience. The second problem of excess adipocytes is the more difficult problem to solve and this is where wrapping plays a major role. In a normal reversal of excess weight to normal as would be seen in seasonal weight gain, when the animal gained its weight in the summer months with high carbohydrate feeds it would have been negated by fat burning done in winter with low food levels and poor light. Fat cells shrink then because being LS in winter not only burn the fat in those cells but it cause adipocyte apoptosis. In humans we have a biologic novelty. We stay obese over multiple seasons and often years chronically being leptin resistant and sustaining massive levels of inflammation. We do not see normal season changes. This biologic divorce over extended time cause heightened inflammation to persist in skin not allowing it to go back. This is clinically seen in stretch marks and spider angiomas. This is why turmeric and resveratrol are highly useful. It also is helped by the K2 and caprylic acid of coconut oil because leptin apoptosis is not affective in most cases of obesity. One place it is working well is in women who are fit who get pregnant. They rarely get long lasting stretch marks and shred their pregnancy fat quick. In the obese the reason the wraps work is that it places compression on the skin and the subcutaneous fat to decrease blodd flow of both the skin and the fat. This effect is hormetic and without proper leptin functioning to reduce fat cells the compression helps reduce fat cell numbers when strong antiinflammatories (resveratrol turmeric and caprylic acid trepenone) are used in combination. Resveratrol also augments adipocyte apoptosis via mTor pathways and is especially important. That is why it works.

### zoebarry@yahoo.com — 2012-04-22T21:28:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-17847>

No this is not my first round.....Unfortunately, I've done HGC at least 5 times before and kept gaining it back!!!

A year ago I went to the doctor and started BHRT. I found out about Dr. K through MDA and did the Leptin Reset even though my Leptin test said it was fine. I felt better.

After LR, I did a round of HCG for 3weeks and decided to stabilize and proceed with eating Paleo/LR style. But, I was still having cream in my morning coffee and occasionally some cheese. Fast forward six months, it seem like I was losing inches off my thighs, but my waist was getting bigger. I hadn't weighed myself during those six months because I wanted the freedom of not being obsessed with weight and just wanted to enjoy how I felt.

WELL! I stepped on the scale two weeks ago and found that I had gained over 12lbs!! At this time I learned about CT and HCG combo and how effective it was, so I decided to try it. I would like to lose 30lbs by the middle of July.

Also, when I was having cream in my coffee, I finally connected the dots that I would wake up with a stuffy nose every morning and/or would have to blow my nose during the night after I got up to go pee. When I stared this round of HCG, I replaced the cream with butter/coconut oil and I no longer have to blow my nose in the middle of the night.... A lesson finally learned!!

### sums-sums — 2012-04-24T17:13:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-18363>

Just wanted to say Hi again, and not sure where to post as there are so many good threads going on this board.

I am starting my first load day tomorrow. I recently read where Colleen & some others were loading in a Paleo way. I wanted to chime in that this will be my 3rd round and it is the first time I will be loading without 'sugary/starchy crap.' OK, maybe some pizza one night for dinner, but I plan on Bacon/eggs/cheese for breaky, coffee w/ HWC, salmon/oilve oil veggies cooked in butter for lunch, homemade cheese/bacon burgers for dinner (no roll), avacodo's for snacks and my favorite homemade lowcarb SF cheesecake. I have been LCHF/Paleo for almost 1 yr so I feel confident that my fat reserves are good and I don't have the desire to go on a binge like I have in the past. So thank you to the board for giving me the idea that I don't 'have to' make myself sick from food for two days. And I think as someone else mentioned, it is a big deal that I have come to this place about food. I think it is really cool that I am not sitting in front of the fridge tonight crying over my limited menu for the next 43 days

Also, I just wanted to say thank you to everyone who is experimenting with the HCG diet and posting about it. I am too chicken to do add fats and I decided to drop the fruit & melba and add in the 100 calories to protein instead. So tomorrow I will be weighing/bagging & freezing my meats. I will be figuring out the calorie breakdown on fitday.

Should be interesting to see how load days go & see how I do with the added protein.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-30T15:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-ct-fat-what-are-you-doing-and-what-are-your-results.908/post-20046>

Another goose egg for me. Sigh. Not sure why I'm even continuing on this way. There's science and then there's plain stupidity. LOL I think I've crossed over into the plain stupid realm. It's very clear that fat is not working with hcg. There will always be exceptions. Just like on the big forum, there is a rogue thread and some younger folks who could lose just about any old way they want to can cheat and still lose okay. But for those of us who fit the Pounds and Inches criteria, fat simply doesn't work with it, even with the addition of CT.

I had someone else ask if I was sure my hcg is good and yes it is. I have the normal effects: very "up" mood, and on day 4 I got the head pressure which is my usual signal while on hcg to lower my female hormones. Just to be sure I remixed on Friday too so it's not the hcg. It's either the fat or it's the CT itself that has nullified the effects of hcg.

Hey Colleen,

What is your level of 'mad scientist' at this point? Many many people (including me) have had huge drops (5-7lbs) doing the Atkins fat fast for 3-4 days while on HCG.. It is 1000 cals and as big a % of fat you can get in. The more the better but above 80-85% is key. It is not hard, although the amount of food isn't huge- what you can eat is very filling. Mac nuts are the best, I think if you can do a day with just those it is like 95% fat. At this point you have already added fat so any of that 'messing up the resetting of the hypothalamus' has already happened, at least you could get a woosh so this round isn't a total bust-- and still be able to help out Doc on the HCG+fat+CT frontier
