### Rick — 2012-04-30T18:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20094>

Hi Dr. K, here is the first food pic, guacamole in the stone mortar, no peppers or tomatoes but with roasted garlic, lots of cilantro and deflamed (washed) white onion. Other herbs/spice options are roasted ground cumin seed, Mexican oregano or ground dried hoja santa...The dippers are jicama sticks.

Attached files [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/20167=168-Guacamole.jpg)

### Rick — 2012-04-30T20:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20143>

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/20216=169-Guacamole.jpg)

Hello Dr. K here is a pic of guacamole in the stone mortar with jicama dippers...

### Rick — 2012-05-01T16:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20387>

> **Jack Kruse;15772 wrote:** Hi Rick......Post some pics!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/20460=172-Guacamole2.jpg)
[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/20460=173-Guacamole3.jpg)

Hi Dr K and all, here is my guac recipe:

3 firm ripe avocados

4 cloves dry roasted garlic

small handful of cilantro stems

cilantro leaves, washed dried and chopped

1 medium white onion, medium dice, washed 3 x's in cold water and drained

coarse sea salt

Grind the salt, garlic and cilantro stems in the stone mortar to a paste, split, pit and spoon the avocados into the mortar and mash, fold in the chopped cilantro leaves and de-flamed onion, check for seasoning and garnish with more chopped cilantro and white onion on top. Serve with jicama sticks for dipping. Other herb/spice options; toasted ground cumin seed, crushed Mexican oregano or ground dried hoja santa...

### Guest — 2012-05-01T17:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20400>

Nice presentation, Rick.

Going to make some this week. Never de-flamed an onion before but will when i make this. I love guacamole on hamburgers with sauted onions on top.

### Rick — 2012-05-01T18:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20434>

Hi Suhenna, thanks so much and yes I de-flame onions always for raw use because it removes the acrid compounds that cause onions to repeat on most everyone...

I totally concur with the guac on hamburgers, an awesome combo, enjoy!

### Rick — 2012-05-02T14:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.927/post-20633>

Hey Suhenna! I should clarify, when u wash the diced onion, use your fingers to break up the layers between each of the 3 washings so the water can get in between the onion membranes. Then drain them well and use a salad spinner to remove any remaining water...

# Thread: CT for domestic animals
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-domestic-animals.928/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mei — 2012-04-16T21:49:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-domestic-animals.928/post-15677>

I've got a few creatures near and dear to my heart. As they've taught me most of what I know, I started thinking maybe they could explain CT to me a little better. The ones I've got are mammals, after all...

Most animals are closer to cold adapted than we, their human compatriots. However, obviously living in a heated home, eating lame pet food and being kept from natural rhythms correspond with health issues in pets. While I've noticed that my dog begs to go outside simply to lie flat in the cold grass, I haven't implemented any cold protocol with him or the cats. They're on dog/cat-paleo (raw meat!), though, and Francis Augustus the Cat supplements with a rat or two every day. I also (hehehe) kind of started the dog (he's old and slowing down) on the Leptin Rx, in as much as I have started feeding him a big ol' breakfast early in the morning, no lunch, and a small dinner. He seems to be diggin it.

One of my horses, Amalia, who has worn a blanket (non horse people read: horse coat) every winter due to projected and/or complicated health issues has clued me in that even outdoor pets can benefit from cold thermogenesis. While the other horse, Sparrow, is happy as a clam, healthy as a - well, a horse - and spent the winter shivering his naked butt off, Amalia suffers from periodic laminitis (a foot condition caused by/indicative of insulin and other metabolic issues, brought on by - you guessed it - too much sugar!), has difficulty gaining weight, and is alternately lovely and sweet and a total dragon depending on, I'm guessing, her hormonal activity and/or balance/imbalance. What if she's less cold adapted than Sparrow, which denies her the healing benefits of the cold?

Amalia has an inch and a half long hole in her left front hoof wall, left over from an abscess that blew last year, which only started to trouble her now. In the past couple weeks, she's also had tender feet, an absolutely ferocious attitude, and a weird inflammation in her shoulder. That was my clue (key word: inflammation!) to trace this episode back to the barn manager's comment: "Oh, I wanted to put more weight on her so I gave her Hi-Fat and Cob!" NOOO!! 1. horses don't have gallbladders. They can't digest fat, their bodies don't know what to do with it and turn it into a toxin. 2. Cob = grain (oats, corn, etc) coated in molasses. YIKES. Crash course in paleo for horses: they create all the energy they need from fermenting indigestible fibre in their enormous hind gut. They need low, low sugar forage, small and frequent portions, and should walk 10 - 50 miles a day at a slow pace (think living in mountains or deserts in extreme conditions and how extremely efficient you would need to be, especially as a herbivore). So pumping her with sugar and fat sent her body into failure and inflammation...

Long story short? I became obsessed with CTing my horse... only one session thus far but I remembered a common treatment for lameness issues is to soak the foot - maybe many of the benefits come from unknown CT effects! So I filled a bucket full of cold water, and put her foot in it. The end. She loved it, stood there all relaxed and happy like she knew full well what I was up to - and normally, she won't put her toes in any amount of water.

I will update if I notice any more improvements with further treatment.

Meanwhile, anybody else think their household companions might be wonderful and willing experimental subjects? If so, how far would you take it, so long as they showed no signs of distress? I mean, let's be quiet about it so as not to alert PETA (I'm sure they'd object to ice baths for shi-tzus or whatever), but if I'm going to be optimum, I think my long-suffering animal friends might as well be, too.

### TerrierMom — 2012-04-17T03:00:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-domestic-animals.928/post-15780>

My poor 10 year old cairn terrier needs any help she can get. Years of "allergies" treated with the occasional corticosteroids and that's not a road I like to go down!!

Seriously considering starting her on GAPS and paleo diet along with some ct and repopulating the gut flora her meds have caused. Anyone else do this?

### Marie — 2012-04-17T04:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-domestic-animals.928/post-15806>

(Edited to add) For Krusing...

I've been a Raw feeder for 9 years, and a Prey Model feeder for 7. I'd suggest that you search Prey Model Raw and get going. It is very simple, once you get the hang of it and I spend less than $30/mo to feed my 65lb dog high quality meats and supplements.

Find a local feeders co-op, supplement with lots of Salmon oil and GreenTripe.com products. You'll be amazed at the difference in your dog, how he/she smells especially, and how healthy they become. Animals, like humans, bloom on an evolutionary diet. It is best to get a mentor to help you sort through the beginning stages as it is a bit like having a baby and having a lot of questions...then it all becomes common sense.

(And btw, for all the cat owners, cats do extremely well on raw as they are a pure carnivore and do not have the same forgiving physiology that your dog has...)

Check out DogtorJ's website ([http://dogtorj.com/)](http://http://dogtorj.com/\)) as he is a member here and has been preaching the evolutionary path for a long, long time. He's a bit wordy, so it is best to print out his essays and highlight them

### Mei — 2012-04-17T06:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-domestic-animals.928/post-15826>

yeah, like we're not used to wordy doctors around here...

# Thread: CT, leptin reset and breastfeeding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-04-16T22:13:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15684>

I'm still nursing my 16 month old pretty regularly. He nurses at night and a few times during the day, and for comfort if he hurts himself, etc. I started CT about 5 days ago and I really love it, I'm practically addicted now, but I'm starting to wonder if "detox" means all that junk is going in to my breastmilk. Should I hold off until I wean him? I really don't want to, since I plan on letting him self wean (which could mean 1/2 a year to two more years of nursing.) Are there any ladies out there doing all this stuff and nursing their little ones? How does this change your LR diet if at all? Are you doing CT? I have always loved cold showers so I just dove in and did full body in an icy tub right off the bat.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-16T22:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15688>

I'm in the process of trying to wean my now 2yo. I've been working on this since Feb! I've been CTing since Feb. granted he's only nursing 2x/day at most. I know there's toxin dumps, and Dr K indicated you shouldn't CT if you're nursing a baby. I really would have thought the BHRT I'm on would have dropped my supply - but nope.

you might just want to spot CT, or limit the # of CT bath sessions you do; alot depends on where your heath is in this process...

as for diet - I'm KETO. yes I'll occasionally have some berries - and when I say some - its about 1/4c no more. they've become too sweet for me these days, and I'll be honest... I'm looking for fat to eat. there have been times I've seriously considered opening my duck, or bacon fat and eating a spoonful! I do it w/CO and Ghee....why not fat? I've been primal/paleo since ds was 6mths old...so no real issues w/eating.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-16T23:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15719>

I would guess it has to do with how much detoxing you have to do. And if you take it slowly, you will be able to manage the rate of detoxing too.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-04-17T00:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15729>

I'm nursing my twin 10 month olds. They eat very little food and are still almost exclusively breastfed. I haven't noticed any problems or red flags.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-17T00:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15733>

Been nursing for 14 years nonstop, and expect (hope) quite a few more. I always take "not while nursing" with a grain of salt, b/c nursing is (and biologically, is intended to be) a lifestyle, not a short phase, for me.

So long story short, I agree that you can manage the pace of die-off, and on top of that, you can help yourself and your wee one detox with epsom salt baths and/or activated charcoal. I try to go slow (no 40 degree baths for me, no sudden dietary changes (go slow)), and if signs show up in my nursling (now 28 months old), give her nice warm baths full of epsom salts or ACV or sea salt or baking soda.

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-17T02:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-15772>

I've been eating pretty clean and lowish to moderate carb (25-100) for over a year so I don't think I should have any die off issues. I was more considering the hormonal dump but if you ladies aren't having issues I'll keep going.

### Nourish — 2012-05-07T18:43:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-21947>

I'm in a similar situation to you Beingvenus....I am still nursing my 16 mo old and concerned about starting CT. I've only done spot CT so far to try to minimize the detox. Any fat soluble toxins (and hormones) will be released as fat is burned and pass through breastmilk, so I was concerned about exposing my little one to those toxins when he is still growing and developing so fast. My understanding is that the cells are more sensitive to damage from toxins during the time when kids are growing and developing so fast....mainly under 5 yoa and esp during pregnancy/infancy? It can influence their genetics/epigenetics. But I've already lost so much with breastfeeding alone that maybe its not worth worrying about the extra release from CT? Maybe I should just focus on getting him to eat foods that support his own detox....

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-07T20:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-leptin-reset-and-breastfeeding.929/post-21977>

> **Nourish;22024 wrote:** I'm in a similar situation to you Beingvenus....I am still nursing my 16 mo old and concerned about starting CT. I've only done spot CT so far to try to minimize the detox. Any fat soluble toxins (and hormones) will be released as fat is burned and pass through breastmilk, so I was concerned about exposing my little one to those toxins when he is still growing and developing so fast. My understanding is that the cells are more sensitive to damage from toxins during the time when kids are growing and developing so fast....mainly under 5 yoa and esp during pregnancy/infancy? It can influence their genetics/epigenetics. But I've already lost so much with breastfeeding alone that maybe its not worth worrying about the extra release from CT? Maybe I should just focus on getting him to eat foods that support his own detox....

follow what MamaG does - she's been nursing or pregnant for quite a while and has hit several different rounds of detox depending on what she's been doing... go slow... back off if you feel bad.

W/my ds - he's only nursing 2x/day anyway - I'll be honest I'm not really sure how much milk I'm making these days or if he's getting any. let your little one keep nursing, have him eat the foods you eat; and keep your CT slow - you should be fine.

# Thread: Please Hack some High Markers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-some-high-markers.930/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pamela — 2012-04-16T23:19:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-some-high-markers.930/post-15710>

Hi,

I had some blood work done and was wondering if anyone knows what these are as they are showing high out of range:

BUN 30 range mg/dL 6-24

BUN/Creatinine Ratio 34 range 9-23

Phosphorus, Serum 4.6 mg/dL range 2.5-4.5

TSH 5.0 uIU/mL range 0.450-4.500

currently on 30mg Armour, probably need more. I did not get the full thyroid panel in this workup.

### mambo — 2012-04-29T21:42:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-some-high-markers.930/post-19905>

Your BUN is high, sign of either dehydration at the time of testing or protein overload. Your TSH is also high, sign of hypo. But you need other #s to put these out of range #s in context. They don't mean much isolated from the rest of your metabolic and blood count panels.

# Thread: What's your Temp?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-04-17T00:10:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-15730>

Ok...my water temp comes out ~45-55degrees depending on the temp out side.

So I've noticed my body temp is increasing as well. I'm running HOTTER. I used to run btwn 96.6-97.6 and now I'm running towards 98.9-99.5 range; but I'm not running a fever.

So today before my CT I took my temp - FWIW its freaking 88 degrees out right, my body was running hot but my actual temp was 96.6, even though it's hot out. Got into the tub and felt immediately cooler! I took my temp just before I got out of the tub - temp 99.5! no shivering at all... what is going on????

anyone else experiencing something similar? the kids keep telling me I' too hot, and they don't want to cuddle b/c I'm so hot....

### Kpcst — 2012-04-17T00:22:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-15732>

Isn't that what is supposed to happen. Welcome... You are cold adapted now.

### Jim — 2012-04-17T00:41:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-15735>

Sounds like BAT is in overdrive.

[http://www.amazon.com/s/?ie=UTF8&keywords=exogen+thermometer&tag=googhydr-20&index=aps&hvadid=13445108610&hvpos=1t1&hvexid=&hvnetw=g&hvrand=19393952892037714114&hvpone=&hvptwo=&hvqmt=e&ref=pd\_sl\_2kexb6qy4b\_e](http://http://www.amazon.com/s/?ie=UTF8&keywords=exogen+thermometer&tag=googhydr-20&index=aps&hvadid=13445108610&hvpos=1t1&hvexid=&hvnetw=g&hvrand=19393952892037714114&hvpone=&hvptwo=&hvqmt=e&ref=pd_sl_2kexb6qy4b_e)

>
>

> PaleoDentist;16247 wrote:** shijin, how is your body comp now? any progress since dec?

as for body comp - I can now fit in 3 pairs of my per-pregnancy jeans & most of my per-pregnancy business suits (they're actually starting to get big - fortunately since I work from home, and I don't have to go to client sites - I don't have to put them on) - I've got my real skinny jeans up in the attic - my size 5/6 calvin klein button fly boot cut jeans that I wore when I lived on Guam, and I was my skinniest/strongest (these are the goal!) I still have a bit of a tummy/love handles - but things are changing I've definitely have more squishy fat, also I'm discovering I can eat less and not be hungry - yesterday I had 3 eggs, BPC for HAB, and for lunch/dinner I had 1 beef short rib -

### Inger — 2012-04-18T19:19:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-16237>

I measured my temps today, for that purpose I did 3 CT sessions ususally I do one or two...

So here is what I did and what my temps (all temps taken rectally) was;

After BAB,

10 min. shower, water was 41 degree F, I did 10 min. alltogehter. 2 min. for each leg, 2 min. for each arm and 2 min. whole body.

Temp before, 98,94 F

Immidiately after the shower 98,92

\+ 15 minutes 99,01

\+ 15 minutes 98,47

\+ 60 minutes 98,34

After a 30 minutes slow walk outside my temp was 99,80 F.

Next session was with empty stomach about 2 PM in my barrel. I submerged fully, just left my head out of the water.

The water-temp was 40,1 degrees F. I sat in there for 5 minutes.

Right before my temp was 99,80 F.

Right after, 99,06

+15 min. 98,13

\+ 15 min. 97,89

\+ 60 min. 97,88

Then, after Dinner (I had more protein and fat for breakfast maybe that is why my temp dropped more this time?) at 4 PM I got into the barrel again as before, but stayed for 6 minutes because the water was warmer, 42,8 degrees F.

My temps was;

Right before, 99,21

Right after, 98,60

\+ 15 min. 98,07

\+ 15 min. 97,88

\+ 60 min. 99,60 (this was after a 30 minutes long slow walk outside again..)

It looks like eating a big meal before really helps the body not drop the temps too far!

I guess I have no risk for hypothermia.. haha.. But fun to check the temps like this! Now I will check them regularely to see if there happends any changes!

I guess I am far from coldadapted yet.. as my temp do fall a lot after. It should rise I guess.. so I have to be patient!

### Larry — 2012-04-19T04:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-16452>

Just did 40 minutes in a 54F degree tub. All temperatures were taken with an oral thermometer. Pre CT temp. was 96.4; after 10 minutes of sitting up while legs were immersed & water up to my navel, my temp was 97.8; the next 18 minutes I leaned back into the water with my legs half out and my temp was 99.2; did the last 12 minutes sitting up again with an exit temp of 99.1. What this all means I honestly don't know but I think it is a good thing that my body is reacting positively to the cold. I am happy!

Pre - 96.4

10 min - 97.8

28 min - 99.2

40 min - 99.1

### Inger — 2012-04-19T08:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-16522>

Oh Larry, you happy guy! You are totally adapted as it looks like...!

I hate that I am not warming up like you... patince.. patience...

Why I have the feeling that guys have such a easier time CT-adapting..? I am not afraid to jump in icy water, I just hate that it takes so long for me to adapt! Strange enough, all BAT studies I have read are done on men too. Whats up with women.. Is it the fact that we have much less muscles naturally, that makes the heating harder? But I thought it was the BAT.. maybe men have a lot more of it naturally. Thats why women are always easily cold.. on average at least.

I wonder if I need to drop the coffee... I do drink one cup / day..

The doc says, it slows down adaption..

### Larry — 2012-04-19T13:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-16531>

Inger, you are too kind & I hope you are correct about me being adapted. I don't know what to tell you about the coffee but I can tell you that I drink quite a bit of iced tea everyday. Also, I have been doing CT since the middle of February & I have a couple of weeks on you, so maybe you are getting close. I do lift weights regularly & carry quite a bit of muscle (under a layer of fat) so I have to believe my mass is helpful in generating heat. In general, it has become quite obvious to me by reading comments that you ladies have a lot tougher time than we guys do at resetting. I just want to encourage you to continue on your path & don't give up!! You can do it if you so desire & I will cheer you on the whole way. I love seeing people reach their goals & it gives me great happiness!

SW 285 lbs. May 2011

CW 245

GW 220

### AKMan — 2012-04-19T19:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-16638>

> **Inger;16592 wrote:** Oh Larry, you happy guy! You are totally adapted as it looks like...!
>
> I hate that I am not warming up like you... patince.. patience...
>
> Why I have the feeling that guys have such a easier time CT-adapting..? I am not afraid to jump in icy water, I just hate that it takes so long for me to adapt! Strange enough, all BAT studies I have read are done on men too. Whats up with women.. Is it the fact that we have much less muscles naturally, that makes the heating harder? But I thought it was the BAT.. maybe men have a lot more of it naturally. Thats why women are always easily cold.. on average at least.
>
>
>
> I wonder if I need to drop the coffee... I do drink one cup / day..
>
> The doc says, it slows down adaption..

I've alawys read the exact opposite--women tend to have more BAT than men, as referenced here: [http://www.plosone.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0017247](http://http://www.plosone.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0017247)

"A limitation in our analysis is fact that our previous study was composed of male subjects, whereas the morbidly obese subjects were mostly females. However, retrospective studies have actually shown higher BAT activity in women,\[19\], \[20\] which would only further strengthen the conclusion that BAT activity is reduced in obesity."

This is a really great, full-text article, you all should read it. From 2012, too!

My only problem with this study is the cooling methods, described as this:

"One hour in thermoneutral conditions was followed by two hours of personalized cooling. In the first hour the room temperature was 22.3Â±0.4Â°C (baseline), followed by the second hour where the temperature of the room and cooling mattress were decreased until subjects subjectively reported shivering. This was confirmed by continuous measurement of muscle activity (pectoralis major) by use of on-skin electrodes attached to an electromyograph (Nicolet Viking, Nicolet Biomedical Inc, USA). When shivering occurred, the air and water temperatures were increased by steps of 1Â°C until shivering just stopped. In this manner NST was maximized for each individual without shivering. Stable cooling conditions were reached within 28.6Â±11.8 minutes. "

I wonder what the results would have been if the subjects did daily 50 degree ice baths for 2 months!

### Larry — 2012-04-19T20:42:01+0300

AKMan, great study thanks for linking it. I wish I knew more to help Inger since she seems discouraged at this point. Keep on chillin'.

### Larry — 2012-04-20T17:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-17069>

Just an interesting observation from last night CT: I made the water colder than usual with some added ice to where it was 50 degrees (normally about 54), my pre CT temp (oral thermometer) was 96.5 & after 15 minutes it was 99.2 but after leaning back into the tub and another 20 minutes my temp had dropped to 97.4. ### Souldanzer — 2012-04-23T04:20:40+0300

### Larry — 2012-04-23T13:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18019>

> **Souldanzer;18032 wrote:** Um, so body temp increases with regular CT? I thought it was mild hypothermia that was so healing to the body... I'm confused.

I believe body temp should increase when you are subjected to cold. That would seem to be the proper response in my mind at least. I know mine has increased (oral thermometer readings) from 96.4 to 99.1 when the water was in the mid 50s but it did start to drop (after 30 minutes) when I had water that was 50 degrees. I may be completely wrong but I think the goal is for your body temp to go up with exposure to cold.

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-26T02:54:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18911>

Anyone have this experience? I CT'd today (and this has happened the last 2 times before this as well) and I held a good 98.9-99.1 the entire time until AFTER I got out and I drank the ice water. Then my temp dropped to 94.5 and stayed there for a good half hour and now 1 1/2 hours later I'm at 97.8. Anyone know why I would hold the heat for the entire hour in the ice water and then drop AFTERwards?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-26T02:55:16+0300

### nonchalant — 2012-04-26T03:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18914>

> **Jack Kruse;18985 wrote:** i did CT this AM......i went in at 96.2 and came out at 101.3

Holy mackerel! We're amateurs.

### nonchalant — 2012-04-26T03:15:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18917>

> **BeingVenus;18984 wrote:** Anyone have this experience? I CT'd today (and this has happened the last 2 times before this as well) and I held a good 98.9-99.1 the entire time until AFTER I got out and I drank the ice water. Then my temp dropped to 94.5 and stayed there for a good half hour and now 1 1/2 hours later I'm at 97.8. Anyone know why I would hold the heat for the entire hour in the ice water and then drop AFTERwards?

Venus, you're supposed to drink the ice water before you CT. The quick start guide has been corrected.

Also, after you get out, your body senses that you are now in a warm place. The peripheral blood vessels open up and your core has to deal with all the cold blood. Also, your BAT deactivates once you are out of the water and no longer warms you.

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-26T04:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18924>

Ohhhh that makes sense. I was just pleased because I went in at 97.7 and went up to 99 in there and then suddenly dropped to 94 and was wondering what was wrong with me. Hahahaha Glad to

Know that it's normal and I'll fix the cold water timing. Hopefully that will help. Thank you!!

> **Nonchalant;18990 wrote:** Venus, you're supposed to drink the ice water before you CT. The quick start guide has been corrected.
>
> Also, after you get out, your body senses that you are now in a warm place. The peripheral blood vessels open up and your core has to deal with all the cold blood. Also, your BAT deactivates once you are out of the water and no longer warms you.

### donkjellberg — 2012-04-26T08:06:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-18976>

> **Nonchalant;18990 wrote:** Venus, you're supposed to drink the ice water before you CT. The quick start guide has been corrected.
>
> Also, after you get out, your body senses that you are now in a warm place. The peripheral blood vessels open up and your core has to deal with all the cold blood. Also, your BAT deactivates once you are out of the water and no longer warms you.

This is nice to know and explains the uncontrolled shivering I experience post CT. Thanks for the pointer!

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-26T17:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-your-temp.931/post-19039>

# Thread: Hormone Help Please!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2012-04-17T16:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-15871>

So I started the Leptin Rx in July 2011. Lost 3lbs, noticed body recomp, felt awesome. In Sept I went off of suplements in anticipation of testing, life got stressful, I didn't follow the Rx to a tee... I also started progesterone cream (1/2ml of 3mg cream from day 15 to menstruation) in Nov. By Dec my weight was upa couple of pounds. My moods were improving but still unstable.

Fast forward to March, I started CT and renewed my Leptin Rx. More like post Rx, because I ate less breakfast. No dairy, no wine, no chocolate. And I began ketogenic then too. I have now gained a total of 7lbs since August, and I think its my hormones. I am asking my Dr to increase my progesterone because I feel its too low. But do I need help clearing estrogen too? Should i be taking DIM or milk thistle?

My estrogen wasn't high, it was normal. As was my Testosterone. But my progesterone was nearly nonexistant. So I didn't think I really had a problem with estrogen. But why am I gaining weight?

Day 21:

Estrogen: 215 pg/ml (

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-17T19:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-15922>

I am not an expert but can offer what I know. First here are the optimal ranges for hormones (I think these are Dr. Dzugan's ranges but not sure):

Estrogen 250-437

Progesterone 6-28

Total Testosterone 60-76

You are a little low in estrogen but your progesterone is really low. It's all about a balance between the two and you are out of balance. From what I understand it appears you are estrogen dominant because you still have too much estrogen for the amount of progesterone in your blood. You probably just need a dab of progesterone to balance that out. The progesterone may (or may not) also help the thyroid.

As far as your thyroid labs (I know more about these) your FT3 should be near the top of the range and the FT4 mid to mid-high part of the range. Although most of us believe that the TSH is a useless measure, many of us feel best when it is at 1.0 or below. If your thyroid continues to go in this direction and you are not on any meds, it make take a toll on the adrenals. My thyroid totally bonked my adrenals last year.

Are you CTing? This will help both your adrenals and thyroid but will take some time.

### tweety — 2012-04-17T20:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-15937>

I am CTing, since MArch. At first I felt great, but this month my bloating and weight gain is at its worst. I guess I don't know how to interpret how much progesterone I need. I was using a very small amount, and it seemed to be causing moodiness and weight gain. So I doubled the amount, from 1/2ml to 1ml of progesterone cream, and it seemed to help for two months. But since the CTing now I am back to gaining weight and being bloated!

My adrenals are already bad, low cortisol across the morning and early readings, which is why I thought my thyroid was bad. I don't know! I would love to have a dr I could trust, and not have to fight with or educate myself. To be honest I am not even 100% confident of what I am educating them about! I have a good grasp on what I need to do, but not enough to tell someone else what they need to do to help me!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-17T20:46:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-15946>

I have been dealing with hypothyroid (Hashis) for 5 years on my own. I know how you feel. I just found a doc who is basically a thyroid and hormone doc and she is wonderful. I take oral micronized bio-identical progesterone right now and don't know about strength of the creams. So I guess I really can't help much there.

Your CTing adventure sounds like mine. Great and energizing for the first two weeks then I got hit with detox, bloating, nausea, lethargy, and gained 6# back that I had just lost two months prior. Now after 8 weeks of CT my body seems to be settling down and I have lost 3 of the 6 I gained back. It takes w while. The best thing to do is hang in there and let your body continue to detox. I was freaking out a little but just kept on and low and behold it did settle. My hormones are a mess so it should be less time for your body to calm itself, unless there are other detox things going on inside of you.

There's a lot to learn and I know how frustrating it is when you are trying to figure it all out. One thing leads to another and you don't know what direction to turn! Been there! If I were in your shoes, if you aren't super fatigued, I would try and keep up treating the adrenals, get the progesterone straightened out, hang in there with the CT, and then see where your thyroid is. I was so fatigued I got to the point of desperation and just started taking thyroid meds that I bought on the internet. But my numbers were really bad compared to yours. Felt a lot better as I continued to educate myself and learned how to adjust the meds slowly without over doing it. Maybe someone else can chime in here to help.

### tweety — 2012-04-17T22:52:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-15974>

Thanks! I am going to buy the Dzugan book for myself I think. There's just so much to keep track of. Two days ago I had a lightbulb moment on what I needed to do to help with the bloating and gain. Now, I can't remember for the life of me what it was! I know this is hormonal because it coincides with ovulation. I feel good most days, but I am also aware that the regimen I have going right now is just until I am able to do well on my own. So I keep watching for that. It feels like a game sometimes- adding and subtracting supplements or doses of supplements. And while I am improving, clearly my mind fog is not gone or I might be able to remember things a little better!

### hazyjane — 2012-04-18T00:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-16007>

It sounds like your low cortisol is probably causing pregenolone steal- hence the low estrogen and progesterone. That TSH# is def. a red flag (I have a feeling your weight gain is related to it.)

If you need a good practitioner to figure out your hormones, I've heard great things about Kari Vernon DC, in Scottsdale, AZ: 480-905-1883. I'm thinking of using her for my own hormones.

### tweety — 2012-04-18T00:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-16013>

Thanks. I asked my dr. if supplementing pregnenolone would help. She's progressive, but around here that doesn't exactly mean she's on the same level of progressive as maybe I need her to be.

I will definitely look into Kari Vernon. I assume she does phone consultations for long distance clients?

### hazyjane — 2012-04-18T01:32:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-help-please.940/post-16036>

> **tweety;16083 wrote:** Thanks. I asked my dr. if supplementing pregnenolone would help. She's progressive, but around here that doesn't exactly mean she's on the same level of progressive as maybe I need her to be.

>
